### el_79c523c3835a4e0e9e1045fbd4479de7
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_46a7162c3cdc469a9fe193a32a058f25`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Engineered > Spare Parts > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Maintenance & Cleaning of the Screen Basket 7.2.12 > Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate > Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Discharge Chute Removed > Removal of the Press Zone > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Removal of the Screen Basket > Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming! > Check the Screen Basket > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Screen Basket Installation > Fitting the Press Zone`
- page_start/page_end: `63`
- order_index: `1251`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `If the screen basket has been inserted correctly, then the press zone can be re-attached to the press. Ensure it easily fastens without any gap to the main housing.`

### el_0561eee1b6d14fe3b5aebafa85c126a3

- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_e369e43033544b76b6f28a86541ce321`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Engineered > Spare Parts > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Maintenance & Cleaning of the Screen Basket 7.2.12 > Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate > Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Discharge Chute Removed > Removal of the Press Zone > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Removal of the Screen Basket > Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming! > Check the Screen Basket > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Screen Basket Installation > Fitting the Press Zone > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Engineered > Maintenance of the Screw 7.2.13`
- page_start/page_end: `64`

- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `If it is necessary to change the screw or carry out an inspection, then first remove the screen basket as described above. After dismantling the screen basket, the outer holding screws of the screw mounting plate can be released.`

### el_1e25aea22a164bab9c973830e149186f
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_dfb28c7e55564efab0f86e429ab1e114`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Pulling out the Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `65`
- order_index: `1276`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The locating holes for the screw holder can be used for inserting a tool for pulling the screw from the shaft (available on request from FMD).`

### el_9b3718f57eac45b4b2b36c404bae8ef5
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_dfb28c7e55564efab0f86e429ab1e114`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Pulling out the Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `65`
- order_index: `1277`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Once the screw has been released and the initial friction overcome, the screw can be pulled out carefully. Two people may be required to lift it out.`

### el_b2be533bb0cd4506b4a686fa4d7c3600
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_dfb28c7e55564efab0f86e429ab1e114`

- page_start/page_end: `65`
- order_index: `1278`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ``

### el_041280d3a72841f6a332fa12b79e123a
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_dfb28c7e55564efab0f86e429ab1e114`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Pulling out the Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `65`
- order_index: `1279`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ``

### el_e759146f087740d78a00a9aa5538c0e3
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_dfb28c7e55564efab0f86e429ab1e114`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Pulling out the Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `65`
- order_index: `1280`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `CAUTION: As the screw is pulled through the rear hole, DO NOT allow the screw to drop freely into the housing! The carbide edges of the screw are brittle and easily damaged by impact!`

### el_0165b89bd95a45d99e69d5c97137b53f
- type: `caption`
- section id: `sec_dfb28c7e55564efab0f86e429ab1e114`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Pulling out the Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `65`
- order_index: `1281`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `CAUTION: As the screw is pulled through the rear hole, DO NOT allow the screw to drop freely into the housing! The carbide edges of the screw are brittle and easily damaged by impact!`

### el_96e63dcde6624e2f840a0840b0427fed
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_610642a4202e42f3b6fb24e6ab020e72`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Greasing of Rear and Front`
- page_start/page_end: `65`
- order_index: `1283`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Prior to reinserting a renovated or new screw, the holding and mounting zones of the screw must be treated with grease so that installation (and subsequent removal) can be performed easily:`

- page_start/page_end: `66`
- order_index: `1295`

- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Inserting the Screw`

### el_99a9196b982144fe9dff3d0b01691cc8
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_77484bb88aee4444ab87472197987678`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Inserting the Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `66`
- order_index: `1296`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Lift the screw onto the shaft and make sure not to strike the carbide edges of the screw as they are easily damaged by impact.`

### el_1667271dc9b64975b6150b167a2384a0
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_77484bb88aee4444ab87472197987678`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Inserting the Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `66`
- order_index: `1297`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ``

### el_3c023986f8c0466499c57e74a7bc2ee8
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_31973cbd70244d32b234cadde444c0d4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Screw in Fully Home Position`
- page_start/page_end: `66`
- order_index: `1298`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Screw in Fully Home Position`

### el_682048f913b44f38835ca1af41ba3e1c
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_31973cbd70244d32b234cadde444c0d4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Screw in Fully Home Position`
- page_start/page_end: `66`
- order_index: `1299`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `At the end of the mounting zone, the resistance when introducing the screw will be noticeably greater. At this point, the fully home position should be reached carefully using a soft-face hammer.`

### el_f6dc7c5335cd40a798328753e77f1b7c
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_1fe60593a16b4bcda273969c6e9cb6e7`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Attaching the Fixing Plate`
- page_start/page_end: `66`
- order_index: `1300`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Attaching the Fixing Plate`

### el_b80181b19a7941878663de1c07fb4814
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_1fe60593a16b4bcda273969c6e9cb6e7`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Attaching the Fixing Plate`
- page_start/page_end: `66`
- order_index: `1301`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Finally, the fixing plate must be attached to the shaft. In this process, the correct play-free installation must be ensured and the correct tightening torque applied.`

### el_bc722a24495b4ae8a878418e3e5f307d
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_1fe60593a16b4bcda273969c6e9cb6e7`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Attaching the Fixing Plate`
- page_start/page_end: `66`
- order_index: `1302`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `After this step, the screen basket can be reinserted as described previously.`

### el_d15ccb74e7564ce5bd90554e806abb31
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_c9b917eb53cb41248f7fb60bd6dd3990`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `67`
- order_index: `1313`
- effective heading_level: `5`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw`

### el_f44c33d051044d41b66f89aaa5790810
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_c9b917eb53cb41248f7fb60bd6dd3990`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw`
- page_start/page_end: `67`
- order_index: `1314`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `If the screen basket and the screw are removed, maintenance work and replacement of the shaft and the shaft seals can be performed. To do this, the screw of the retaining plate is to be released on the gear side.`

- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft`
- page_start/page_end: `67`
- order_index: `1316`
- effective heading_level: `6`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Driving out the Shaft`

### el_54729a7ae2444f8bb74ff0cc8a516b22
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_62a664f062b847bc8b13369a5a2cbe97`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft`
- page_start/page_end: `67`
- order_index: `1317`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The shaft can be driven forward to the press side by lightly hitting it with a soft-faced hammer. Ensure that the shaft is not knocked out completely.`

### el_8d9c9722f8c64017a2cc1dd9911fcf15
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_231ee5c353d842f1971416fa292d082f`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Loosening and Removing the Shaft`
- page_start/page_end: `68`
- order_index: `1328`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `If the shaft has been loosened, it can be pulled out to the front/separator side. The feather key will fall out and remain on the gearbox side. Make sure that the feather key does not fall out of the housing. The key and shaft should be...`

### el_736f2d9363974f0d98509053bed66713
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_d95949e7a8c64543950ff8588086a3b0`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft > Environmentally > Responsible Solutions > Loosening and Removing the Shaft > Shaft seals in Position`
- page_start/page_end: `68`
- order_index: `1331`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `During shaft removal, be sure not to damage the shaft seats or the holder for the shaft seals located in the rear housing.`

### el_e6405109be1b4fc2926285ca825828b3
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_445b670038ed45dab55f825f2f3591b7`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Mount the Retaining Plate`
- page_start/page_end: `70`
- order_index: `1356`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Once the feather key has been inserted flush, the retaining plate can be attached. For corrosion protection, the grease discharged during installation can be distributed on the surface and the excess grease removed.`

### el_7aeca2365bba4ebc866146c9f610a6f1
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_445b670038ed45dab55f825f2f3591b7`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Mount the Retaining Plate`
- page_start/page_end: `70`
- order_index: `1357`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The retaining plate must be attached with the specified torque.`

### el_ab163af24d134e109273ba25f56efe45
- type: `text`

- resolved section path: `7 Components > Inserting the Front Shaft Seal`

- order_index: `1368`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `If the rear shaft is inserted, then the front shaft can be inserted. It must be ensured that careful and correct positioning of the seal is carried out.`

- order_index: `1371`
- effective heading_level: ``

- text preview: `If both shaft seals have been inserted correctly, they seal flush with the housing. When the seals are properly inserted, the installation of the screw and the screen basket can be carried out as previously described.`

- text preview: `This manual contains basic instructions which must be observed when installing, operating and servicing the vacuum / transfer pump. It is essential for the user / installer or responsible technician to read the manual thoroughly prior to...`

- type: `text`

- text preview: `A copy of the manual must always be at hand where the pump is being used. In addition to the general safety instructions listed in the main manual section on safety, it is necessary to observe the specific safety rules included in the va...`

- section id: `sec_c26d285e3fd847029790c19c5c7a0f75`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions 7.3.1`
- page_start/page_end: `72`
- order_index: `1381`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Any work on the machine shall only be performed when it is at a standstill, it being imperative that the procedure for shutting down the machine described in this manual be followed. Pumps and pumps units which convey hazardous media mus...`

### el_abce47b5b5e546c6a73a5a9f39f4295d
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_c26d285e3fd847029790c19c5c7a0f75`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions 7.3.1`
- page_start/page_end: `72`
- order_index: `1382`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Replacement parts that have not been supplied by the manufacturer are not tested and not approved for use. The installation and usage of such replacement parts can have a negative impact upon the pump characteristics.`

### el_dc66898fd03644b69468f27e0747945b
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_c26d285e3fd847029790c19c5c7a0f75`

- order_index: `1383`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The manufacturer is not liable for any damages caused by the usage of non-original parts.`

- text preview: `The illustrations in these instructions may not always correspond exactly to the installed machine. But they correctly describe the basic structure and the sequence of work steps.`

### el_5d7c4894150a4c619467cd7ca4f36db5
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_df977425a23e4d978c1ae6260a19ae49`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions 7.3.1 > Technical Data 7.3.2 > Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered > Description 7.3.3`
- page_start/page_end: `73`
- order_index: `1392`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The vacuum / transfer pump is a rotary lobe pump, which is of a positive displacement type. The main components which determine the pumping principle are two lobe rotors rotating in opposite directions within a housing, basically as show...`

- type: `text`

### el_5daccbc929244afd9c277cb4021b934d
- type: `table`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4`
- page_start/page_end: `74`
- order_index: `1428`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `| (1) | drive shaft | (5) | hull | |-------|---------------|-------|-------------| | (2) | housing | (6) | lobe rotor | | (3) | shaft | (7) | plate | | (4) | wear plate | (8) | front cover |`

### el_8a6ed4a56b7544c6a28580af08b8b892
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4`
- page_start/page_end: `74`
- order_index: `1429`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).`

### el_9719ea85e9f14d2b8b7c18eb89fc4857
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4`
- page_start/page_end: `74`
- order_index: `1430`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) of the housing (2).`

### el_0f44bc14d92a42d982e5a4385985ac08
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4`
- page_start/page_end: `74`
- order_index: `1431`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` In the housing (2) the movement of the drive shaft (1) is transmitted to shaft (3b), which turns in the opposite direction.`

### el_1d0d4229029d4cf6a5e3a158a7ef8e25
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4`
- page_start/page_end: `74`
- order_index: `1432`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` The pump housing is formed by the front plate of the housing (2), the two hulls (5), the two wear plates (4), the plate (7) and the cover plate (8).`

### el_9356cb27486c467d909aeb36ee6f8c75
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4`
- page_start/page_end: `74`
- order_index: `1433`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` The lobe rotors (6) transport the medium within the pump housing.`

### el_51cfeeb2bf324f638d697e34165860f6
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`

- page_start/page_end: `74`
- order_index: `1434`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Different versions of this pump have different forms and numbers of lobe rotors (6).`

### el_e5a57c6923f347d3a6cee97d9c5ced96
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_9c82e5dd6cba449496d3633c13177bb1`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1438`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered`

### el_a48d060f2d0a42c9863a35b895565f0e
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_2159a597ee4049be969ab3eeba2fac8a`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage / Transport 7.3.5`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1439`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Storage / Transport 7.3.5`

### el_7d83603aacd74788bc3bf89f9ecd6f2e
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_2159a597ee4049be969ab3eeba2fac8a`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage / Transport 7.3.5`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1440`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The pumps are preserved for transport and short-term storage unless specified otherwise. In cases of longer storage, the pumps should be handled as follows until commissioning:`

### el_327504a91e374912a5d93714e20029a3
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_4fff818d35de4cea8c3a5091850a4444`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage of Lobe Rotors`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1441`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Storage of Lobe Rotors`

### el_26c1a698ca0d47c8993ce552e50297dd
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_4fff818d35de4cea8c3a5091850a4444`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage of Lobe Rotors`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1442`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The following applies to a storage period of up to six months. Standard DIN7716 summarizes detailed information on the storage of rubber products, of which the following is an extract:`

### el_50c1a6db815a4c898f9eff17d67f7459
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_d6cbd52596d949c99e9614f5cb19b70e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1443`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `General`

### el_0707854dfdba4a72b8caa9262769db65
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_d6cbd52596d949c99e9614f5cb19b70e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1444`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Most rubber products may change their physical properties under unfavourable conditions or if treated improperly, which will result in a shorter lifetime. They can become unsuitable for use through excessive hardening, ageing, degenerati...`

### el_4bdc00aa7165431a908061435364b3e4
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_d6cbd52596d949c99e9614f5cb19b70e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1445`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `These changes may occur under the influence of oxygen, ozone, heat, light, humidity, solvents or because of storing the products under load.`

### el_f147465bdfe94cbcbf8d3f6b909aabba
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_d6cbd52596d949c99e9614f5cb19b70e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1446`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `If stored and treated properly, rubber products will retain their properties even during long term storage (some years) with little change. However, this does not apply to uncured rubber compounds.`

### el_a3e1eb2ffd3548d581990e1e1ebef459
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1447`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Storage Environment:`

### el_ac94a83fd62c4ce8803b8b6979051990
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1448`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` The environment in which rubber products are being kept must be cool, dry, free of dust and naturally ventilated.`

### el_ee4f40b841d14d8cba34a420c9fae7d2
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1449`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Rubber products must not be stored in the open, not even in an area sheltered from the weather.`

### el_f3ecd5c2058641d5ae78ba4e0f8c1e16
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1450`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Rubber products should be stored at temperatures above minus 10 °C and not more than plus 15 °C. Storerooms should not be damp and there should be no condensation.`

### el_24d714038ad94030a5f2eb3fdcfef2e5
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1451`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Rubber products should be stored in an environment offering a relative humidity under 65 %.`

### el_9c24cb99b07e49bc9850cf741c382b15
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1452`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Rubber products must be protected against light, particularly direct sunlight or high UV artificial.`

### el_96f760ee7ece45b4ab407b7c032e0b13
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1453`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Rubber products should be kept away from forced ventilation and draughts; under these conditions they should be wrapped.`

### el_92f9b47d799444139d1a23c53741ffc4
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1454`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` As ozone is very aggressive and harmful to rubber products, they should not be stored in the same room with equipment likely to produce ozone, e.g. electric motors or other equipment which could electrically discharge or cause sparks.`

### el_0289c98fbeff49908a6394c879f89182
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1455`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Rubber products should not be stored together with solvents, oil, grease, lubricants or any chemicals.`

### el_a2b316b5ef3c4a73acb9cf1878f2ed13
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- order_index: `1456`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ``

### el_a1503db34745467397ea9527d1ab5a18
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_dd9c5539a9644beeb2618b1fd588161b`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Installation Instructions 7.3.6`
- page_start/page_end: `76`
- order_index: `1462`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Installation Instructions 7.3.6`

### el_9c760bb718ea475db524b5f03a0dd80b
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_dd9c5539a9644beeb2618b1fd588161b`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Installation Instructions 7.3.6`
- page_start/page_end: `76`
- order_index: `1463`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Unless specified otherwise the pump is designed for a horizontal mounting, bolted securely to a sub-structure. The vent plug of the housing must always be mounted at the top of the gear box.`

### el_825d8790ae6a4aae93b02827220bded0

- type: `text`

- text preview: `After bolting the baseplate to the foundation, remove the coupling guard and check the alignment of the coupling with a ruler and re-align if necessary. There is danger of distorting the baseplate if the foundation is uneven which will r...`

### el_4716fc526f664ba09f6e701c08845e29
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_62965d50a7544a1691aac3807248b64f`

- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Permitted maximum misalignment tolerances for the coupling between drive and pump are:`

### el_797c9d2931464788b9eebcdfd0ab31eb
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_62965d50a7544a1691aac3807248b64f`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow`

- order_index: `1484`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``

### el_42983b9a87684dd69e8fb865a6001155
- type: `picture`

- type: `text`

### el_f11aac0a09164181a287ed4af85bdf6a
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_62965d50a7544a1691aac3807248b64f`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow`
- page_start/page_end: `76`
- order_index: `1489`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The lobe pumps are reversible and therefore direction of rotation can be selected to provide for the required direction of flow.`

### el_f30031d6b8a1475bb9692e7ecd730dde
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_62965d50a7544a1691aac3807248b64f`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow`
- page_start/page_end: `76`
- order_index: `1490`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `There are two ways to change the direction of rotation/flow:`

### el_eaf596c75e3b4332b5bb752e3b6b873f
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_62965d50a7544a1691aac3807248b64f`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow`
- page_start/page_end: `76`
- order_index: `1491`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Pumps with one drive shaft: by changing the direction of rotation of the drive`

### el_311f1ef0fea141ecae68d8568b6bc49d
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_62965d50a7544a1691aac3807248b64f`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow`
- page_start/page_end: `76`
- order_index: `1492`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Pumps with two drive shafts: by switching the cardan shaft (3) from drive shaft (1) to (2)`

### el_cd63bb05f6864d5880f6ec8d1b9310d4

- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_7388b9b684724f269f46e2faf6267185`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System`

### el_0e018222f7d74c4dadda7b49d871dd42
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_7388b9b684724f269f46e2faf6267185`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- order_index: `1497`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Clean the pipework and rinse thoroughly before installing the pump.`

### el_c283d98f7e374c1c80a89396f0595cf4
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_7388b9b684724f269f46e2faf6267185`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- order_index: `1498`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Arrange the inlet and outlet pipework so that when the pump is not running, the pumped media is still present before and after the pump (pump remains flooded); sufficient pumped media should remain inside the pump head to lubricate the...`

### el_874dd822717641429628b7b3fbaa1271
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_7388b9b684724f269f46e2faf6267185`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- order_index: `1499`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Connect the pipework ensuring sufficient pipe supports are used so that no external forces or stress act on the pump. Where required pulsation dampers or compensators can be used between the pump and pipework`

### el_b7723b556de44d5892ec916ffb86234d
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_7388b9b684724f269f46e2faf6267185`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- order_index: `1500`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The maximum allowable twisting loads (Fx, Fy, Fz) and bending loads (Mx, My, Mz) for the pump inlet and outlet connections have to comply with the requirements of API 676 and EN ISO 14847:`

### el_5de7bb60cf9e43b79a6426a9bc53b93f
- type: `table`
- section id: `sec_7388b9b684724f269f46e2faf6267185`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- order_index: `1501`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `| Standard nominal diameter [DN] | Fx, Fy, Fz [N] | Mx, My, Mz [Nm] | |----------------------------------|------------------|-------------------| | 50 | 650 | 350 | | 65 | 850 | 435 | | 80 | 1040 | 560 | | 100 | 1300 | 700 | | 125 | 1700...`

### el_c8d47095d120458cbe0f321256894933
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_f7b0d0c5e88e48989db672948624a32d`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.4 Electrical Connection`

- text preview: `7.3.6.4 Electrical Connection`

### el_76f036f4361f493b83fd264a37b29c92
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_f7b0d0c5e88e48989db672948624a32d`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.4 Electrical Connection`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- order_index: `1503`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Electric current can kill or cause serious injuries. All work relating to the electrical suppl should be carried out by authorized and qualified personnel and should be in compliance with the requirements of the relevant national regulat...`

### el_62c0be71e29a4d53b7498e909fa72af9
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_f7b0d0c5e88e48989db672948624a32d`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.4 Electrical Connection`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- order_index: `1504`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `In particular for control systems the latest version of the EC-Directive for Machinery, Annex I, Section 1.2 Controls should be observed.`

### el_240250b66d574d42b0fda2e9c43b62bc
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_f7b0d0c5e88e48989db672948624a32d`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.4 Electrical Connection`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- order_index: `1505`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `WARNING: Follow all local and national electrical codes for all wiring. A qualified electrician must perform all wiring. Failure to comply may result in severe personal injury or property damage`

### el_e202629a1f6c4dd1ab6c439e78a19a1c

- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1515`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered`

### el_e89d78dd41f64a7bbb594cbb60d87e81
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1516`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `numbering_hierarchy`
- text preview: `7.3.7`

### el_49ac32a8cc874013b51441c908184476
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1517`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Start-Up`

### el_c725dc81d1da469292a8e3d32e1b4869
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1518`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ``

### el_3f1fb6300ebe40b3abff6098a3c1d2ba
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1519`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Take Note: Never run the pump dry! - A few rotations in dry condition will damage the rotor lobes.`

### el_5ae3f042508649ba9c86e5c0e1bcbbd8
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1520`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Pay strict attention to the following:`

### el_2e8fc15380d944b9aa5fd56fdc85f89f
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1521`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Before starting up for the first time, fill the pump with medium to prime it.`

### el_a828b29b49ae4679a4d036e5bd0ddbc5
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1522`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Priming is vital to initially lubricate the rubber lobe parts.`

### el_e477235e79f743bd863a7b4f655a92a4
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1523`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Open valves and vents before starting the pump.`

### el_f52f9e8f007443689ca779b87e1bb3fa
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1524`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Check the direction of rotation and flow by briefly switching on the pump drive.`

### el_5e6098331f7341ce8d7557c7cc49b36f
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1525`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ``

### el_22e13de28e514b548d07efd9559b8895
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1526`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `CAUTION: This a positive displacement pump, which is capable of generating pressures that could cause damage – in extreme cases the bursting of pipes or vessels.`

### el_b28ce2a0a3c043c6b1d207c69fd872d7
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1527`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ``

### el_0b58b7f000cc4e0bacf1f64024e8115b
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_1b6fcfd036f6452bbbe477068a97b7a4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1528`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `CAUTION: Possible damage to the pump by exceeding the pressure rating of the pump head and inlet and outlet connections. Never run the pump against a closed valve.`

### el_3cd3fd18f3434d5e95a491262aa761be
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_9884fe4280cd4f339151cc98591a6878`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Temporary Shutdown 7.3.8`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1529`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Temporary Shutdown 7.3.8`

### el_8b52afafac4946c8b79950db2a99650c
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_6f75bbf33cef446fa83945ce9682f426`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1530`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `General`

### el_d980793471864780811c51dd5354204b
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_6f75bbf33cef446fa83945ce9682f426`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1531`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Stop the pump by turning off the power and make sure it cannot be turned on accidently.`

### el_05b97ed5bcd145869dd95a06dac92a79
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_6f75bbf33cef446fa83945ce9682f426`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1532`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Drain the pump head and if necessary, wash through if there is a risk of freezing or if the medium may solidify.`

### el_1fe0d1a8ef964cb7a594128c414534ad
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_7f1dce5ecc664a348ad9cfc834f448e4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Standby Pumps`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1533`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Standby Pumps`

### el_041e80b517144f679aaffa881ad2a614
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_7f1dce5ecc664a348ad9cfc834f448e4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Standby Pumps`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1534`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Where a standby pump is installed as a back-up for the main pump and it is likely to stand idle for an extended period then it is recommended that it is operated from time to time.`

### el_086d3d38e4394d5e9320096f5091d3f1
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_4270041f847a44cd92afce685dbdc7c5`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Maintenance 7.3.9`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1535`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Maintenance 7.3.9`

### el_40dfb9b68fb74e79ad1f1e92d27cd746
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_b66a0c38a736435fb98673aa0d18804d`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.1 Pump in General`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1536`
- effective heading_level: `4`
- heading level source: `numbering_hierarchy`
- text preview: `7.3.9.1 Pump in General`

### el_8a4d6f5ef0d34991b0e553dae55912aa
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_b66a0c38a736435fb98673aa0d18804d`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.1 Pump in General`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1537`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The pump head should be regularly washed through or rinsed out if sediment from the pumped media is likely to settle out in the pump head.`

- order_index: `1539`

- text preview: `A`

### el_da5f070772d74e66b1795ddf83a8c1c9
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_b66a0c38a736435fb98673aa0d18804d`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.1 Pump in General`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- order_index: `1540`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `WARNING: Hands can be crushed by moving parts! Before opening the pump head (removing the cover plate) turn off the power supply and ensure it cannot be turned on accidently (remove fuse).`

### el_36ff10913ea64554bcf1a1f20ac3af73
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_415ce38817ba4387bb81ad37623a6347`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.2 Lubricating the Shaft Seals`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- order_index: `1547`
- effective heading_level: `4`
- heading level source: `numbering_hierarchy`
- text preview: `7.3.9.2 Lubricating the Shaft Seals`

### el_9790d720672d46f09ba745b974012a30
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_415ce38817ba4387bb81ad37623a6347`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.2 Lubricating the Shaft Seals`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- order_index: `1548`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The pump shaft seals are lubricated with grease via two grease points on the side of the gear housing, with two grease outlet points on the opposite side.`

### el_38e0e3c0bb4b457db2e7af0dd686dc72
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_415ce38817ba4387bb81ad37623a6347`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.2 Lubricating the Shaft Seals`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- order_index: `1549`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Lubrication should always be carried out while the pump is running – never during standstill . For this purpose, a high duty water-resistant grease, type NLGI – class 2, should be applied.`

### el_11fe59cda1f54d988b2b83d81c4b8721
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_a29264ecc31c409f88c89e967e3fab93`

- page_start/page_end: `79`
- order_index: `1550`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Lubrication Schedule`

### el_c39fecc32fa74c20879e58bda96ba72a
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_a29264ecc31c409f88c89e967e3fab93`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Lubrication Schedule`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- order_index: `1551`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` After every 350 hours of operation`

### el_83dc49f72ee844f98e2072f609af2c94
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_a29264ecc31c409f88c89e967e3fab93`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Lubrication Schedule`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- order_index: `1552`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `NB: The filling quantity with the hand-lever grease gun should not exceed 2 to 3 strokes per grease nipple.`

- text preview: `The deaeration screw (1) has always to be at the top position. The magnetic oil drain screw (3) has always to be on the lowest position.`

### el_35c8ca7f7ade48ddb33e325bf83e39cd
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_f501c76987b8474697b823097476e5c3`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Draining`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- order_index: `1591`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Draining`

### el_c4c81738725f414fabc253940fb04c61
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_f501c76987b8474697b823097476e5c3`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Draining`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- order_index: `1592`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Open the magnetic drain screw (3) and let all the oil flow out.`

### el_5443f969be09484ebcc2a3f046d05972
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_f501c76987b8474697b823097476e5c3`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Draining`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- order_index: `1593`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Clean the magnetic oil drain screw (3) at every oil change.`

### el_b4abf966b2454f7691d37aa5c3431ad0
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_f501c76987b8474697b823097476e5c3`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Draining`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- order_index: `1594`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Close the magnetic oil drain screw (3).`

### el_f49413fad1464b9d92657c638e78f494
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_fe1a09c61fed4d729e9e3d1bee2ac280`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Filling`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- order_index: `1595`
- effective heading_level: `3`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Filling`

### el_7a2fa2c57be141f0b1b0519b53aec591
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_fe1a09c61fed4d729e9e3d1bee2ac280`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Filling`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- order_index: `1596`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Fill through the deaeration screw (1) until approx. the middle of the sight glass (2).`

### el_62499864ae0f4a9c991c69cf1c7baff8
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_fe1a09c61fed4d729e9e3d1bee2ac280`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Filling`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- order_index: `1597`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Close the deaeration screw (1).`

### el_1f071a0d25db4c54a6cc0346d697c51a
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_c30317eec5064800b125362066137459`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly`
- page_start/page_end: `83`
- order_index: `1655`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Remove screws/spring washers (5030/5040) or hex nuts/spring washers (5035/5040).`

### el_86fa4d77642941478e325b221a09fba2
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_c30317eec5064800b125362066137459`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly`
- page_start/page_end: `83`
- order_index: `1656`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Remove the cover plate (5000).`

### el_ede121e384164e38a7253fc3e4f3c380
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_c30317eec5064800b125362066137459`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly`
- page_start/page_end: `83`
- order_index: `1657`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Remove screws (2070 with O-ring (2075) and fuse (2260).`

### el_dd5207d71b1e4803b9acb389e5aee1e2
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_c30317eec5064800b125362066137459`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly`
- page_start/page_end: `83`
- order_index: `1658`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Remove end parts (2080) with O-rings (2085).`

### el_7d27afb677214af793ce65db837321ca
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_c30317eec5064800b125362066137459`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly`
- page_start/page_end: `83`
- order_index: `1659`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Pull off the lobe rotors (2010) separately using a lobe extractor (pull-off device).`

- text preview: `Assembly is a reversal of the above disassembly procedure, but additionally:`

- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_336ab57483d04a48876bc32ac895b21a`

- order_index: `1667`
- effective heading_level: ``

- text preview: ` In case of visible wear of the housings, crescents or wear plates these should be replaced.`

- type: `list_item`

### el_69ffd234e29a4750a03d97670045a85d
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_d91ef861f94a463095e4862ddf0d03a6`

- order_index: `1802`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Intended use for moderately dirty liquids (maximum size of solids: 4 mm), non-explosive, nonaggressive for the pump materials. Liquid temperature from -10 °C to +90 °C.`

### el_6539e0eda16f4a4e8c1f955bb6945b3e
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- page_start/page_end: `89`

- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``

### el_c6cd73cd3f7642018a51591f9503d35d
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- page_start/page_end: `89`
- order_index: `1809`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Take Note: When first starting, check the direction of rotation is as per the arrow on the casing Never run the pump dry, only start the pump after filling with liquid and venting the case.`

### el_79f37bbafcc84fac9a0e3c62067e1663
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- page_start/page_end: `89`
- order_index: `1810`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Pay strict attention to the following:`

### el_d60a5c61bb3042048925b10640817f61
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- page_start/page_end: `89`
- order_index: `1811`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Open valves and vents before starting the pump.`

### el_2e7cf981c1ff42078e68894bd6f6d234
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- page_start/page_end: `89`
- order_index: `1812`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Before starting up for the first time, fill the pump and vent the casing using the vent fitted.`

### el_cda03910172041bead8dfd575b06ecd9
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- page_start/page_end: `89`
- order_index: `1813`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` Check the direction of rotation and flow by briefly switching on the pump drive.`

### el_5223778ddca944e092f39ad4dc090af0
- type: `list_item`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- page_start/page_end: `89`
- order_index: `1814`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ` If the pump discharge piping is routed above the liquor tank a non return valve will be required to prevent back filling of the food waste system, a valve suitable for water with sediment should be selected.`

### el_e00dc3e6a6b84f39982811822f413445
- type: `text`

- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `CAUTION: When installing or maintaining the actuated valve shut off and lock out power before removing any covers. Never make adjustments while in operation, except for those prescribed in this manual.`

- text preview: `The actuators are suited to the operation of ball and butterfly valves with a 90° rotation angle. They feature a water-resistant body with overload protection and a heating device against condensation formation. Power is transferred to t...`

- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_97c82bd0a83241a3ba946af662c03259`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Adjustment of 1 st "CLOSED" Limit Switch`
- page_start/page_end: `95`
- order_index: `1978`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `Manually set the valve in the closed position. Loosen screw (1) on the lower cam (2) and adjust the cam to contact the lower micro switch (3). If the mechanical stop prevents the cam rotating sufficiently turn the mechanical stop screw 2...`

- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_9b93f29914524b009fc5f6a32f3238c4`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Adjustment of 2 nd "CLOSED" Limit Switch`

- order_index: `1980`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``

### el_44cb95040bd84c4a9d01ac36aaefe099
- type: `section_header`
- section id: `sec_23123ca927a2466faf1b7a302558273e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7`
- page_start/page_end: `96`
- order_index: `2005`
- effective heading_level: `2`
- heading level source: `toc_context`
- text preview: `Emergency Operation 7.5.7`

### el_67da812c7b5a4dda886c623382ba2326
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_23123ca927a2466faf1b7a302558273e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7`
- page_start/page_end: `96`
- order_index: `2006`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `In the case of a power or control failure, or a fault in the actuator, the actuator has the capability for manual operation. Take care about the turning direction of the manual override.`

### el_da10e24c11504b2ab39379099b06904a
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_23123ca927a2466faf1b7a302558273e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7`
- page_start/page_end: `96`
- order_index: `2007`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `The actuator is fitted with an Allen key on the underside, it fits in the socket on the end of the gear shaft. To close the actuator, turn the Allen key clockwise and to open the actuator turn the Allen key counterclockwise.`

### el_975d129e32534904a19b5e7a3335765d
- type: `picture`
- section id: `sec_23123ca927a2466faf1b7a302558273e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7`
- page_start/page_end: `96`
- order_index: `2008`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: ``

### el_86e78995863f4c238e2a8562b952e656
- type: `text`
- section id: `sec_23123ca927a2466faf1b7a302558273e`
- resolved section path: `7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7`
- page_start/page_end: `96`
- order_index: `2009`
- effective heading_level: ``
- heading level source: ``
- text preview: `CAUTION: When finished with manual operation, remove the Allen key from the gear shaft to prevent the injury of persons or the damage of the actuator by normal operation of the electric actuator.`

```json
"AssetMetadata(source=SourceLocation(page_start=23, page_end=23, bbox=BoundingBox(x1=366.7292785644531, y1=757.7649917602539, x2=538.4196166992188, y2=694.7575378417969)), caption=None, nearby_text='Select Auto to De-watering Press on the home page then press the Automatic Run button, this will set the FWC12 plant to automatic operation whereby pressing of the macerator Start button will set the run sequence in operation and discharge the macerated food waste into the de-watering press.')"

It will follow the automated sequence programmed through to completion and')"

In general, these are nonpathogenic strains, however, the inhalation of aerosols and contact with the skin and mucous membranes should be avoided.\\n\\nWARNING: Biohazard! When working on the system the operator must wear suitable clothes, eye protection and rubber gloves suitable for contact with wastewater. Failure to do so could lead to illness or death.\\n\\nThe nature, control and extent')"
```

At installation site the separately delivered 3 legs must be fixed to the 3 clamps in the disposer bottom plate.\\n\\nWhen mounting the legs:\\n\\n\\uf0b7 Measure the height (X mm) between the flange of the mounting assembly and the floor/floor plate.')"

### picture_c8cb9322de294133b7fc489144a270f7
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- element id: `el_60312550245042fba3fd7e3349ecf2e4`
- page_start/page_end: `28`
- image path: ``
- caption/text: ``
- metadata:
```json
"AssetMetadata(source=SourceLocation(page_start=28, page_end=28, bbox=BoundingBox(x1=366.67572021484375, y1=757.8021965026855, x2=538.4971923828125, y2=694.6683807373047)), caption=None, nearby_text='\\uf0b7 Use a jack or a lever to push up the disposer towards the flange of the mounting assembly.\\n\\nWhile this upward pressure is maintained, untighten each leg and pull it downwards. Make sure that all legs are firmly pressed against the floor. Tighten the clamps using a torque of 17 Nm. As this is a critical matter, a torque wrench should be used. If no torque wrench is available use a')"
```

### picture_f8bc73e2b763400aa5b3d919c977c7f7

Check that the rotary shredder in the inlet opening of the disposer turns freely 360\u00b0 in both directions by hand, and make sure that no foreign object has been dropped into the grinding unit during installation.')"

(See photo below.)\\n\\nLoosen the axle screw (P12). If the rotating shredder (P10) keeps turning, and cannot be held still manually, use a pipe wrench to hold one of its shredder blades.')"

If the rotating shredder (P10) keeps turning, and cannot be held still manually, use a pipe wrench to hold one of its shredder blades. ```

(See photo below.)\\n\\nRemove the V-ring seal (P8) and loosen the 4 fixing screws (P7) of the end shield (P3). Note the position of the outlet on the end shield (P3) relative to the terminal box for cable connection on the motor (P1), so that')"

### picture_f08bcbfb54f141228631faf3cfc28303
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- element id: `el_38724938b737492c8462ae7b17e652c8`
- page_start/page_end: `34`
- image path: ``
- caption/text: ``
- metadata:

```json
"AssetMetadata(source=SourceLocation(page_start=34, page_end=34, bbox=BoundingBox(x1=129.07150268554688, y1=492.4136657714844, x2=290.30859375, y2=370.5765380859375)), caption=None, nearby_text='Turn the end shield (P3) upside down and remove the locking ring (P6) by means of a circlip pliers. With some adequate protection in between (e.g. a piece of wood), apply pressure to the washer (P4) in order to get the two axle seals (P5) out. (See photo below.)\\n\\nAfter this dismantling make sure that the surface of the sealing washer (P4) remains in good condition.')"
```

Alternatively, hold the carrier with a pair of pliers and a soft cloth in between.\\n\\nWhen changing the motor (P1),')"

In general, these are nonpathogenic strains, however, the inhalation of aerosols and contact with the skin and mucous membranes should be avoided.\\n\\nWARNING: Biohazard! When working on the system the operator must wear suitable clothes, eye protection and rubber gloves suitable for contact with wastewater. Failure to do so could lead to illness or death.\\n\\nThe nature, control and extent')"

Inform FMD immediately and')"

### picture_d2ffa10b86a9486d8177ba976656d847

There is a risk of crushed hands and limbs from the rotating shaft/screw in the drive area.\\n\\nAll system parts and operating media connected upstream of the press must be protected against unintended start.')"

### picture_302fff2f91454633b47edbd6f6003da1
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- element id: `el_cd1550e50b1a49cc9f71b7714b2447b0`

- caption/text: ``


### picture_a3e815dd8f4e44129259d9061928da79
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- element id: `el_03226e3219644500abc3c8eaca6cb12f`
- page_start/page_end: `65`
- image path: ``
- caption/text: `©Copyright 2026 FMD`
- metadata:
```json
"AssetMetadata(source=SourceLocation(page_start=65, page_end=65, bbox=BoundingBox(x1=109.15986633300781, y1=257.03533935546875, x2=323.2212219238281, y2=68.391845703125)), caption='\u00a9Copyright 2026 FMD', nearby_text='Prior to reinserting a renovated or new screw, the holding and mounting zones of the screw must be treated with grease so that installation (and subsequent removal) can be performed easily:')"
```



Ensure that the shaft is not knocked out completely.')"

The feather key will fall out and remain on the gearbox side. Make sure that the feather key does not fall out of the housing. The key and shaft should be cleaned of dirt and grease residues before they are reinstalled.')"


### picture_87e8b97926f94d7c8b7090968273b170
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- element id: `el_53a13e9799c548dba0f9cb64a502c356`

- caption/text: ``

```json
"AssetMetadata(source=SourceLocation(page_start=69, page_end=69, bbox=BoundingBox(x1=123.18426513671875, y1=590.5859985351562, x2=399.368408203125, y2=384.1857604980469)), caption=None, nearby_text='Once the shaft has been removed, the shaft seals located in the rear housing can be removed. After an inspection or replacement of the shaft, the shaft seals must always be replaced. The holder for the shaft and for the shaft seals must be cleaned and greased prior to inserting the shaft.')"
```

### picture_05784d4f713049d6b16fd858c076a2cc
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- element id: `el_591cca9c0f4c403f991bb2540734c650`
- page_start/page_end: `69`
- image path: ``
- caption/text: ``
- metadata:
```json
"AssetMetadata(source=SourceLocation(page_start=69, page_end=69, bbox=BoundingBox(x1=123.36651611328125, y1=303.6062316894531, x2=398.90899658203125, y2=96.81536865234375)), caption=None, nearby_text='After cleaning and applying grease, the shaft can be reinserted. During this process, it must be ensured that the bracket for the feather key on the shaft matches the location on the gear unit. The feather key can be introduced through light hitting with a soft-faced hammer.')"
```

During this process, it must be ensured that the bracket for the feather key on the shaft matches the location on the gear unit. The feather key can be introduced through light hitting with a soft-faced hammer.')"

### picture_4cce1934541a4d578e11400bda6833ce


But they correctly describe the basic structure and the sequence of work steps.\\n\\nThe vacuum / transfer pump is a rotary lobe pump, which is of a positive displacement type. The main components which determine the pumping principle are two lobe rotors rotating in opposite directions within a housing, basically as shown below:')"

### picture_8e244a87f1a94a529eb10617ebd8d539
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- element id: `el_5e6098331f7341ce8d7557c7cc49b36f`
- page_start/page_end: `78`
- image path: ``
- caption/text: ``
- metadata:
```json
"AssetMetadata(source=SourceLocation(page_start=78, page_end=78, bbox=BoundingBox(x1=76.37296295166016, y1=480.34637451171875, x2=119.75958251953125, y2=441.4966125488281)), caption=None, nearby_text='\\uf0b7 Open valves and vents before starting the pump.\\n\\n\\uf0b7 Check the direction of rotation and flow by briefly switching on the pump drive.\\n\\nCAUTION: This a positive displacement pump, which is capable of generating pressures that could cause damage \u2013 in extreme cases the bursting of pipes or vessels.')"
```

### picture_3d242bf7d22e4626a92fd0039db858fc
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- element id: `el_b28ce2a0a3c043c6b1d207c69fd872d7`
- page_start/page_end: `78`

- caption/text: ``
- metadata:

```json
"AssetMetadata(source=SourceLocation(page_start=78, page_end=78, bbox=BoundingBox(x1=76.6649169921875, y1=427.9152526855469, x2=119.52375030517578, y2=389.54290771484375)), caption=None, nearby_text='CAUTION: This a positive displacement pump, which is capable of generating pressures that could cause damage \u2013 in extreme cases the bursting of pipes or vessels.\\n\\nCAUTION: Possible damage to the pump by exceeding the pressure rating of the pump head and inlet and outlet connections. Never run the pump against a closed valve.')"
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 1 General > 1.1 Explanation of Documentation > Safety Instructions This documentation is designed to assist with becoming familiar with the system and how to operate it for its i...`
- content:
```text
This documentation is designed to assist with becoming familiar with the system and how to operate it for its intended purposes. Important safety and hazard notices help you operate the system safely and properly. This system documentation is subject to revision. All previous releases are invalid when the new release has been delivered.
```

### chunk_d65dffbe441b418a9a33c044f705d3bb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_caca12036dbb430799fb17bf23ab93d1`
- sequence_number: `8`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `certification_info`
- page_start/page_end: `6`
- token_count: `92`
- section_path: `1 General > 1.2 Other Applicable Documents`
- element_ids (2): `el_264660f6315044ff972846825e80c1ba, el_69b24efe3f274d9c92e574622bc2998a`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 1 General > 1.2 Other Applicable Documents The components of other manufacturers which are used within the plant (e.g. e-motors), have a risk assessment from the respective manuf...`
- content:
```text
The components of other manufacturers which are used within the plant (e.g. e-motors), have a risk assessment from the respective manufacturer. The obligation to ensure that their design complies with the applicable national and European regulations is certificated by these manufacturers.
The operation, maintenance, and repair manuals of the other manufacturers for the relevant parts are inseparable components of this technical manual. The instructions contained in these documents regarding safety, set-up, installation, operation, maintenance, dismantling and disposal of the components are to be followed without limitation by the personnel operating the plant.
```

- content:
```text
Important safety-related notes in this technical manual are signified by symbols. These notes regarding work safety must be adhered to and complied with to secure the protection of persons and the safe operation of the plant.
Note: This symbol indicates and identifies tips and information to help ensure efficient and trouble free operation of the plant.
```

```text
CAUTION: This symbol indicates and identifies information that, if not complied with, may lead to minor damage or injury.
WARNING: This symbol indicates and identifies information that, if not complied with, may cause death or serious injury.
DANGER: This symbol indicates and identifies information that, if not complied with, will cause death or serious injury.
WARNING: Electrical hazard! This symbol identifies information that, if not complied with, could lead to health hazards, injury or death.
WARNING: Biohazard, This symbol identifies information that, if not complied with, could lead to illness, or death.
DANGER: This symbol indicates and identifies information that, if not complied with, will cause death or serious injury.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 1 General > 1.6 Copyright Protection The technical manual must be treated confidential. It is distributed exclusively for those persons working on and with the plant. All content...`
- content:
```text
The technical manual must be treated confidential. It is distributed exclusively for those persons working on and with the plant. All content-related information, text, drawings, pictures, and other representations are protected as defined by copyright protection law and are subject to other industrial property rights. Any improper utilization is liable to prosecution.

It is not permitted to pass on the technical manual to third parties, to copy it in any way or form even extracts - or to recycle and/or communicate the contents without written permission of FMD (the manufacturer).

Violations incur the obligation to pay compensation, all other rights reserved. FMD reserves the right to exercise industrial property rights.
```

- content:
```text
Alterations or changes to the treatment plants are only permissible with express FMD written confirmation thereof.

1.8 Liability and Warranty

This technical manual has been compiled while taking into consideration the applicable regulations, current technology and the experiences and developments of many years. FMD assumes no liability for any damages or malfunctions that occur from non-compliance to the contents of the technical manual. The English version of this technical manual is the authorized version; any translations of the manual were created to the best of our ability, however we cannot assume liability for any translation errors.

All obligations of the manufacturer are defined in the purchase contract, which also includes the entire and sole valid warranty agreement. The contents of the technical manual will not amplify or restrict any contractual warranty.

The texts and illustrations do not necessarily correspond exactly to the scope of delivery. The drawings and diagrams are not scaled 1:1. FMD reserves the right to make technical changes to the product within the context of improving the functionality and further development.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 1 General > 1.8 Liability and Warranty Context: All obligations of the manufacturer are defined in the purchase contract, which also includes the entire and sole valid warranty a...`
- content:
```text
Context: All obligations of the manufacturer are defined in the purchase contract, which also includes the entire and sole valid warranty agreement. The contents of the technical manual will not amplify or restrict any contractual warranty.

The texts and illustrations do not necessarily correspond exactly to the scope of delivery. The drawings and diagrams are not scaled 1:1. FMD reserves the right to make technical changes to the product within the context of improving the functionality and further development.
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: 2 Safety

This section includes instructions that must be observed during installation, operation, and maintenance of the equipment.

The operation instructions must be read by any contractor, operator or person prior to installation, start-up, operation, or maintenance and must always be available.

Subsections: 2.1 General Operating Information; 2.2 Operator Safety; 2.3 Maintenance Safety; Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered; 2.4 Proper Use; 2.5 Qualified Personnel; 2.6 Protective Equipment; 2.7 Risk of Ignored Safety Instructions
```

```text
This section includes instructions that must be observed during installation, operation, and maintenance of the equipment.
The operation instructions must be read by any contractor, operator or person prior to installation, start-up, operation, or maintenance and must always be available.
```

### chunk_a8b5cd28993f4ac7b6dc9e3a3bdf24d8
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_87826e56f19b435a9aada7b3aa368d2b`
- sequence_number: `22`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `9`
- token_count: `90`
- section_path: `2 Safety > 2.1 General Operating Information`
- element_ids (2): `el_199313f1c7ef4fc49e8c03fddb02a436, el_f10b20919c144fcf87edf7fed03abf5d`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.1 General Operating Information Personnel charged with installation, operation, maintenance, inspection, and assembly must be appropriately qualified. Before carryin...`
- content:
```text
Personnel charged with installation, operation, maintenance, inspection, and assembly must be appropriately qualified. Before carrying out any work which involves complete or partial opening of the plant, the person responsible must ensure that power to the plant is locked out (following the ships' isolation procedures) and that it is impossible for the plant to be started. Guards and covers may be removed only when the plant has been secured and locked out. All electrical components must be disconnected before disassembly.
Before restarting the plant, all guards must be in place.
```

### chunk_12eb2d2e01d04277bbf6ce5648aca56e
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c5e5f491b8284549b7e2097d94597680`
- sequence_number: `25`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `9`
- token_count: `115`
- section_path: `2 Safety > 2.2 Operator Safety > · WHEN IN DOUBT ASK > Shutdown`
- element_ids (5): `el_aa051558a5cb498b97fedba0d097b559, el_e39fb56ee513493c869d83f7c4af0d73, el_3bda1ab5f7b1404fbaf30b31d91d8cac, el_068ff19fd62f4a3d9b3a905b28849d22, el_6f1cd9636b524a27b7c37f2f7b58d089`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.2 Operator Safety > · WHEN IN DOUBT ASK > Shutdown When opening a component of the system such as a pump, pipe or housing WEAR PPE including, but not limited to, pro...`
- content:
```text
When opening a component of the system such as a pump, pipe or housing WEAR PPE including, but not limited to, protective clothing, gloves and a face shield.
DO NOT ASK a tradesman to repair the equipment if they may come in contact with the process stream by doing so.
MAKE IT SAFE FIRST flush out bio-residues from the pumps and pipelines and isolate the equipment. Many pipes contain wastewater even when shut down for some time.
Always electrically isolate the equipment and lock out the local isolator BEFORE you dismantle any electrical equipment. Remember that equipment can be turned on by accident.
Do not undertake any works unless the consequences are carefully thought through.
```

### chunk_edd9100276eb489680fc351570f09e8b
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_be4bbd355f984591b24e5a639205fdd3`
