- token_count: `72`
- section_path: `2 Safety > 2.4 Proper Use > Approval information`
- element_ids (3): `el_61c285c0e3094bb3849100b233ce6cec, el_d0a3ff0d481645a6abdb5e9b56e97da4, el_6975ca42e0a6465f99dbac0dd0669654`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.4 Proper Use > Approval information For safety and functionality reasons the FWC12 may not be altered or modified. If any modification of the system is required, FMD...`
- content:
```text
For safety and functionality reasons the FWC12 may not be altered or modified. If any modification of the system is required, FMD is to be consulted for prior written approval.
The FWC12 is designed and manufactured with the latest technology and is safe to operate.
Any change in the plant which indicates a malfunction (increased noise level, higher temperatures than normal etc.) must be immediately investigated and where possible the fault rectified.
```

### chunk_1d40a596625e4261a8f1a0d81ef0d8f5
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_f8fed64102154e58b9e3c4d6aa31f0d9`
- sequence_number: `29`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/3`
- chunk type: `safety_warning`
- page_start/page_end: `10`
- token_count: `217`
- section_path: `2 Safety > 2.5 Qualified Personnel`
- element_ids (5): `el_a0c06269f6164b6dbcddd9014d693098, el_aa51947f0d7b4bb6beab13c2c30ef471, el_d8fd0ccca226454b87acefb5fb1095fb, el_2758350fcd5d4c54a439f5c37787d1dd, el_186d507c6f5f41d4872c47aa75c33480`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.5 Qualified Personnel All personnel that are instructed to work with or on the system must observe the rules and regulations for operational safety and accident prev...`
- content:
```text
All personnel that are instructed to work with or on the system must observe the rules and regulations for operational safety and accident prevention and must have read the system documentation, including all warning notices, prior to starting work. The plant must be operated, serviced, and maintained by suitably trained and instructed personnel. Instructions and procedures given by the vessel operator to prevent and avoid potential dangers are compulsory.
Qualified personnel are by reason of their training, experience, instruction and their knowledge of the relevant standards, regulations, accident prevention rules and working conditions, authorized by the person responsible for the safety of the plant to perform the appropriate activities required, and thereby able to recognize and prevent potentially dangerous situations. Knowledge of first aid and local rescue organization arrangements must also be included.
Persons under the influence of drugs, alcohol or reaction-impairing medication must not be allowed to operate or work on the plant.
The personnel responsible for the installation, operation and maintenance of the equipment must be appropriately qualified. The operator must ensure that they have completely understood the instructions contained within this manual.
All warranties and guarantees with respect to the machine function and durability shall be void should the operator fail to adhere to these safety instructions or any other instructions within this manual.
```

```

### chunk_af140a1bb057469295b48528b0330aa6
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (5): `el_69c7e3b13b5a413282654e1c35862d1a, el_50b8333957c94f24b0d1c80c66d66c63, el_dceb360cc28340e0a0257945143f3efc, el_af74640aebf045539744b8a7b3cb2d05, el_b120dd7cb1c44551bf7271bb1f976fd4`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.7 Risk of Ignored Safety Instructions Rotating parts and the energy released with a sudden stop can cause serious damage to personnel and equipment, which can also r...`
- content:
```text
Rotating parts and the energy released with a sudden stop can cause serious damage to personnel and equipment, which can also result in extended downtime and high repair costs with damaged parts.
The associated risks due to failure to adhere to these safety instructions include but are not limited to:
 Endangering people due to electrical, mechanical, and/or chemical/biochemical hazards.
 Endangering the environment due to leakage of hazardous material (where chemicals are involved).
Failure of important equipment and process functions leading to inferior performance.
```

### chunk_5ba11f6efd4c491d8de8ab8e1b10e068

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.8 Spare Parts Only original spare parts and equipment authorised by FMD are suitable and safe for use. Note: Incorrect or faulty spare parts can lead to damage, malf...`
- content:
```text
Only original spare parts and equipment authorised by FMD are suitable and safe for use.
Note: Incorrect or faulty spare parts can lead to damage, malfunction or complete breakdown of the equipment!
Installation of any parts or any modifications not authorised by FMD will nullify liability for any resulting damage or injury and change in performance, all guarantee, service, compensation, and liability claim against the manufacturer, its agents or representatives are forfeit.
```

- section id: `sec_dabd511a49c64f5a8afe4782194fd124`
- sequence_number: `39`

- section_path: `3 System Introduction > 3.3 What it Does`
- element_ids (1): `el_a1ad6a2ed26b4630b294f93514d402f0`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 3 System Introduction > 3.3 What it Does The FWC system is designed to collect food waste from attached macerator stations using vacuum generated by the integrated pump and trans...`
- content:
```text
The FWC system is designed to collect food waste from attached macerator stations using vacuum generated by the integrated pump and transfer the macerated slurry to the food waste dewatering press for separation of the solids from the liquid or to the holding tank for later processing or discharge overboard.
```

### chunk_20f149a1cad946b7919ab083c979e5e9
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_2dc494e053fd4b67a9fba8a66a2d17dc`
- sequence_number: `40`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `13`
- token_count: `215`
- section_path: `3 System Introduction > 3.4 How it Works`
- element_ids (4): `el_cbe26dbb9c424d5bb5e6f0d68136632d, el_e4da4dd12c6240a6816ceeb51bee65bf, el_bcfdf0d23e314fd1921c47c427c31974, el_f244ccab1e294a2aa8073074245ebd89`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 3 System Introduction > 3.4 How it Works When a macerator station lid is closed and the start button is pressed, the control system of the FWC12 opens the appropriate valves base...`
- content:
```text
When a macerator station lid is closed and the start button is pressed, the control system of the FWC12 opens the appropriate valves based on the selected mode and then starts the vacuum pump. When vacuum builds up in the piping system to the required level the macerator will start and the transport water solenoid will open, after a few seconds the macerator discharge valve will open and the macerated food waste will be drawn through the pipe work by vacuum until it reaches the pump.
Normal operation will see the food waste sent to the dewatering press immediately with the food waste tank remaining empty during normal operation, this prevents the anaerobic breakdown of any organic material sitting in the tank and the production of associated smells.
The food waste press separates the solids and the liquids at 150 microns, the liquid is transferred via pump to the ships grease trap. The solids fall by gravity into an endless bag that seals in all smells whilst the unit is in operation. The bag is monitored by a sensor that measures how full the bag is stopping the system and sending a common alarm when the bag requires changing.
Food waste can be discharged in accordance with the most recent revision Marpol Annex V regulations.
```

- content:
```text
Normal operation will see the food waste sent to the dewatering press immediately with the food waste tank remaining empty during normal operation, this prevents the anaerobic breakdown of any organic material sitting in the tank and the production of associated smells.
The food waste press separates the solids and the liquids at 150 microns, the liquid is transferred via pump to the ships grease trap. The solids fall by gravity into an endless bag that seals in all smells whilst the unit is in operation. The bag is monitored by a sensor that measures how full the bag is stopping the system and sending a common alarm when the bag requires changing.
Food waste can be discharged in accordance with the most recent revision Marpol Annex V regulations.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 3 System Introduction > 3.5 Don'ts Section overview: 3.5 Don'ts Do not attempt to process the following waste groups in the macerators or FWC12 system:  Cooking oils & Fats  Do...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: 3.5 Don'ts

Do not attempt to process the following waste groups in the macerators or FWC12 system:

 Cooking oils & Fats

 Dough

 Cutlery, glass, crockery

 Plastic or solid waste

 Paints, aerosols, acids or alkali

 Chemicals

 Substances that can potentially lead to explosion or infection

Note: Equipment can be damaged if inappropriate items or materials are processed.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 4 Installation > 4.1 General > Safety Instructions All safety regulations and instructions are to be observed. The regulations and instructions in the Technical Manual must be ob...`
- content:
```text
All safety regulations and instructions are to be observed.
The regulations and instructions in the Technical Manual must be observed.
Prior to commissioning FMD must be consulted.
It is recommended that commissioning be completed by a service technician from FMD.
The power supply may not vary from the contract specifications of the system.
The installation may not vary from the FMD approved installation drawings without prior written agreement.
```

- content:
```text
Lifting connections should only be made and executed as per the Lifting and Transport drawing supplied (where relevant). Transport and lifting should only be carried out with appropriate lifting equipment by trained persons. Attention is to be paid to all components extending past the main structure, so no damage occurs during lifting and transport operations.
```

Section overview: 4.4 Mounting

The FWC System is to be mounted in the upright position on the appropriate baseplates in accordance with the Mounting and Pipe Connection drawing supplied.

Note: FMD is to be consulted prior to any welding directly on the supplied plant.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

Note: If the ship's piping is of CuNi the FWC system piping will have to be correctly isolated.
```

FMD

- element_ids (8): `el_ddaf25768917413488250371ab46d207, el_34dd04ceb44b412ea8f52d53be54b972, el_faf85bb23ead4b189f6df08ee0f61f42, el_72670192e2aa4fd8bca4eeac6463db53, el_b3a4950dc7a24267a066d8c01bf685bd, el_77c1b8ba7e1649d595a618f3276b7dd5, el_d9e0ff7ce2e5454eac421a89fa5161c0, el_32e47137869a48a1be9af98a4df4ca15`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 5 Commissioning > 5.2 Objective  Installation check of all main and auxiliary components.  Check all interconnecting pipework is complete and correct with no leaks.  Testing o...`
- content:
```text
 Installation check of all main and auxiliary components.
 Check all interconnecting pipework is complete and correct with no leaks.
 Testing of all individual components and instrumentation for correct operation
 Testing of the control system to ensure correct function as per design and contract requirements.
 Establish plants operation in automatic and manual modes as required by contract documents and specifications.
 Confirm that all relevant acceptance test criteria have been met.
 Confirm that all alarms work as intended.
 Running of the system in auto for process establishment and evaluation.
```

- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 5 Commissioning > 5.3 Communication Section overview: 5.3 Communication Commissioning meetings with relevant stakeholders should be held as required. Topics should include:  Com...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: 5.3 Communication

Commissioning meetings with relevant stakeholders should be held as required. Topics should include:

 Commissioning program / schedules / procedures and checklists

 Commissioning resource requirements and allocation

 Commissioning progress to date

 Interfaces and interruptions with operating plant or external systems where they occur

 Interference issues between Construction, Commissioning and Handover

 Permit to Work requirements, operational requirements

 Planned major events, e.g. shutdowns, performance test periods

 Notice to relevant authorities

 Safety requirements

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 5 Commissioning > 5.6 Pre-Commissioning Section overview: 5.6 Pre-Commissioning All equipment is on site installed as per relevant instructions, GA, schematics & drawings with el...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: 5.6 Pre-Commissioning

All equipment is on site installed as per relevant instructions, GA, schematics & drawings with electrical connections and tests completed.

Pre-commissioning starts after completion of the installation of the Plant and includes all activities that are needed to verify the correct installation of each individual component.

At the start of this phase the power should be connected to the main isolator and locked out by the commissioning engineer in charge.

 Check all supplies are connected correctly and isolated

 Undertake pre-commissioning checklists

 Close out any punch list items

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

All equipment is on site installed as per relevant instructions, GA, schematics & drawings with electrical connections and tests completed.
Pre-commissioning starts after completion of the installation of the Plant and includes all activities that are needed to verify the correct installation of each individual component.
At the start of this phase the power should be connected to the main isolator and locked out by the commissioning engineer in charge.
 Check all supplies are connected correctly and isolated
 Undertake pre-commissioning checklists
 Close out any punch list items
```

Normal use mode with all control and safety systems active. From the home page select the Auto button on the screen, the Automatic Operation Page will appear on screen. Pressing the Home button will return to the home page where the red Stop All button is available to stop the plant running.

- content:
```text
Normal use mode with all control and safety systems active. From the home page select the Auto button on the screen, the Automatic Operation Page will appear on screen. Pressing the Home button will return to the home page where the red Stop All button is available to stop the plant running.
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Manual Operation Page 6.1.4

All main components fitted to the system can be operated manually from the HMI. All components usable in manual appear on the screen with a yellow box highlighting them, this forms the Start / Stop button for the specific component.

CAUTION: When operating in manual it may be possible to start pumps with valves closed, particular care must be taken not to damage the plant with this type of operation.

User:

Overboard/Ashore

mbar

FoodwasteTank

2.5m3

Water

50

25

mEStop

m3/h

100

mbar

HotWater

mbar

ds33

mbar

Grease Trap

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- content:
```text
The day-to-day operator involvement required during normal operation is minimal and consists of basic daily observations, minor maintenance that has come due and response to any alarms.
Any changes or adjustments should be made by appropriately qualified persons and should ideally be recorded.
```

### chunk_7f316eb4a1fd41288630caecfd5ab599
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_76d349ddf89348d09dd24bbe09c5318b`

- token_count: `47`
- section_path: `6 Operation & General Maintenance > Auto to Tank 6.2.1`
- element_ids (1): `el_447ac0d969f5431e88e6fa98bbc79e70`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 6 Operation & General Maintenance > Auto to Tank 6.2.1 Select Auto to Tank on the home page then press the Automatic Run button, this will set the FWC12 plant to automatic operat...`
- content:
```text
Select Auto to Tank on the home page then press the Automatic Run button, this will set the FWC12 plant to automatic operation whereby pressing of the macerator Start button will set the run sequence in operation and discharge the macerated food waste into the holding tank.
```

- content:
```text
Select Auto to De-watering Press on the home page then press the Automatic Run button, this will set the FWC12 plant to automatic operation whereby pressing of the macerator Start button will set the run sequence in operation and discharge the macerated food waste into the de-watering press.
```

### chunk_e3fd4c2d9b994a60a5a575db88a9ee0c
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_bfc6fc9b82f648539662fa11677b9968`
- sequence_number: `86`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `24`
- token_count: `93`
- section_path: `6 Operation & General Maintenance > 6.3 Operation Macerator`
- element_ids (1): `el_729ca31c380546b5aca8a1081b7b63ba`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 6 Operation & General Maintenance > 6.3 Operation Macerator To start the macerator, it must be in the ready status, the E-Stop should not be illuminated, and the Start / Run butt...`
- content:
```text
To start the macerator, it must be in the ready status, the E-Stop should not be illuminated, and the Start / Run button should be illuminated solid green. Once the macerator is filled with food and the lid is closed the press Start / Run button it will change from solid green to flashing green to show the macerator is active. It will follow the automated sequence programmed through to completion and the Start / Run button will return to solid green showing the macerator is complete and ready for the next batch.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Owner / User Responsibility There are several forewarnings indicated throughout this manual that may stress a possible unsafe condition or importa...`
- content:
```text
There are several forewarnings indicated throughout this manual that may stress a possible unsafe condition or important information regarding the use of this equipment.
The system operator must ensure that all relevant accident prevention regulations of their industry are observed and complied with. These safety regulations are only supplementary to the existing safety regulations of the entire system.
This manual has been prepared to provide you with a simple and understandable guide for equipment operation and maintenance. To keep your unit in top running condition, follow the specific maintenance procedures given in this manual.
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > General Warnings: WARNING: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and symbols and understand all instructions before installing, operating, o...`
- content:
```text
WARNING: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and symbols and understand all instructions before installing, operating, or servicing this machine . Failure to do so could result in severe personal injury or property damage .
CAUTION: When installing or maintaining the pre-screen, shut off and lock out power before removing any covers. Never make adjustments while in operation, except for those prescribed in this manual.
DANGER: Keep doors and guards in place at all times to avoid exposure to rotating parts. While the power is on, severe injury can occur if hands or feet reach inside the unit.
WARNING: A qualified electrician must perform all wiring. Failure to comply may result in severe personal injury or property damage.
```

- element_ids (3): `el_6a7107d9309a43fb90a01a96da97fa34, el_949e005412cb42b2bd82004e6929d5de, el_1b7bc17b1d4a4b2985d716e65c6f6dc0`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Electrical System Precautions > Safety Instructions The electrical equipment must be secured against unauthorised access and may only be tested an...`
- content:
```text
The electrical equipment must be secured against unauthorised access and may only be tested and maintained by trained specialists.
WARNING: Electrical Hazard! ALWAYS check for no voltage before starting work!
When working on the switchgear and motors, the system must de-energized, tagged out and secured against re-connection in accordance with the ship's working practices and permit to work policy.
```

### chunk_13e320d498474d7a8d43c87dd68becb4
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c7cf3f24d4eb441d9cab1ddc057d61e6`
- sequence_number: `96`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `safety_warning`
- page_start/page_end: `26`
- token_count: `90`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Biohazard > Safety Instructions`
- element_ids (3): `el_5feeb5fdd9694d0a9927a8867cf3e22f, el_dcb0d501c228490dbfa07d61278b3056, el_e8dfbfb940984517811abef90b81bc92`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Biohazard > Safety Instructions The food waste press can contain a variety of bacteria and viruses. In general, these are nonpathogenic strains, h...`
- content:
```text
The food waste press can contain a variety of bacteria and viruses. In general, these are nonpathogenic strains, however, the inhalation of aerosols and contact with the skin and mucous membranes should be avoided.
WARNING: Biohazard! When working on the system the operator must wear suitable clothes, eye protection and rubber gloves suitable for contact with wastewater. Failure to do so could lead to illness or death.
The nature, control and extent of the necessary vaccinations and pre/post treatment examinations should be determined by the ship or company medical professionals.
```

- content:

Context: WARNING: Biohazard! When working on the system the operator must wear suitable clothes, eye protection and rubber gloves suitable for contact with wastewater. Failure to do so could lead to illness or death.

The nature, control and extent of the necessary vaccinations and pre/post treatment examinations should be determined by the ship or company medical professionals.
```

Food waste is fed into the machine and ground into small particles for transport by vacuum to the holding tank or dewatering press.
```

### chunk_c68cb118866b436d9d6d913f3c55768e
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > How it Works 7.1.4 Food waste disposers use flails mounted on a spinning plate taking advantage of centrifugal power to force food waste particles...`
- content:
```text
Food waste disposers use flails mounted on a spinning plate taking advantage of centrifugal power to force food waste particles against a grinding ring breaking the food down into small particles.
```

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Transport of the Macerator 7.1.5`
- element_ids (3): `el_76ac44a1cfd74dba8984646ae7fe25f4, el_14feb6b5b78a427bae8c3069f5649515, el_657699f1cbd5453f96b41f0fdbeb92ab`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Transport of the Macerator 7.1.5 If the machine is to be stored before installation it should be stored in a dry and clean room where the relative...`
- content:
```text
If the machine is to be stored before installation it should be stored in a dry and clean room where the relative humidity may not exceed 60%.
Recommended storage temperature of -20 °C to + 60 °C.
The machine should not be lifted or moved by hand. During installation, we recommend using a pallet jack or forklift.
```

### chunk_804133ca4b614b63a69cd315457ba0d2
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_b344dfbaca66463780e4b9516bbd1513`
- sequence_number: `105`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `installation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `28`
- token_count: `70`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Mounting 7.1.6`
- element_ids (1): `el_31d2cd706a4f4bf585391e8b3f39ab50`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Mounting 7.1.6 In order to weld a strong joint and to be able to grind to an even and fine surface between the discharge cone and the working benc...`
- content:
```text
In order to weld a strong joint and to be able to grind to an even and fine surface between the discharge cone and the working bench/tabletop, the cone is welded with its upper flange against the underside of the working top. The opening in the worktop must be at least 398 ± 2 mm in diameter. The disposer is then screwed into the lower attachment ring of the cone.
```

### chunk_a1d43b4748ce4183bd84e23f543161ea
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c518b0040a4a4c439d36f3cff09d23d6`
- sequence_number: `106`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/3`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `28`
- token_count: `264`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Mounting of models 520, 530, 550 and 575, delivered with legs as standard`
- element_ids (10): `el_b6d436b9dae944c2b42169f8c4aa4f34, el_c8938b89e72647ecbee7d5f8cf359166, el_9a0ad3f7f8bb4811a5c9bdfeec497a25, el_f0379be35c384fa8b5fec3a7877d6ff5, el_19e8415a049a4522b26ba16239ab1ae0, el_bf6600b06d434f01819a886be0261ea7, el_656c066d7f3b48d3acf63d496ac6dfb8, el_373d0e00f0d5492aa9ae028de135fb3c, ... (+2 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Mounting of models 520, 530, 550 and 575, delivered with legs as standard The weights of models 520, 530, 550 and 575 require legs for support. At...`
- content:
```text
The weights of models 520, 530, 550 and 575 require legs for support. At installation site the separately delivered 3 legs must be fixed to the 3 clamps in the disposer bottom plate.
When mounting the legs:
 Measure the height (X mm) between the flange of the mounting assembly and the floor/floor plate.
 Measure the height (Y mm) of the disposer without legs including the 3 mm rubber seal.
 Lay down the disposer carefully on its side. Slide the three legs into respective clamp. Each leg should protrude X – Y - 4 mm.
 Tighten the clamps. Make sure that the legs are fixated, and that their position have not changed during the tightening of the screw.
 Place the disposer so that it stands on its legs. Move the disposer carefully into position under the flange of the mounting assembly.
 Using the six screws with nuts fixate the disposer onto the flange with the 3 mm rubber seal in between. Tighten the screws alternately and evenly until the rubber seal is compressed approximately 1 mm.
 Use a jack or a lever to push up the disposer towards the flange of the mounting assembly.
While this upward pressure is maintained, untighten each leg and pull it downwards. Make sure that all legs are firmly pressed against the floor. Tighten the clamps using a torque of 17 Nm. As this is a critical matter, a torque wrench should be used. If no torque wrench is available use a leaver of approximately 30 cm and a force of 6 kg.
```

- content:
```text
Context: The weights of models 520, 530, 550 and 575 require legs for support. At installation site the separately delivered 3 legs must be fixed to the 3 clamps in the disposer bottom plate.

When mounting the legs:

 Measure the height (X mm) between the flange of the mounting assembly and the floor/floor plate.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Electrical Installation 7.1.7 The electrical connection must be done by an authorized electrician and in accordance to valid local regulations. Th...`
- content:
```text
The electrical connection must be done by an authorized electrician and in accordance to valid local regulations.
The wiring made at the factory, and the electrical connections to be done during installation, are shown in the electrical wiring diagram attached to this manual. The machine's specific electrical data are given in this manual, as well as on its serial number plate located on the outer cover of the macerator.
```

```text
Check that the supply voltage to be connected corresponds to the specified voltage on the machine's serial number plate.

Check that the supply voltage for the delivered machine is fused and protected as specified in this manual.
```

- content:
```text
Use connection cable having 1.5 mm² wires for machines having a rated current up to 14A. For machines having a rated current above 14A, use 2.5 mm² wires. The rated voltage and current is specified on the machine's serial number plate.
```

- content:
```text
The disposer operates correctly irrespective of the motor's rotational direction. It is therefore irrelevant in which sequence the electrical phases are connected to the disposer motor.
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Check before Start Up

Disconnect the power at the main isolator and lock out according to vessel lockout procedures. Check that the rotary shredder in the inlet opening of the disposer turns freely 360° in both directions by hand, and make sure that no foreign object has been dropped into the grinding unit during installation.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up Section overview: Checks during Start Up  Start the disposer and...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Checks during Start Up

 Start the disposer and determine that the grinder rotate.

 Check for automatic flushing water. The water should come directly when starting the machine.

 Check assembly, flushing water connections and drain connections for possible leaks.

 Make sure that the safety interlock switch functions and stops the machine if the lid is lifted.

AL

DANGER: Keep doors and guards in place at all times to avoid exposure to rotating parts. While the power is on, severe injury can occur if hands or feet reach inside the unit.

Subsections: Operation 7.1.9
```

### chunk_a7414dc0468949338e07491076649b85
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_ae7a220eaf69449a93da6ab3f11eff44`
- sequence_number: `121`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `30`
- token_count: `143`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9`
- element_ids (6): `el_d5f56a2ac0f045609b1fce26bc2934e7, el_14212adaf9ab4eceb14888d102c7cb8d, el_166f4e4e0d6845eeb6df98455c73f673, el_3b98dca6ed84430ba195602febce45c1, el_99663111b60e4f578cf715b5d2355408, el_9c566c4aca5f4f9d8e1ce669746c710f`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 Section overview: Operation 7.1.9 Food waste tha...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Operation 7.1.9

Food waste that is difficult to grind, such as fibrous vegetables, tough fish skins and sinewy meat, should be broken down in size and mixed with other food waste.

Dry and sticky food waste (such as steamed rice, pasta, mashed potatoes, bread) should soaked in water before being fed into the machine.

Hard shells in various forms (e.g. from clams, oysters, crabs etc.) must not be fed into the machine as it among other things causes drainpipe blockages.

Also larger amounts of eggshells must not be fed into the machine as it also causes drainpipe blockages.

For batch fed machines of 2.5kW or more, and with tightly sealed top cover above the inlet for food waste, also any single larger sized bone can be mixed with other food waste when being fed into the machine.

Subsections: Start and stop
```

- element_ids (10): `el_14212adaf9ab4eceb14888d102c7cb8d, el_166f4e4e0d6845eeb6df98455c73f673, el_3b98dca6ed84430ba195602febce45c1, el_99663111b60e4f578cf715b5d2355408, el_9c566c4aca5f4f9d8e1ce669746c710f, el_84ed2ba886f247c48ade9818fd598367, el_655e06b5a542479b807ba985b27da39c, el_51b3946cf4a1468f9f2709b4506a266f, ... (+2 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 Food waste that is difficult to grind, such as f...`
- content:
```text
Food waste that is difficult to grind, such as fibrous vegetables, tough fish skins and sinewy meat, should be broken down in size and mixed with other food waste.

Dry and sticky food waste (such as steamed rice, pasta, mashed potatoes, bread) should soaked in water before being fed into the machine.

Hard shells in various forms (e.g. from clams, oysters, crabs etc.) must not be fed into the machine as it among other things causes drainpipe blockages.

Also larger amounts of eggshells must not be fed into the machine as it also causes drainpipe blockages.

For batch fed machines of 2.5kW or more, and with tightly sealed top cover above the inlet for food waste, also any single larger sized bone can be mixed with other food waste when being fed into the machine.

Start and stop

Open lid

Fill the feeding hopper to a maximum half of its volume

Close lid

Press the start button – disposer and flush water starts

When complete and the grinder has stopped wait a further 10 seconds before opening the lid to the feed hopper
```

### chunk_bd6de4df214246cfbde3c7b1b3ea0402
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start`
- element_ids (21): `el_40f51eac6496473f8f48659ab2879ded, el_2be34f5276f5438a8825abc077114731, el_1662500063014700b412909fa9fc3ccf, el_a6f233d9048d4c5b943b44a9e081abae, el_a02b709d658f4c43af16c627a7de8916, el_c8e00bad02914404b2cb5c3119f43b35, el_6c2647dba4624453b8d885d8310aeeb0, el_65d18abdb82f4a6288bac4f71f5b3ee7, ... (+13 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start

A humming sound may be heard from the disposer motor.

 Press the red stop button

 Use the main electrical isolator to disconnect electrical supply and lock it out

 Use protective gloves

 Open the disposer inlet lid

 Check if something is jammed in the disposer

 If something is jammed, place the jam release wrench on the center washer

 The recess on the lower plate of the wrench should grab one of the two bars on the disc of the grinding unit, as shown below

 Release the disc of the grinding unit by rotating the wrench backwards and forwards until this shredder disc rotates freely a complete turn in both directions. If needed, extend the bar on the wrench and hit the bar with a hammer/rubber mallet.

 Remove the jam release wrench.

 Remove any non-grindable object from the disposer grinding unit, e.g. cutlery.

 Close the disposer inlet lid

 Check and reset breakers & motor overload protection.

 Unlock and reset the main electrical isolator.

 Press start button and check the disposer functions correctly.

View of grinder and jam release wrench from

above

中

00

Subsections: Disposer starts but there is no flushing water
```

```

- content:

 Check if something is jammed in the disposer
 If something is jammed, place the jam release wrench on the center washer
 The recess on the lower plate of the wrench should grab one of the two bars on the disc of the grinding unit, as shown below
 Release the disc of the grinding unit by rotating the wrench backwards and forwards until this shredder disc rotates freely a complete turn in both directions. If needed, extend the bar on the wrench and hit the bar with a hammer/rubber mallet.
 Remove the jam release wrench.
 Remove any non-grindable object from the disposer grinding unit, e.g. cutlery.
 Close the disposer inlet lid
 Check and reset breakers & motor overload protection.
 Unlock and reset the main electrical isolator.
 Press start button and check the disposer functions correctly.
```

 Press start button and check the disposer functions correctly.

- element_ids (6): `el_058e2bf8e4a34922afb439a7477c3304, el_15ab2942b2054934bb2846f4756e447c, el_52ba2a30410d45108c5d9b2c994a71df, el_e3aa21aa34be434e8a85e800c1b65456, el_8055215e31584dce9309ecbb225204a0, el_c5624106f98842b18ff34c539bd716f3`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Disposer starts but there is no flushing water

 Is the water supply isolation valve open?

 Is a clicking sound heard when activating the water solenoid valve? If not, change the coil.

 Is the water strainer clogged? Isolate the water supply, open strainer and clean.

Open this nut in order to clean the line strainer

Subsections: Machine does not Start and makes no Sound
```

 Is the water supply isolation valve open?

 Is a clicking sound heard when activating the water solenoid valve? If not, change the coil.

 Is the water strainer clogged? Isolate the water supply, open strainer and clean.
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Machine does not Start and makes no Sound

 Check that the disposer inlet lid is in place and properly closed.

 Check that the main power isolator is in ON-position.

 Make sure breakers & motor overload protection are in order and switched on.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

- element_ids (4): `el_4d811843258c4d1bb35c11f38512e96c, el_5c4ed33ca52945efb904a6f226947903, el_321fa594d0ee48e880504031ae036fce, el_d389ccd6fe604d8fa5b0f9d2a1b60117`
- table_ids (1): `table_c551247dc5c7415fbc827cb786059b14`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Maintenance Intervals
| Description                                         | Interval                                                                                              | Refers to                                                                                                                                                                         |
|-----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Cleaning of the machine After daily use             |                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Check of the line strainer  in the flush water pipe | First time after a month use,  then when needed                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                                                     | Preventive maintenance 1 First time after 1 month use,  then after 1 year, and 3 yearly  from then on | For this maintenance, please note  that no parts need to be exchanged. Check electrical connections.  Check pipe connections for leaks                                            |
|                                                     | Preventive maintenance 2 First time at 2 years, and 3  yearly from then on.                           | Preventive maintenance 1  Replacement of rubber seals with  Position No. P14 and P17                                                                                              |
| Preventive maintenance 3                            | First time at 3 years, and 3  yearly from then on.                                                    | Preventive maintenance 1 & 2 Replace parts included in Service  Package 1 shown in spare parts  table.  Inspect if the carrier (Pos. No. P2,)  needs to be ordered and exchanged. |
| Replacement of parts due  to wear                   | After approx. 9000 operating  hours                                                                   | Replace parts included in Service  Package 2 shown in spare parts  table.                                                                                                         |
| Exchange of other parts When needed                 |                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                   |
WARNING: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and symbols and understand all instructions before installing, operating, or servicing this machine . Failure to do so could result in severe personal injury or property damage .
WARNING: Disconnect the power at the main isolator and lock out according to vessel lockout procedures.
```

### chunk_1228629f10434e86907effb2a5273c2f
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_810cc78ebe6d4cefa951599f83509f5d`
- sequence_number: `142`

- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `33 -> 34`
- token_count: `378`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer`
- element_ids (19): `el_9147f788c5744d3db547d4a4441c9334, el_44cc5b3dc954497caaea07c1b455434d, el_9fedcd6e235049178d0e9efeb34ec70a, el_600ad647daf44b71abd85f3322423ebf, el_4e3b9d9bdb834895a29e94ca31d23c2e, el_0a3c250486b044e5bd3a3012689b182c, el_3af711b0018b4e62bf942747c15aa08b, el_ee1707bdd058417684c2437713c8f64f, ... (+11 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Dismantling of Disposer

The below designation within brackets refers to the position numbers on the exploded view drawing and spare part list.

Isolate the power at the main isolator and lock out according to vessel lockout procedures.

Isolate the water supply to the disposer by closing a valve in the supply line.

Disconnect the disposer motor (P1) connection cable from the start & stop control unit.

Disconnect the disposer outlet.

Use a trolley with lifting plate or similar that can be inserted under the disposer. Detach the disposer from the assembly (6 screws and nuts, P18), and move the disposer to a suitable workbench for the continued dismantling.

Turn the grinder upside down and remove the housing (P20) by loosening the two screws (P23) , and loosen the cable gland (P22) and insert the cable into the housing.

Mark on the end shield (P3) the position of the strap (P19) and then remove it by loosening two nuts (P16). Turn the grinder over again. Loosen the remaining two nuts with washers (P16) .

These four nuts (P16) must always be changed for new ones before reassembly, as they are lock nuts. Note the location of the disposer outlet on the end shield (P3) relative to the hole pattern on the hood (P15) upper side, so that the outlet later may be reassembled in the same direction. For this purpose, is also an arrow engraved on the hood (P15) lower edge, which when reassembling shall be above the outlet of the the end shield (P3). Remove the hood (P15) .

Remove the seal (P14) on the stationary shredder. This seal must always be replaced with a new seal when reassembled.

Pry the stationary shredder (P13) loose from the end shield (P3) using a crowbar that levers against the large tooth on the inside edge and is supported by the rotary shredder (P10) close to one of the two shredder blades. (See photo below.)

Loosen the axle screw (P12). If the rotating shredder (P10) keeps turning, and cannot be held still manually, use a pipe wrench to hold one of its shredder blades. Alternatively, lock the rotary shredder with a screwdriver placed through one of the holes at the disposer outlet. (See photo below.)

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

### chunk_75bcec7061ee45edb90697f6591ac66a
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_eae9c9cb02e64e2c8a16456e889d6200`

- chunk type: `overview`

- token_count: `179`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer`
- element_ids (14): `el_5ec3ef089e4c497baa8bc4f7313d7e0a, el_dc4200025a034549934c82647eccd082, el_45326212b7de4c73a71d73531af5d102, el_9a896432b0944bc4a48e0186c7a47b62, el_fbb2a56c9fa04dc7b5ce55173ac643f4, el_67d9075eb2d545c0ace656a54198b748, el_068534988ced4891b01b3a7884e243b6, el_59f103049cce42769ea5ae07b3400c6f, ... (+6 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Responsible Solutions

Engineered

Remove the rotary shredder (P10) by placing two crowbars opposite one another under the edge of the shredder, supported by the side of the end shield (P3). (See photo below.)

Remove the V-ring seal (P8) and loosen the 4 fixing screws (P7) of the end shield (P3). Note the position of the outlet on the end shield (P3) relative to the terminal box for cable connection on the motor (P1), so that this outlet will receive the same direction when reassembling the disposer. Remove the end shield.

Inspect the washer (P4) for possible wear caused by the V-ring seal. Replace the washer if necessary.

Turn the end shield (P3) upside down and remove the locking ring (P6) by means of a circlip pliers. With some adequate protection in between (e.g. a piece of wood), apply pressure to the washer (P4) in order to get the two axle seals (P5) out. (See photo below.)

After this dismantling make sure that the surface of the sealing washer (P4) remains in good condition.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

### chunk_52eab728988a4f349b4ae4671362bb7d
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_981acb7e64874676ac88f254e5317efd`
- sequence_number: `145`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/4`
- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `35`
- token_count: `269`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer`
- element_ids (14): `el_9546dfc9be724a4bb3914fa2e77e4731, el_f505a74eeb2d4574ab4d37a56f1af242, el_d1a632b5dcc845c5a65c2fc7d170aabe, el_09234ccdf17d4dab8bd697add2ea2d25, el_333d7b33c22f495f85c5c635a0b2b2f2, el_1361a797c3fe4f9d98401ce6671d1cdc, el_5a885e057bb542f2b87f294705f27a21, el_708a09c97326455c9fbe745344ad8dd9, ... (+6 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Responsible Solutions

Engineered

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Examine the contact surfaces of the carrier (P2) for wear caused by the axle seals. There should only be two small symmetrical (barely noticeable) tracks on the carrier (P2) if it is to be reused. If the tracks are noticeable, asymmetrical or more than two, replace the carrier.

Terminate dismantling here if carrier (P2) and motor (P1) are not to be replaced. Start the reassembling of the disposer from item 5 in section Assembly of Disposer below.

The V-ring seal (P8) and the two axle seals (P5) with special grease (P26) together with stationary seals (P14) and (P17) must be replaced at each dismantling.

In Service Package 2 a disassembly screw (P31) for the carrier (P2) is included. Use this screw on top of the carrier and screw it down. This will remove the carrier from the motor axle. If needed, use the rotary shredder (P10) as a handle to prevent the motor axle from rotating. Alternatively, hold the carrier with a pair of pliers and a soft cloth in between.

When changing the motor (P1), make sure that the new motor has the same classification and quality as the original. The motor must have two holes in the upper flange to allow for ventilation and drainage of water condensation (see arrows on photo below and original motor). When drilling these holes in the motor upper flange, it is important to drill at the correct angle, so that it does not penetrate the motor winding. All replacement motors delivered by the manufacturer have premade drainage holes.

i

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

### chunk_0c9417f118864a85b97fa6955f2f5c18
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_810cc78ebe6d4cefa951599f83509f5d`
- sequence_number: `147`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/5`
- chunk type: `spare_parts_table`
- page_start/page_end: `33`
- token_count: `319`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Spare Parts`
- element_ids (11): `el_44cc5b3dc954497caaea07c1b455434d, el_9fedcd6e235049178d0e9efeb34ec70a, el_600ad647daf44b71abd85f3322423ebf, el_4e3b9d9bdb834895a29e94ca31d23c2e, el_0a3c250486b044e5bd3a3012689b182c, el_3af711b0018b4e62bf942747c15aa08b, el_ee1707bdd058417684c2437713c8f64f, el_75c42f5e6552440581143945c291137d, ... (+3 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
The below designation within brackets refers to the position numbers on the exploded view drawing and spare part list.
Isolate the power at the main isolator and lock out according to vessel lockout procedures.
Isolate the water supply to the disposer by closing a valve in the supply line.
Disconnect the disposer motor (P1) connection cable from the start & stop control unit.
Disconnect the disposer outlet.
Use a trolley with lifting plate or similar that can be inserted under the disposer. Detach the disposer from the assembly (6 screws and nuts, P18), and move the disposer to a suitable workbench for the continued dismantling.
Turn the grinder upside down and remove the housing (P20) by loosening the two screws (P23) , and loosen the cable gland (P22) and insert the cable into the housing.
Mark on the end shield (P3) the position of the strap (P19) and then remove it by loosening two nuts (P16). Turn the grinder over again. Loosen the remaining two nuts with washers (P16) .
These four nuts (P16) must always be changed for new ones before reassembly, as they are lock nuts. Note the location of the disposer outlet on the end shield (P3) relative to the hole pattern on the hood (P15) upper side, so that the outlet later may be reassembled in the same direction. For this purpose, is also an arrow engraved on the hood (P15) lower edge, which when reassembling shall be above the outlet of the the end shield (P3). Remove the hood (P15) .
Remove the seal (P14) on the stationary shredder. This seal must always be replaced with a new seal when reassembled.
Pry the stationary shredder (P13) loose from the end shield (P3) using a crowbar that levers against the large tooth on the inside edge and is supported by the rotary shredder (P10) close to one of the two shredder blades. (See photo below.)
```

- content:

Mark on the end shield (P3) the position of the strap (P19) and then remove it by loosening two nuts (P16). Turn the grinder over again. Loosen the remaining two nuts with washers (P16) .
These four nuts (P16) must always be changed for new ones before reassembly, as they are lock nuts. Note the location of the disposer outlet on the end shield (P3) relative to the hole pattern on the hood (P15) upper side, so that the outlet later may be reassembled in the same direction. For this purpose, is also an arrow engraved on the hood (P15) lower edge, which when reassembling shall be above the outlet of the the end shield (P3). Remove the hood (P15) .
Remove the seal (P14) on the stationary shredder. This seal must always be replaced with a new seal when reassembled.
Pry the stationary shredder (P13) loose from the end shield (P3) using a crowbar that levers against the large tooth on the inside edge and is supported by the rotary shredder (P10) close to one of the two shredder blades. (See photo below.)
Loosen the axle screw (P12). If the rotating shredder (P10) keeps turning, and cannot be held still manually, use a pipe wrench to hold one of its shredder blades. Alternatively, lock the rotary shredder with a screwdriver placed through one of the holes at the disposer outlet. (See photo below.)
```

Context: After this dismantling make sure that the surface of the sealing washer (P4) remains in good condition.
```

### chunk_982fb892682347d3995a460922cec1fb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_981acb7e64874676ac88f254e5317efd`

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer`
- element_ids (1): `el_1361a797c3fe4f9d98401ce6671d1cdc`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Examine the contact surfaces of the carrier (P2) for wear caused by the axle seals. There should only be two small symmetrical (barely noticeable) tracks on the carrier (P2) if it is to be reused. If the tracks are noticeable, asymmetrical or more than two, replace the carrier.
```

### chunk_192fc71782a54df483fe2a26f89e6c92
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_981acb7e64874676ac88f254e5317efd`
- sequence_number: `156`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `3/4`
- chunk type: `spare_parts_table`
- page_start/page_end: `35`
- token_count: `179`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Spare Parts`
- element_ids (3): `el_708a09c97326455c9fbe745344ad8dd9, el_bc43f9be61ae42b193181887d1ca9225, el_36eadaf6e81841bb9da70cc609bd219d`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
The V-ring seal (P8) and the two axle seals (P5) with special grease (P26) together with stationary seals (P14) and (P17) must be replaced at each dismantling.
In Service Package 2 a disassembly screw (P31) for the carrier (P2) is included. Use this screw on top of the carrier and screw it down. This will remove the carrier from the motor axle. If needed, use the rotary shredder (P10) as a handle to prevent the motor axle from rotating. Alternatively, hold the carrier with a pair of pliers and a soft cloth in between.
When changing the motor (P1), make sure that the new motor has the same classification and quality as the original. The motor must have two holes in the upper flange to allow for ventilation and drainage of water condensation (see arrows on photo below and original motor). When drilling these holes in the motor upper flange, it is important to drill at the correct angle, so that it does not penetrate the motor winding. All replacement motors delivered by the manufacturer have premade drainage holes.
```

### chunk_f8d5d3e9df9e43379c67decec939d1d3
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_aad699ab973445658a10fa538e382339`
- sequence_number: `158`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/5`
- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `36`
- token_count: `350`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (21): `el_8bf48b2275d04057968d5f657ae4112c, el_3e8af6ed4a2c4346bcce70a7235ab3f9, el_d2f7199a95d7472aac64a9ff9e63d9b9, el_6dd0b8e04ed141c7bbfbc64bd5996632, el_641b1cd501e949e5bf102f9d2d9af7be, el_5851f4a3716a4091841cfffcffc47798, el_73e9946707ad499989a45c008ac4658c, el_d015f2c0cd194e52ae4df7f2466cc2b9, ... (+13 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Assembly of Disposer

Clean all components carefully. Wipe the surface of the motor (P1) upper flange and its shaft. Make sure that there is no dust or grease residue.

Make sure that the motor shaft key (P30) is in the correct position. Apply a thin layer of manufacturer specified special grease (P26) on the motor shaft.

Wipe the carrier (P2) clean on the inside and outside.

Mark onto the motor shaft the depth of the hole in the carrier (P2). From the top of the motor shaft, this measure is 38 mm for models 510, 515, and 48 mm for model 520, and 58 mm for models 530, 550 and 575. Carefully press the carrier (P2) onto the motor shaft all the way down to the mark. If hand power is not enough, use the axle screw (P12) as a mounting tool. If needed, use the rotary shredder (P10) as a handle to prevent the carrier from rotating.

On the end shield (P3), clean the center hole for the axle seals (P5)

.

Place the washer (P4) in the end shield with the flange facing towards the motor (see the sketch below under item 8, where the parts are seen with the end shield placed upside down as on the photo to the left).

Grease the sealing face of this center hole in the end-shield (P3) with special grease (P26)

.

To avoid damaging the seals (P5, 2pcs), press each seal one at the time into position in the end shield using a tool (for example a shaft end or other round bar, see below photo) that has the same outer diameter as these seals. The seals shall be placed with the opening and stainless spring upwards towards the washer (P4) according to the below sketch.

The seals are fixated by fitting the locking ring (P6) into the groove in the end shield (P3) according to the above sketch under item 8. Tap on this ring to ensure that it is seated properly in the groove in the end shield.

P3

P6

P5

P4

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

### chunk_537ff5850b5d48dba08581f697c75966
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_b3829490b7e54f3aaab7a5dbffff3e11`
- sequence_number: `159`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/4`
- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `37`
- token_count: `304`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (21): `el_0bcfcb90bf704db7866dbabee92b6bf9, el_22e587261138419ab73310b18c3e4c7a, el_ffa3a952b3a14ff6a94ef39ceaa4cb67, el_d48cef15a23940db8e597b0a2d7f6ff3, el_3ef0008ecaf34eeaab37ac49546504a8, el_a5e391c145734a3bb7d90b6b619adb0e, el_507de323e09a4cba8a8918d710d0e4a2, el_ff772068dfe64a6bab82bc5670dc973d, ... (+13 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Responsible Solutions

Engineered

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

With a clean and soft putty knife fill the grooves along the sealing lips in the axle seals (P5) with special grease (P26). Be sure no dirt or particles happen to fall into this greasing.

P26

With a clean soft cloth lubricate a thin layer of special grease (P26) on the carrier (P2) sealing surface for the axle seals (P5) .

With reference to section 9.4, item 13 above, refit the end shield (P3) so that its outlet ends up 180° offset from the motor (P1) terminal box for cable connection. Take great care so that the lips on the axle seals (P5, 2pcs) not are damaged. For disposer models 510, 515 and 520 secure the end shield with tightening torque 47 Nm on the four screws (P7), and for models 530, 550 and 575 secure the end shield with tightening torque 81 Nm on the four screws (P7) .

Grease the sealing lip of the V-ring seal (P8) and its seal washer (P4) using special grease (P26) . Fit the V-ring seal over the carrier (P2) with its seal lip against the seal washer (P4) .

Insert a small click of special grease (P26) into the keyway of the carrier (P2) and push on the key (P9) .

Wipe clean the axle hole in the rotary shredder (P10). Also make sure that the mating surfaces on the end shield (P3) and the stationary shredder (P13) are clean.

Carefully fit the rotary shredder (P10) on the carrier (P2) .

Wipe the carrier (P2) clean from excess grease.

Apply a layer of rubber sealing compound (P27) over the joint between rotary shredder and carrier (see photo below). Make sure that no sealing compound enters the screw hole of the carrier.

P2

P8

P4

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

### chunk_cc60af292f5143f09507c9ad8331beb0
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_394163a017da45a9910b2b3294d960aa`
- sequence_number: `160`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/4`
- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `38`
- token_count: `271`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (13): `el_6eafa422e4084e27bb732f3c47f509cf, el_f135d813b93f48e2a412fdf9729a51b4, el_8cad5a61485e4f25ab9bf5914ebfa4db, el_7ae14ad79cc34436b20b64c59412ec54, el_5eee62828ecd4723a0d61a312e74ed75, el_5db97aeeff77417f9fb8dbb7e9f70931, el_d765376acb984cae84cae6e78df7eeac, el_88007a14f2dc436d8477f756c965073c, ... (+5 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Fit the axle washer (P11) on the rotary shredder. Apply rubber sealing compound around the underside of the entire head of the axle screw (P12) and mount this screw. Make sure that sealing compound is squeezed out around the entire circumference of the axle washer, and also around the entire head of the axle screw. For disposer models 510 and 515 the axle screw is tightened with torque 9,3 Nm, and for model 520 the axle screw is tightened with torque 22 Nm, and for models 530, 550 and 575 the axle screw is tightened with torque 44 Nm.

Apply a thin uninterrupted string of rubber sealing compound (P27) in the seat of the end shield (P3) in which the stationary shredder (P13) should be pressed down. See below photo.

Carefully position the stationary shredder (P13) with its upper large tooth closest to the outlet of the end shield (P3) .

Tap around the top of the stationary shredder (P13) with a plastic hammer to fix it in the end shield (P3), and make sure that the stationary shredder is all the way down in the seat of the end shield.

Secure the stationary shredder (P13) to the end shield (P3) by hammering 6 punch marks on the end shield with even distances around the joint between these two articles. See below photo.

Fit a new rubber seal (P14) on the top of the stationary shredder (P13). Make sure that it is centered.

Wipe clean the surface of the hood (P15) that faces the rubber seal (P14) .

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

### chunk_742bcb0e01164714a6087e5812e46021
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_d46576028bd3495781292143a1eb2c36`
- sequence_number: `161`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/3`
- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `39`
- token_count: `393`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (12): `el_0611e19aff5c47328cf4657b38b3c4fc, el_e14899f0a0574bd69c927e2505bb5da3, el_a54308f5a7cc47489cd1c0165d756e85, el_1b4dc39eea3c4c14b30a549717cc19ba, el_d861c8ebef964f71838942b660bed557, el_aaa08b54838e4ffca913f26683ca490c, el_a398bacd2d674ec99a84ffbfa40f043d, el_c451d58f89ea4b1ba901d82ffc83babb, ... (+4 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Carefully fit the hood (P15). For the upper hole pattern of the hood to end up in the same way as before dismantling, the outside arrow mark on the hood lower edge shall be positioned centrally above the waste outlet on the end shield (P3). This to make sure that the disposer outlet will have the same direction towards the sewer connection as before. Also feel with your fingers that the seal (P14) still is centered.

Secure the hood (P15) with 2 pcs. of washers and 2 pcs. of new unused locking nuts (P16) in the two positions that have no previously made markings for the strap (P19, see section Dismantling of Disposer, item 7 above). Tighten these two lock nuts (P16) only a little so that the seal (P14) is fixed. The final tightening is made after the strap (P19) has been fitted as per item 29 below.

For disposers installed in cabinet models (e.g. model 500-MB and 500-MC) the parts with position number P19 up to and including P25 are not used. For these disposers secure the hood (P15) with 4 pcs. of washers and 4 pcs. of new unused locking nuts (P16). Tighten all these lock nuts crosswise with torque 9,5 Nm for models 510, 515 and 520, and with 21 Nm for models 530, 550 and 575. Continue the assembly of the disposer installed in cabinets from item 34 below.

Turn the disposer upside down.

Place the strap (P19) diagonally over the two remaining screws and fix it with 2 pcs. of washers and 2 pcs. of new unused locking nuts (P16). Tighten all four lock nuts crosswise. For models 510, 515 and 520 with torque 9,5 Nm and for models 530, 550 and 575 with 21 Nm.

Pass the motor cable through the cable gland (P22) in the disposer housing (P20) .

Mount the disposer housing (P20) over the motor (P1). While the housing is being lowered over the motor, pull the cable so that the remaining part of the cable comes out through the cable gland. Make sure that the sealing strip (P21) is in the right place on the housing. Also make sure that the six small "heels" of the housing (P20) end up inside the lower edge of the hood (P15) .

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

### chunk_4e0a4bc56a8a4b59a6e2dc156c12f1f1
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_8fa9c3e157da4dac9f36dbab0461c39e`
- sequence_number: `162`

- element_ids (19): `el_a21640a9d3b24f9d8849bcfa9ecd137b, el_4b49325ed2b94742ad42ce06327b04f2, el_2fc63322c1154158a5ce2a2b8c75139a, el_412cfac339b143b78404f995e49ef5e9, el_277f52e1df3b4ed0a6f74d7989581c2a, el_97fd5f1569664962ae18648bbc7c97f9, el_348e592603a147ec8c06c7a0291c1f3b, el_eedcaaa45f6b415db3fcb7a0914927e8, ... (+11 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered

P21

Fasten the housing (P20) to the strap (P19) with 2 pcs. of screws and 2 pcs. of washers (P23) .

Pull out the cable as far as it goes through the cable gland. Then feed back about 5 cm to get a good relief of the cable. Lock the cable by tightening the nut of the cable gland (P22). Turn the disposer over again.

Check with a blade measure that the stationary seal (P14) is properly fixed between stationary shredder (P13) and hood (P15) .

The disposer is now ready to be remounted under the assembly using the same trolley with lifting plate (or the same steady jack with lifting plate) as previously used when the disposer was dismantled. Be aware of the motor power supply cable so it does not get pinched and damaged.

Make sure that the seal for the hood (P17) is placed correctly before you move the disposer into position under the assembly for mounting with 6 pcs. of screws and nuts (P18) .

Refit any disposer fixation to the wall/bulkhead or floor/floor plate.

Reconnect and seal the water trap with sewer pipe to the outlet on the disposer end shield (P3) .

Reconnect the disposer motor (P1) connection cable to the start & stop control unit according to the electrical connection diagram supplied with the machine from Disperator.

Reconnect electrical supply using the main electrical safety breaker on the wall/bulkhead.

Reconnect the flushing water supply to the disposer by open the valve in the supply line.

Start up and test the disposer according to applicable start-up procedures described in section 6.5 of this user manual.

1214

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Spare Parts 7.1.12
```

Clean all components carefully. Wipe the surface of the motor (P1) upper flange and its shaft. Make sure that there is no dust or grease residue.
Make sure that the motor shaft key (P30) is in the correct position. Apply a thin layer of manufacturer specified special grease (P26) on the motor shaft.
Wipe the carrier (P2) clean on the inside and outside.
Mark onto the motor shaft the depth of the hole in the carrier (P2). From the top of the motor shaft, this measure is 38 mm for models 510, 515, and 48 mm for model 520, and 58 mm for models 530, 550 and 575. Carefully press the carrier (P2) onto the motor shaft all the way down to the mark. If hand power is not enough, use the axle screw (P12) as a mounting tool. If needed, use the rotary shredder (P10) as a handle to prevent the carrier from rotating.
On the end shield (P3), clean the center hole for the axle seals (P5)
.

Grease the sealing face of this center hole in the end-shield (P3) with special grease (P26)
.
To avoid damaging the seals (P5, 2pcs), press each seal one at the time into position in the end shield using a tool (for example a shaft end or other round bar, see below photo) that has the same outer diameter as these seals. ### chunk_a178003c13894ea594b3aaf268558d8c
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_aad699ab973445658a10fa538e382339`
- sequence_number: `164`
