- element_ids (9): `el_6dd0b8e04ed141c7bbfbc64bd5996632, el_641b1cd501e949e5bf102f9d2d9af7be, el_5851f4a3716a4091841cfffcffc47798, el_73e9946707ad499989a45c008ac4658c, el_d015f2c0cd194e52ae4df7f2466cc2b9, el_2dea7215483e4a6b8d0837fcea24274a, el_00b7307b8f644101932545b936a63f65, el_e59769244a3741bca8dd118dbc2fb307, ... (+1 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Wipe the carrier (P2) clean on the inside and outside.
Mark onto the motor shaft the depth of the hole in the carrier (P2). From the top of the motor shaft, this measure is 38 mm for models 510, 515, and 48 mm for model 520, and 58 mm for models 530, 550 and 575. Carefully press the carrier (P2) onto the motor shaft all the way down to the mark. If hand power is not enough, use the axle screw (P12) as a mounting tool. If needed, use the rotary shredder (P10) as a handle to prevent the carrier from rotating.
On the end shield (P3), clean the center hole for the axle seals (P5)
.
Place the washer (P4) in the end shield with the flange facing towards the motor (see the sketch below under item 8, where the parts are seen with the end shield placed upside down as on the photo to the left).
Grease the sealing face of this center hole in the end-shield (P3) with special grease (P26)
.
To avoid damaging the seals (P5, 2pcs), press each seal one at the time into position in the end shield using a tool (for example a shaft end or other round bar, see below photo) that has the same outer diameter as these seals. The seals shall be placed with the opening and stainless spring upwards towards the washer (P4) according to the below sketch.
The seals are fixated by fitting the locking ring (P6) into the groove in the end shield (P3) according to the above sketch under item 8. Tap on this ring to ensure that it is seated properly in the groove in the end shield.
```

- content:

.
To avoid damaging the seals (P5, 2pcs), press each seal one at the time into position in the end shield using a tool (for example a shaft end or other round bar, see below photo) that has the same outer diameter as these seals. The seals shall be placed with the opening and stainless spring upwards towards the washer (P4) according to the below sketch.
The seals are fixated by fitting the locking ring (P6) into the groove in the end shield (P3) according to the above sketch under item 8. Tap on this ring to ensure that it is seated properly in the groove in the end shield.
```

- content:
```text
Context: With a clean and soft putty knife fill the grooves along the sealing lips in the axle seals (P5) with special grease (P26). Be sure no dirt or particles happen to fall into this greasing.

P26

With a clean soft cloth lubricate a thin layer of special grease (P26) on the carrier (P2) sealing surface for the axle seals (P5) .

With reference to section 9.4, item 13 above, refit the end shield (P3) so that its outlet ends up 180° offset from the motor (P1) terminal box for cable
```

### chunk_fb3a3876a31b4b1abd52b7118fb866c8
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_b3829490b7e54f3aaab7a5dbffff3e11`
- sequence_number: `168`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `3/4`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `37`
- token_count: `253`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (8): `el_68636915b2d145c0b0f7dcbc8c0c6d02, el_a09aafbe8bd64e01beeb795ed3dda069, el_eb93d2223edd43e2897711fc9c3d6f28, el_33732675742b4ea28381c524fa92233a, el_b489c2499f2d487595e4e7da5feaf6b7, el_797fe0ed5bb843b8b29a3b6f32d38a41, el_5366ddd77c024ff7951b9ed4f2f461e5, el_ed6f59da13ce4ae4809f7ae5b6b07857`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
With a clean soft cloth lubricate a thin layer of special grease (P26) on the carrier (P2) sealing surface for the axle seals (P5) .
With reference to section 9.4, item 13 above, refit the end shield (P3) so that its outlet ends up 180° offset from the motor (P1) terminal box for cable connection. Take great care so that the lips on the axle seals (P5, 2pcs) not are damaged. For disposer models 510, 515 and 520 secure the end shield with tightening torque 47 Nm on the four screws (P7), and for models 530, 550 and 575 secure the end shield with tightening torque 81 Nm on the four screws (P7) .
Grease the sealing lip of the V-ring seal (P8) and its seal washer (P4) using special grease (P26) . Fit the V-ring seal over the carrier (P2) with its seal lip against the seal washer (P4) .
Insert a small click of special grease (P26) into the keyway of the carrier (P2) and push on the key (P9) .
Wipe clean the axle hole in the rotary shredder (P10). Also make sure that the mating surfaces on the end shield (P3) and the stationary shredder (P13) are clean.
Carefully fit the rotary shredder (P10) on the carrier (P2) .
Wipe the carrier (P2) clean from excess grease.
Apply a layer of rubber sealing compound (P27) over the joint between rotary shredder and carrier (see photo below). Make sure that no sealing compound enters the screw hole of the carrier.
```

### chunk_8cf86e3f9c5f4df1997d5555965a1e57
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_394163a017da45a9910b2b3294d960aa`
- sequence_number: `170`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/4`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `38`
- token_count: `258`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (7): `el_5eee62828ecd4723a0d61a312e74ed75, el_5db97aeeff77417f9fb8dbb7e9f70931, el_d765376acb984cae84cae6e78df7eeac, el_88007a14f2dc436d8477f756c965073c, el_77ab1ec4f2294931b50c67070201e5da, el_65e3ebb0c0e94831a6f6a2b846c9248c, el_e6057c6a25fd4517bc90d32d827ed7e6`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Fit the axle washer (P11) on the rotary shredder. Apply rubber sealing compound around the underside of the entire head of the axle screw (P12) and mount this screw. Make sure that sealing compound is squeezed out around the entire circumference of the axle washer, and also around the entire head of the axle screw. For disposer models 510 and 515 the axle screw is tightened with torque 9,3 Nm, and for model 520 the axle screw is tightened with torque 22 Nm, and for models 530, 550 and 575 the axle screw is tightened with torque 44 Nm.
Apply a thin uninterrupted string of rubber sealing compound (P27) in the seat of the end shield (P3) in which the stationary shredder (P13) should be pressed down. See below photo.
Carefully position the stationary shredder (P13) with its upper large tooth closest to the outlet of the end shield (P3) .
Tap around the top of the stationary shredder (P13) with a plastic hammer to fix it in the end shield (P3), and make sure that the stationary shredder is all the way down in the seat of the end shield.
Secure the stationary shredder (P13) to the end shield (P3) by hammering 6 punch marks on the end shield with even distances around the joint between these two articles. See below photo.
Fit a new rubber seal (P14) on the top of the stationary shredder (P13). Make sure that it is centered.
Wipe clean the surface of the hood (P15) that faces the rubber seal (P14) .
```

### chunk_3d15eb1630ca4116a227ff91bb247705
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_d46576028bd3495781292143a1eb2c36`
- sequence_number: `173`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/3`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `39`
- token_count: `380`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (7): `el_d861c8ebef964f71838942b660bed557, el_aaa08b54838e4ffca913f26683ca490c, el_a398bacd2d674ec99a84ffbfa40f043d, el_c451d58f89ea4b1ba901d82ffc83babb, el_22c434cc82fe421b8a28f7fd574272b0, el_eb70801ed7304009951786aea652d4aa, el_89fdd685e43740d390dd2b9825c6ae46`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Carefully fit the hood (P15). For the upper hole pattern of the hood to end up in the same way as before dismantling, the outside arrow mark on the hood lower edge shall be positioned centrally above the waste outlet on the end shield (P3). This to make sure that the disposer outlet will have the same direction towards the sewer connection as before. Also feel with your fingers that the seal (P14) still is centered.
Secure the hood (P15) with 2 pcs. of washers and 2 pcs. of new unused locking nuts (P16) in the two positions that have no previously made markings for the strap (P19, see section Dismantling of Disposer, item 7 above). Tighten these two lock nuts (P16) only a little so that the seal (P14) is fixed. The final tightening is made after the strap (P19) has been fitted as per item 29 below.
For disposers installed in cabinet models (e.g. model 500-MB and 500-MC) the parts with position number P19 up to and including P25 are not used. For these disposers secure the hood (P15) with 4 pcs. of washers and 4 pcs. of new unused locking nuts (P16). Tighten all these lock nuts crosswise with torque 9,5 Nm for models 510, 515 and 520, and with 21 Nm for models 530, 550 and 575. Continue the assembly of the disposer installed in cabinets from item 34 below.
Turn the disposer upside down.
Place the strap (P19) diagonally over the two remaining screws and fix it with 2 pcs. of washers and 2 pcs. of new unused locking nuts (P16). Tighten all four lock nuts crosswise. For models 510, 515 and 520 with torque 9,5 Nm and for models 530, 550 and 575 with 21 Nm.
Pass the motor cable through the cable gland (P22) in the disposer housing (P20) .
Mount the disposer housing (P20) over the motor (P1). While the housing is being lowered over the motor, pull the cable so that the remaining part of the cable comes out through the cable gland. Make sure that the sealing strip (P21) is in the right place on the housing. Also make sure that the six small "heels" of the housing (P20) end up inside the lower edge of the hood (P15) .
```

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (3): `el_412cfac339b143b78404f995e49ef5e9, el_277f52e1df3b4ed0a6f74d7989581c2a, el_97fd5f1569664962ae18648bbc7c97f9`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
Fasten the housing (P20) to the strap (P19) with 2 pcs. of screws and 2 pcs. of washers (P23) .

Pull out the cable as far as it goes through the cable gland. Then feed back about 5 cm to get a good relief of the cable. Lock the cable by tightening the nut of the cable gland (P22). Turn the disposer over again.

Check with a blade measure that the stationary seal (P14) is properly fixed between stationary shredder (P13) and hood (P15) .
```

### chunk_b9cb77476e59483189b08268f62da02c
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_8fa9c3e157da4dac9f36dbab0461c39e`
- sequence_number: `177`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `4/6`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `40`
- token_count: `185`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start > Disposer starts but there is no flushing water > Machine does not Start and makes no Sound > Maintenance 7.1.11 > Dismantling of Disposer > Assembly of Disposer`
- element_ids (8): `el_348e592603a147ec8c06c7a0291c1f3b, el_eedcaaa45f6b415db3fcb7a0914927e8, el_62fa03dffd9f49e39c37d6f67ad10ae9, el_9c6c2a8a0a124daeb5a288c1f3a88af9, el_73b9046a8b8247be8110dd9ba22f9f9c, el_4aea823459714024993c6e85940dea0a, el_8fbb39374a7847e28a71b4fe5d298b99, el_e44c6a8fe6e649e6bb81a1ea430700e3`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up > Operation 7.1.9 > Start and stop > Trouble Shooting 7.1.10 > Dis...`
- content:
```text
The disposer is now ready to be remounted under the assembly using the same trolley with lifting plate (or the same steady jack with lifting plate) as previously used when the disposer was dismantled. Be aware of the motor power supply cable so it does not get pinched and damaged.
Make sure that the seal for the hood (P17) is placed correctly before you move the disposer into position under the assembly for mounting with 6 pcs. of screws and nuts (P18) .
Refit any disposer fixation to the wall/bulkhead or floor/floor plate.
Reconnect and seal the water trap with sewer pipe to the outlet on the disposer end shield (P3) .
Reconnect the disposer motor (P1) connection cable to the start & stop control unit according to the electrical connection diagram supplied with the machine from Disperator.
Reconnect electrical supply using the main electrical safety breaker on the wall/bulkhead.
Reconnect the flushing water supply to the disposer by open the valve in the supply line.
Start up and test the disposer according to applicable start-up procedures described in section 6.5 of this user manual.
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Safety Precautions 7.2.1 > Owner / User Responsibility Section overview: Owner / User Responsibility The owner and/or user must have a sound...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Owner / User Responsibility

The owner and/or user must have a sound understanding of the operating instructions and warnings before using this equipment.

There are several forewarnings indicated throughout this manual that may stress a possible unsafe condition or important information regarding the use of this equipment.

The system operator must ensure that all relevant accident prevention regulations of their industry are observed and complied with. These safety regulations are only supplementary to the existing safety regulations of the entire system.

This manual has been prepared to provide you with a simple and understandable guide for equipment operation and maintenance. To keep your unit in top running condition, follow the specific maintenance procedures given in this manual.

Subsections: General Warnings:
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Safety Precautions 7.2.1 > Owner / User Responsibility > General Warnings: Section overview: General Warnings: WARNING: Ensure you have read...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: General Warnings:

WARNING: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and symbols and understand all instructions before installing, operating, or servicing this machine . Failure to do so could result in severe personal injury or property damage .

AL

CAUTION: When installing or maintaining the pre-screen, shut off and lock out power before removing any covers. Never make adjustments while in operation, except for those prescribed in this manual.

DANGER: Keep doors and guards in place at all times to avoid exposure to rotating parts. While the power is on, severe injury can occur if hands or feet reach inside the unit.

WARNING: A qualified electrician must perform all wiring. Failure to comply may result in severe personal injury or property damage.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Safety Precautions 7.2.1 > Owner / User Responsibility > General Warnings: > Electrical System Precautions Section overview: Electrical Syst...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Electrical System Precautions

The electrical equipment must be secured against unauthorised access and may only be tested and maintained by trained specialists.

WARNING: Electrical Hazard! ALWAYS check for no voltage before starting work!

When working on the switchgear and motors, the system must de-energized, tagged out and secured against re-connection in accordance with the ship's working practices and permit to work policy.

Subsections: Biohazard
```

- element_ids (8): `el_dc9c3431cae24fc7be85838bf94f1c37, el_ac8b3ac6c3b44beeb474183ee8ad1e24, el_2a3aa36849074870aa3ca4b6fb3b06c4, el_81dbc85b93e14684b79b0f70e586945c, el_4400f5dee9144d34bd05ca3114105a77, el_411b251479284f09b1ac48c0aa30398c, el_bf1f5a7186cb48e597de6ea60263b2af, el_e6282d3deae244e9ab407cc8e59fd40b`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Safety Precautions 7.2.1 > Owner / User Responsibility > General Warnings: > Electrical System Precautions > Biohazard Section overview: Bio...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Biohazard

The food waste press can contain a variety of bacteria and viruses. In general, these are nonpathogenic strains, however, the inhalation of aerosols and contact with the skin and mucous membranes should be avoided.

WARNING: Biohazard! When working on the system the operator must wear suitable clothes, eye protection and rubber gloves suitable for contact with wastewater. Failure to do so could lead to illness or death.

The nature, control and extent of the necessary vaccinations and pre/post treatment examinations should be determined by the ship or company medical professionals.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

### chunk_186fb54ae9324ec4825775cb356e80b7
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_1e457a61d9384f9b8e70d7c1e2eeb7ee`
- sequence_number: `204`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `50`
- token_count: `108`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Safety Precautions 7.2.1 > Owner / User Responsibility > General Warnings: > Electrical System Precautions > Biohazard > Food Waste Press Description 7.2.2`
- element_ids (4): `el_5f7235c37e4340bb94907f750f318348, el_c5e09e9e01e0473b93bb70c76c16e876, el_1ab81696de1f45fba9742ce834022d02, el_46e2d39cb5ce4185bab8eff6db571d11`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Safety Precautions 7.2.1 > Owner / User Responsibility > General Warnings: > Electrical System Precautions > Biohazard > Food Waste Press De...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Food Waste Press Description 7.2.2

The FMD food waste press is exclusively designed for separating solids from wastewater at a maximum inlet flow rate of up to 20 m³/hr (the 'intended use'). Intended use also includes compliance with the operating, maintenance and servicing conditions prescribed in this document.

The press must not be used for flow rates higher than those specified and must not be subjected to inlet pressure in excess of 100mbar.

Any use above and beyond the "intended use" is considered improper and the manufacturer assumes no liability for damage resulting from this, such risk is borne solely by the operator.

Subsections: Technical Data
```

- content:

The FMD food waste press is exclusively designed for separating solids from wastewater at a maximum inlet flow rate of up to 20 m³/hr (the 'intended use'). Intended use also includes compliance with the operating, maintenance and servicing conditions prescribed in this document.
The press must not be used for flow rates higher than those specified and must not be subjected to inlet pressure in excess of 100mbar.
Any use above and beyond the "intended use" is considered improper and the manufacturer assumes no liability for damage resulting from this, such risk is borne solely by the operator.
```

### chunk_56aa3eb0493f4756866db214264c64ed
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_5e1bec31031844b0815dfa1af78cfcdc`
- sequence_number: `208`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `installation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `51`
- token_count: `46`
- section_path: `4 Installation > What it Does 7.2.4`
- element_ids (1): `el_3d7e758664f3405cbecb5a5f56f70584`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 4 Installation > What it Does 7.2.4 The press separates the solids from wastewater by means of a 150 micron screen and a rotating screw. The screened liquid falls by gravity to t...`
- content:
```text
The press separates the solids from wastewater by means of a 150 micron screen and a rotating screw. The screened liquid falls by gravity to the intermediate tank and the solids fall into the wheelie bin, via the solids discharge chute, into an endless plastic bag.
```

### chunk_b8a94e9911b147eb8eebad00904ae8ba
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_dab3face7f1b42db85cdacb4840d4564`
- sequence_number: `209`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `installation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `51`
- token_count: `170`
- section_path: `4 Installation > How it Works 7.2.5`
- element_ids (6): `el_c16ba58d1f0d48e4ab591eba1401a5fa, el_dce29b3ed43a48e193a3ff32100e41c9, el_31476925b3794f86a954d0a78605e72e, el_a02ae61d7a4848aba9e106dfa5e5c8ef, el_9aa8d5d0548d4ca2b756eb6fb9fdeb8a, el_dd94cfb3df434db382d73a2f338d8837`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 4 Installation > How it Works 7.2.5 The press has been designed specifically for screening solids from wastewater and sludge streams. The inlet wastewater should be such that it...`
- content:
```text
The press has been designed specifically for screening solids from wastewater and sludge streams. The inlet wastewater should be such that it freely flows into the press without being pressurized.

The principle is simple: a slow rotating screw is enclosed in a basket with a "press zone" integrated at the end which is controlled and adjusted automatically by the air cylinder load on the conical end cap.

The liquid passes through the openings of the screen basket while the slowly rotating screw transports the retained solids to the press zone.

In the press zone the material is compressed, de-watered and finally pushed through the conically tapered end cap to remove the solids with a consistent dryness quality.

The solid content is a function of the input material, the installed basket screen size, and the press zone cone pre-load (which is adjusted using a precision air pressure regulating valve).

The pressed solids drop by gravity through the solids discharge chute into the endless bag which is contained in the wheelie bin.
```

The pressed solids drop by gravity through the solids discharge chute into the endless bag which is contained in the wheelie bin.
```

- content:
```text
Lifting & Positioning
WARNING: During lifting and transporting of loads, there is the risk that lifting accessories may fail. Never step under a suspended load, keep a safe distance and cordon off the danger area.
CAUTION: Ensure that the used lifting equipment is adequate for the load specified.
When lifting the press with a crane or chain block, it is only permitted to lift the machine by the provided holders:
Under no circumstances is the machine to be lifted by the pneumatic cylinder or the front plate eye:
©Copyright 2026 FMD
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 4 Installation Engineered 7.2.6.1 Mounting The frame to which the press is bolted must be flat and horizontal. If this is not the case, the screw separator alignment will be dist...`
- content:
```text
Engineered

7.2.6.1 Mounting

The frame to which the press is bolted must be flat and horizontal. If this is not the case, the screw separator alignment will be distorted when bolted down to an uneven surface – and this will potentially damage the screw separator and invalidate the warranty.
```

### chunk_3727d69d773e4434a1b34fff78608b38
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_48f9231f335d45369dd58e3d3d79b412`
- sequence_number: `217`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `drawing_reference`
- page_start/page_end: `53`
- token_count: `77`
- section_path: `4 Installation > 7.2.6.1 Mounting`
- element_ids (1): `el_99111c3be1b5432a9082cb49731ecf55`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (1): `picture_9afba5433ac44afc9d20a42f6720bb41`
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 4 Installation > 7.2.6.1 Mounting Context: The frame to which the press is bolted must be flat and horizontal. If this is not the case, the screw separator alignment will be dist...`
- content:
```text
Context: The frame to which the press is bolted must be flat and horizontal. If this is not the case, the screw separator alignment will be distorted when bolted down to an uneven surface – and this will potentially damage the screw separator and invalidate the warranty.

CAUTION: If the press is solidly bolted to an uneven surface there may be severe misalignment that can permanently damage the press! Inform FMD immediately and carefully document the incidence.
```

- element_ids (3): `el_c886ee3010234092a279eb253895e5af, el_c14f57febd894dffb1aeedab557aab51, el_51adbe59c31d4eb5894b5fc0b36b3845`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 4 Installation CAUTION: If the press is solidly bolted to an uneven surface there may be severe misalignment that can permanently damage the press! Inform FMD immediately and car...`
- content:
```text
CAUTION: If the press is solidly bolted to an uneven surface there may be severe misalignment that can permanently damage the press! Inform FMD immediately and carefully document the incidence.

7.2.6.2 Electrical Installation

If all mechanical connections are examined and satisfactory, the drive can be connected according to the switching plan attached to the terminal box.

Thermal motor protection (T1 and T2) is only optionally installed.
```

### chunk_0440aa80cbca48ef91fc2625f9aa43a1

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 4 Installation > 7.2.6.2 Electrical Installation > Safety Instructions The press must be earthed according to the ship's electrical requirements. WARNING: For the electrical conn...`
- content:
```text
The press must be earthed according to the ship's electrical requirements.
WARNING: For the electrical connections, the relevant safety regulations should be followed! Electrical installation must be in accordance with relevant standards and completed by a qualified electrician.
CAUTION: The correct direction of rotation is to be observed; this is clearly marked by the red arrow on the drive. Incorrect direction of rotation can lead to the damage of system parts!
Picture: Direction of Rotation
```

- content:
```text
Prior to the first test run, all points below should be verified:
 Are all safety provisions available in accordance with the regulations?
 Are all electrical connections made, tested and accepted according to the standards?
 Are all transport locks removed?
 Are the inlet and outlet pipes connected?
 Open the inspection covers of the screen and check for any foreign objects such as construction or mounting materials, tools, rags etc.
```

### chunk_953c39087ed346c2a86c351edfc98af3
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_76a24d4ccd5c4283bc93106354cdb93b`
- sequence_number: `224`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/3`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `54`
- token_count: `286`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > 7.2.7.2 Initial Test Run > Safety Instructions`
- element_ids (12): `el_003896446fcd45d494eb14257f28159d, el_3b7f6119c5eb4c1aa196ca132c3532b8, el_dea349dc559a4d23a40acd5fc45447b4, el_c8bb692384c1423c941e5184a98fb264, el_a2065a8a87344ca1bf7ccef6d52fcca5, el_d454763f7882420dbc08e1a9a47250bc, el_0227068d3ad346bca7f957809ab76f83, el_8b0be15122074c24b7c18ec56f094210, ... (+4 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > 7.2.7.2 Initial Test Run > Safety Instructions WARNING: Risk of crushing / rupture of limbs! Ensure the inspectio...`
- content:
```text
WARNING: Risk of crushing / rupture of limbs! Ensure the inspection covers are fitted and secured before starting the machine.
If all the pre-commissioning points are checked and found in order, then the test run can be started by following the below procedure:
 The press gearmotor should be bumped without water to verify the direction of rotation is correct. The press must not be operated in the wrong direction for more than a few seconds.
 Before the first test run with water, the system must be ensured clear of debris, tools, sharp-edged objects, iron filings or other objects located upstream of the inlet. Such objects can damage the press.
 For the first test run with water, the air pressure regulating valve for the press zone should be set to ~1.5 bar. Check that the cone seals the press zone, a drip every 5-10 seconds is normal at this stage.
When starting up the press, liquid may leak briefly from around the press zone gasket.
For safety purposes protect yourself and other from any liquid that may potentially spray out if the doors on the discharge chute are open.
For the first run using wastewater do not run the feed pump at full power. Leave the TSP running for 30-60 minutes with the wastewater inlet flow at ~30% of maximum capacity during this period:
 Check the press for leaks.
 Check the solids bag is not filling with water.
 Observe the press discharge cone for leakage initially and later when using wastewater for movement to indicate a solids plug is forming.
 If the separation and the pressing result are within the required range, the inlet flow rate can be increased slowly.
```

```

### chunk_b18195dc51a34911b1fb64b762cad112
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_12d849b22f5b407e8e64d8f7ecdc8f9e`
- sequence_number: `226`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/2`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `55`
- token_count: `162`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8`
- element_ids (9): `el_a042e5ab54bd4cf8bd086596a834e228, el_4bbb3781fa42450681c398cbba3a679d, el_ed09f6c095724332b63e20893f15ac89, el_96915d95880f4b148d4634a5940a184d, el_08b02f89cac84b3a9580003a66763a4f, el_bdb1fb314fc84984874220d41dc33a63, el_3e0b81600cf241faa89d5d3b59983a20, el_a2c85099792344299ac82a1a41ae2794, ... (+1 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8  Never set the air pressure higher than 2.0 bar.  Once the plug is established the optimum air pressure is genera...`
- content:
```text
 Never set the air pressure higher than 2.0 bar.

 Once the plug is established the optimum air pressure is generally 0.6 – 0.8 bar for GW/BW and 1.0-1.5 bar for food waste.

 The higher the air pressure the higher the chance a blockage may form in the press, which in turn leads to increased wear, break down and possible irreparable damage.

 Observe the press for a while. Reduce or increase the pressing force until the solid discharge runs evenly and continuously.

 If the press begins to vibrate, this is often a sign of excessive pressing force and an impending blockage.

7.2.7.4 Shutdown

 Turn off and isolate the inlet supply pumps to prevent them starting.

 Wait until the feed pipes have emptied.

 Reduce the pressing force and move the compressed air cylinder to the rear.

 Let the separator run for a short while longer until the solids in the machine have been completely discharged.
```

### chunk_8d05a2f717d448858150c367459854de
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_94e7600881d24f8f87a1da530de49c1c`
- sequence_number: `227`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `55`
- token_count: `103`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > 7.2.7.4 Shutdown`
- element_ids (4): `el_e8b798b42819419baba2d248df8fed26, el_292edc7114f24fc4b844f414ceefae3f, el_844cc5cb6fb4456ea9fb9b0ee4b38d33, el_25d52dcd96c542f990da6d40a984e7e8`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > 7.2.7.4 Shutdown  The system must be thoroughly cleaned.  Put up signage to notify that the press has been take...`
- content:
```text
 The system must be thoroughly cleaned.
 Put up signage to notify that the press has been taken out of operation.
Take Note: If the press is to be idle or shut down for more than 72 hours, there is a risk that the solids plug (formed against the cone) will begin dry out and solidify – see below:
If the plug has dried out and the press is restarted, after a short time of operation the pressure behind the plug will increase until it releases suddenly - and there is the potential for wastewater to carry over into the solids bag.
```

### chunk_c41e3e0ff6c04d169ad24d25d7f4c847
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_1ddc71f6cf584f11aa9ebf16855600f1`
- sequence_number: `229`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/3`
- chunk type: `maintenance_interval`
- page_start/page_end: `56`
- token_count: `179`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Overview & Maintenance Intervals`
- element_ids (4): `el_2e88bfcb680d47fb96e0a44ed796fd15, el_254e6b9ca8f54076b8d3d7f072f0ae2a, el_e3d23b524fd443b3b85a437c5506ff51, el_89ecf8b3750f48c2b6a19da85906b0c6`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Overview & Maintenance Intervals To maintain operational readiness, possible damage should be detected at an early stage. To preserve warran...`
- content:
```text
To maintain operational readiness, possible damage should be detected at an early stage. To preserve warranty and guarantee entitlements the operator is obliged to carry out regular inspection and maintenance work on the system in accordance with the respective operating and maintenance instructions as detailed in 7.1.10 Failure to observe these regulations will lead to the invalidation of the warranty. Operating persons must be informed of, and understand, all safety requirements before beginning start-up and maintenance work. For all maintenance, inspection and repair works the press is to be shut down and isolated with the main power isolator locked out in accordance with the onboard regulations and permit system.
CAUTION: Pay attention to all safety instructions during all maintenance and servicing work and the safety policies of the vessel.
WARNING: Before working on the press, isolate the power supply and lock out or remove fuses. There is a risk of crushed hands and limbs from the rotating shaft/screw in the drive area.
All system parts and operating media connected upstream of the press must be protected against unintended start.
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Modifications to the Press 7.2.9

No modifications, attachments or rebuilding of the press may occur without the prior written authorisation of FMD.

Machine parts that are not in a safe usable condition are to be replaced immediately.

Take Note: Only original spare and wear parts may be used. Other parts are not warranted.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

Machine parts that are not in a safe usable condition are to be replaced immediately.
```

- element_ids (15): `el_153326d35cf2488c91a49a406db40839, el_c867173657ad4115939c926b35894c33, el_465a4643cf3c49189da23e33782ff21e, el_fab46858d0d24ae7a49a11ab1562e25b, el_6803c1af014b4a9f98f672559aeb8d74, el_3597fac5b8fc4781a5433c25ef3fe56a, el_f694d3547d354c948cc8c2ad1344b2bc, el_8ea8b69829d4445daeea801c996d5f10, ... (+7 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 Section overview: Preventi...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11

The instructions for all visual inspections, maintenance and repair work must be observed.

WARNING: Before working on the TSP, isolate the power supply and lock out or remove fuses. There is a risk of crushed hands and limbs from the rotating shaft/screw in the drive area.

The essential maintenance for the TSP is summarised in the Preventive Maintenance Tasks table where the following abbreviations/codes are used:

D Daily maintenance work

W Weekly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 100 operating hours

M Monthly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 450 operating hours

Q Quarterly maintenance work

S Half-yearly maintenance work

A Annual maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 5000 operating hours

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- element_ids (10): `el_c867173657ad4115939c926b35894c33, el_fab46858d0d24ae7a49a11ab1562e25b, el_6803c1af014b4a9f98f672559aeb8d74, el_3597fac5b8fc4781a5433c25ef3fe56a, el_f694d3547d354c948cc8c2ad1344b2bc, el_8ea8b69829d4445daeea801c996d5f10, el_507b292a2c4f4f73b3fdfd0188cc9056, el_9072c031783542609d8842164c1cb90b, ... (+2 more)`
- table_ids (1): `table_d6a04d24aff4455d914a00a72b6bb78a`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Safety Instructions The...`
- content:
```text
The instructions for all visual inspections, maintenance and repair work must be observed.
WARNING: Before working on the TSP, isolate the power supply and lock out or remove fuses. There is a risk of crushed hands and limbs from the rotating shaft/screw in the drive area.
The essential maintenance for the TSP is summarised in the Preventive Maintenance Tasks table where the following abbreviations/codes are used:
D Daily maintenance work
W Weekly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 100 operating hours
M Monthly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 450 operating hours
Q Quarterly maintenance work
S Half-yearly maintenance work
A Annual maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 5000 operating hours
| D                                     | Q  Q                                  | M  S  A                                                                                                          | Task Reference                        |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press                                                                            | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press |
| X                                     |                                       | General visual inspection daily or after period of particularly high  load                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check basket for fat, fibre growth or blockages & clogging                                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check the mechanics for possible malfunctions of the drive, shaft  bearing, cone etc                             |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check all nuts, screws, bolts and connecting elements for tightness                                              |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | The complete mechanics, connections and bearings must be  carefully checked and rectified as required.           |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw                                                                                 | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      |
| X                                     |                                       | Check that the screw runs evenly in the basket                                                                   |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                                                                                                      | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           |
| X                                     |                                       | Check gearbox for leaks                                                                                          | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Change the lubricant after every 6 months or after 1000 hrs of  operation and thoroughly flush the gear housing. | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check intake air filter for contamination, clean or replace as  required.                                        | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Clean dirt from the housing                                                                                      | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Clean fan cover, fan wheel, ventilation grille and cooling fins                                                  | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
```

The essential maintenance for the TSP is summarised in the Preventive Maintenance Tasks table where the following abbreviations/codes are used:
D Daily maintenance work
W Weekly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 100 operating hours
M Monthly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 450 operating hours
Q Quarterly maintenance work
S Half-yearly maintenance work
A Annual maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 5000 operating hours

|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press                                                                            | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press |
| X                                     |                                       | General visual inspection daily or after period of particularly high  load                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check basket for fat, fibre growth or blockages & clogging                                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check the mechanics for possible malfunctions of the drive, shaft  bearing, cone etc                             |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check all nuts, screws, bolts and connecting elements for tightness                                              |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | The complete mechanics, connections and bearings must be  carefully checked and rectified as required.           |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw                                                                                 | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      |
| X                                     |                                       | Check that the screw runs evenly in the basket                                                                   |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                                                                                                      | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           |
| X                                     |                                       | Check gearbox for leaks                                                                                          | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Change the lubricant after every 6 months or after 1000 hrs of  operation and thoroughly flush the gear housing. | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check intake air filter for contamination, clean or replace as  required.                                        | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |

| X                                     |                                       | Clean fan cover, fan wheel, ventilation grille and cooling fins                                                  | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |

| X                                     |                                       | General visual inspection daily or after period of particularly high  load                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check basket for fat, fibre growth or blockages & clogging                                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check the mechanics for possible malfunctions of the drive, shaft  bearing, cone etc                             |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check all nuts, screws, bolts and connecting elements for tightness                                              |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | The complete mechanics, connections and bearings must be  carefully checked and rectified as required.           |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw                                                                                 | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      |
| X                                     |                                       | Check that the screw runs evenly in the basket                                                                   |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                                                                                                      | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           |
| X                                     |                                       | Check gearbox for leaks                                                                                          | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Change the lubricant after every 6 months or after 1000 hrs of  operation and thoroughly flush the gear housing. | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check intake air filter for contamination, clean or replace as  required.                                        | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Clean dirt from the housing                                                                                      | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Clean fan cover, fan wheel, ventilation grille and cooling fins                                                  | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate

Loosen and remove the screws on the discharge chute retaining plate, remove the retaining plate including the air cylinder and press cone.

Subsections: Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure
```

- token_count: `58`

- element_ids (3): `el_d379b430ab454ad6bb14ff5104edfe8b, el_d9f3e6763fde4722a4c84798e4d84878, el_fdd71c44bb8244698fa0f41d6e8933b1`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning o...`
- content:
```text
Engineered

Removal of the Screen Basket

Now the screen basket can be pulled out of the separator using care.

Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming!

Once the screen basket has been pulled out of the housing by roughly half of its length, the initial resistance experienced reduces considerably.
```

Section overview: Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming!

Once the screen basket has been pulled out of the housing by roughly half of its length, the initial resistance experienced reduces considerably.

Picture: Removal of the Screen Basket

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Check the Screen Basket
```

- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_d11347d6335d47bdbce1f0eeb8f7bf5b`

- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `62`
- token_count: `90`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning of the Screen Basket 7.2.12 > Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate > Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure > Discharge Chute Removed > Removal of the Press Zone > Removal of the Screen Basket > Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming! > Check the Screen Basket`
- element_ids (8): `el_0e349d6706ae49eabcd5d4b47131ae75, el_7dc7f16ed4c642588466b7482ae375e4, el_9b81616ed51a4bd9ae95a50d36104576, el_0b0c0e406b534b0b9e3b5a2ca3c0cb8e, el_a7f472fd9961434ab2270c07deafcdd7, el_3c6543d4f011449bbdb144a205cd9d33, el_f2cd6b1d794a4e04a664aaa37fcdcb87, el_dea0b38831054129aaff466de6be8167`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning o...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Check the Screen Basket

When the screen basket has been removed and carefully cleaned, prior to re-installation it should be checked to see if damage exists on the surface and that seamless movement of the screw is ensured. The screen basket should not have any deformations or oval distortions. If there is any uncertainty as to what distortion tolerance is permissible, contact FMD.

Any new screen should also be subjected to an inspection for deformations and distortion prior to being fitted.

Picture: Screen Basket

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

### chunk_d82f89302ad74c3c952dc0eaaff33a85
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_ac112fbbe8a74a29997c829d39028756`
- sequence_number: `269`

- chunk type: `overview`

- token_count: `54`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning of the Screen Basket 7.2.12 > Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate > Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure > Discharge Chute Removed > Removal of the Press Zone > Removal of the Screen Basket > Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming! > Check the Screen Basket > Screen Basket Installation`
- element_ids (2): `el_86fa906cd23e433ba877a36fde1beac6, el_4cea2ceae04f43599c8fad12deda7c01`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning o...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Screen Basket Installation

The installation of a cleaned or new screen basket occurs in the reverse order to disassembly. When inserting the screen basket, be sure to push it in as straight as possible in order to prevent it jamming and potentially damaging the screw or basket.

Subsections: Fitting the Press Zone
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- content:
```text
Section overview: Fitting the Press Zone

If the screen basket has been inserted correctly, then the press zone can be re-attached to the press. Ensure it easily fastens without any gap to the main housing.

O

After attaching the press zone, check all screws and tighten to the correct torque of 35 Nm.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- sequence_number: `275`

- page_start/page_end: `64`
- token_count: `52`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning of the Screen Basket 7.2.12 > Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate > Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure > Discharge Chute Removed > Removal of the Press Zone > Removal of the Screen Basket > Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming! > Check the Screen Basket > Screen Basket Installation > Fitting the Press Zone > Maintenance of the Screw 7.2.13`
- element_ids (3): `el_65563129112c4d6eb6342e083be5f441, el_3249fd14aa5642ae95b8e0b6680c59dd, el_ffd2a61c5a11499d8e98a9b005e5be63`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning o...`
- content:
```text
If it is necessary to change the screw or carry out an inspection, then first remove the screen basket as described above. After dismantling the screen basket, the outer holding screws of the screw mounting plate can be released.
Rotate the screw fixing plate in an anticlockwise direction.
Removing the Fixing Plate
```

Context: If it is necessary to change the screw or carry out an inspection, then first remove the screen basket as described above. After dismantling the screen basket, the outer holding screws of the screw mounting plate can be released.
```

- content:
```text
The locating holes for the screw holder can be used for inserting a tool for pulling the screw from the shaft (available on request from FMD).

Once the screw has been released and the initial friction overcome, the screw can be pulled out carefully. Two people may be required to lift it out.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Pulling out the Screw Figure: CAUTION: As the screw is pulled through the rear hole, DO NOT allow the screw to drop freely into the housing!...`
- content:
```text
Figure: CAUTION: As the screw is pulled through the rear hole, DO NOT allow the screw to drop freely into the housing! The carbide edges of the screw are brittle and easily damaged by impact!
```

```

```

- content:
```text
At the end of the mounting zone, the resistance when introducing the screw will be noticeably greater. At this point, the fully home position should be reached carefully using a soft-face hammer.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Attaching the Fixing Plate Finally, the fixing plate must be attached to the shaft. In this process, the correct play-free installation must...`
- content:
```text
Finally, the fixing plate must be attached to the shaft. In this process, the correct play-free installation must be ensured and the correct tightening torque applied.
After this step, the screen basket can be reinserted as described previously.
```

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw`
- element_ids (3): `el_d15ccb74e7564ce5bd90554e806abb31, el_f44c33d051044d41b66f89aaa5790810, el_84f32607016641ae91871bee952652db`

- content:
```text
Section overview: Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw

If the screen basket and the screw are removed, maintenance work and replacement of the shaft and the shaft seals can be performed. To do this, the screw of the retaining plate is to be released on the gear side.

Subsections: Driving out the Shaft
```

If the screen basket and the screw are removed, maintenance work and replacement of the shaft and the shaft seals can be performed. To do this, the screw of the retaining plate is to be released on the gear side.
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Driving out the Shaft

The shaft can be driven forward to the press side by lightly hitting it with a soft-faced hammer. Ensure that the shaft is not knocked out completely.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

### chunk_b0e4429a58e04657bfb3bc0902e22f1b
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (3): `el_0ae1463c584c45bea110ab3c5f08a3a1, el_8d9c9722f8c64017a2cc1dd9911fcf15, el_333dc49079b54644a0f35030f5d9cd01`
- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft > Loosening and Removing the S...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Loosening and Removing the Shaft

If the shaft has been loosened, it can be pulled out to the front/separator side. The feather key will fall out and remain on the gearbox side. Make sure that the feather key does not fall out of the housing. The key and shaft should be cleaned of dirt and grease residues before they are reinstalled.

Subsections: Shaft seals in Position
```

```text
Section overview: Greasing the Holder

Once the shaft has been removed, the shaft seals located in the rear housing can be removed. After an inspection or replacement of the shaft, the shaft seals must always be replaced. The holder for the shaft and for the shaft seals must be cleaned and greased prior to inserting the shaft.

T0

Subsections: Feather Key Insertion
```

Once the shaft has been removed, the shaft seals located in the rear housing can be removed. After an inspection or replacement of the shaft, the shaft seals must always be replaced. The holder for the shaft and for the shaft seals must be cleaned and greased prior to inserting the shaft.

- token_count: `47`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft > Loosening and Removing the Shaft > Shaft seals in Position > Greasing the Holder > Feather Key Insertion`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft > Loosening and Removing the S...`
- content:
```text
After cleaning and applying grease, the shaft can be reinserted. During this process, it must be ensured that the bracket for the feather key on the shaft matches the location on the gear unit. The feather key can be introduced through light hitting with a soft-faced hammer.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > Mount the Retaining Plate Once the feather key has been inserted flush, the retaining plate can be attached. For corrosion protection, the grease discharged during...`
- content:
```text
Once the feather key has been inserted flush, the retaining plate can be attached. For corrosion protection, the grease discharged during installation can be distributed on the surface and the excess grease removed.
The retaining plate must be attached with the specified torque.
```

If both shaft seals have been inserted correctly, they seal flush with the housing. When the seals are properly inserted, the installation of the screw and the screen basket can be carried out as previously described.
```

### chunk_9d62cdf8599b4101b32e8d57952981c0
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c26d285e3fd847029790c19c5c7a0f75`

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions 7.3.1`
- element_ids (7): `el_eaa4da841e604b5389d8a3ed8729b697, el_af53096526eb43718743d846c190c650, el_51932f72c49546fe81bd0129023950bb, el_aeaccba6ab3f429aa876169f679a1940, el_1baac025e5014c89816fd13785df7cc8, el_abce47b5b5e546c6a73a5a9f39f4295d, el_dc66898fd03644b69468f27e0747945b`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions 7.3.1 Section overview: Safety Precautions 7.3.1 This manual contains basic instructions which must be observed whe...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Safety Precautions 7.3.1

This manual contains basic instructions which must be observed when installing, operating and servicing the vacuum / transfer pump. It is essential for the user / installer or responsible technician to read the manual thoroughly prior to installation and operation.

A copy of the manual must always be at hand where the pump is being used. In addition to the general safety instructions listed in the main manual section on safety, it is necessary to observe the specific safety rules included in the various sections of the manual.

It shall be the plant operator's responsibility to ensure that all maintenance, inspection and assembly work is performed by authorized and qualified personnel who have adequately familiarized themselves with the subject matter by studying this manual in detail.

Any work on the machine shall only be performed when it is at a standstill, it being imperative that the procedure for shutting down the machine described in this manual be followed. Pumps and pumps units which convey hazardous media must be decontaminated.

Replacement parts that have not been supplied by the manufacturer are not tested and not approved for use. The installation and usage of such replacement parts can have a negative impact upon the pump characteristics.

The manufacturer is not liable for any damages caused by the usage of non-original parts.

Subsections: Technical Data 7.3.2
```

- content:
```text
This manual contains basic instructions which must be observed when installing, operating and servicing the vacuum / transfer pump. It is essential for the user / installer or responsible technician to read the manual thoroughly prior to installation and operation.
A copy of the manual must always be at hand where the pump is being used. In addition to the general safety instructions listed in the main manual section on safety, it is necessary to observe the specific safety rules included in the various sections of the manual.
It shall be the plant operator's responsibility to ensure that all maintenance, inspection and assembly work is performed by authorized and qualified personnel who have adequately familiarized themselves with the subject matter by studying this manual in detail.
Any work on the machine shall only be performed when it is at a standstill, it being imperative that the procedure for shutting down the machine described in this manual be followed. Pumps and pumps units which convey hazardous media must be decontaminated.
Replacement parts that have not been supplied by the manufacturer are not tested and not approved for use. The installation and usage of such replacement parts can have a negative impact upon the pump characteristics.
The manufacturer is not liable for any damages caused by the usage of non-original parts.
```

- content:
```text
The illustrations in these instructions may not always correspond exactly to the installed machine. But they correctly describe the basic structure and the sequence of work steps.

The vacuum / transfer pump is a rotary lobe pump, which is of a positive displacement type. The main components which determine the pumping principle are two lobe rotors rotating in opposite directions within a housing, basically as shown below:
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4  The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).  The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) o...`
- content:
```text
 The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).

 The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) of the housing (2).

| (1)   | drive shaft   | (5)   | hull        |
|-------|---------------|-------|-------------|
| (2)   | housing       | (6)   | lobe rotor  |
| (3)   | shaft         | (7)   | plate       |
| (4)   | wear plate    | (8)   | front cover |
```

### chunk_854bb4e0fa954fe0b670600a1b7a79f4
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`
- sequence_number: `314`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `3/4`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `74`
- token_count: `110`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4`
- element_ids (6): `el_8a6ed4a56b7544c6a28580af08b8b892, el_9719ea85e9f14d2b8b7c18eb89fc4857, el_0f44bc14d92a42d982e5a4385985ac08, el_1d0d4229029d4cf6a5e3a158a7ef8e25, el_9356cb27486c467d909aeb36ee6f8c75, el_51cfeeb2bf324f638d697e34165860f6`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4  The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).  The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) o...`
- content:
```text
 The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).

 The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) of the housing (2).

 In the housing (2) the movement of the drive shaft (1) is transmitted to shaft (3b), which turns in the opposite direction.

 The pump housing is formed by the front plate of the housing (2), the two hulls (5), the two wear plates (4), the plate (7) and the cover plate (8).

 The lobe rotors (6) transport the medium within the pump housing.

 Different versions of this pump have different forms and numbers of lobe rotors (6).
```
