- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General Most rubber products may change their physical properties under unfavourable conditions or if treated improperly, which will r...`
- content:
```text
Most rubber products may change their physical properties under unfavourable conditions or if treated improperly, which will result in a shorter lifetime. They can become unsuitable for use through excessive hardening, ageing, degeneration or permanent deformation, or because of blistering, cracking or other damage appearing on the surfaces.

These changes may occur under the influence of oxygen, ozone, heat, light, humidity, solvents or because of storing the products under load.

If stored and treated properly, rubber products will retain their properties even during long term storage (some years) with little change. However, this does not apply to uncured rubber compounds.
```

### chunk_ae08c7476d004e7b80e9e68649233017
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- sequence_number: `319`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `certification_info`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- token_count: `119`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- element_ids (6): `el_ac94a83fd62c4ce8803b8b6979051990, el_ee4f40b841d14d8cba34a420c9fae7d2, el_f3ecd5c2058641d5ae78ba4e0f8c1e16, el_24d714038ad94030a5f2eb3fdcfef2e5, el_9c24cb99b07e49bc9850cf741c382b15, el_96f760ee7ece45b4ab407b7c032e0b13`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:  The environment in which rubber products are being kept must be cool, dry, free of dust and naturally ventilate...`
- content:
```text
 The environment in which rubber products are being kept must be cool, dry, free of dust and naturally ventilated.

 Rubber products must not be stored in the open, not even in an area sheltered from the weather.

 Rubber products should be stored at temperatures above minus 10 °C and not more than plus 15 °C. Storerooms should not be damp and there should be no condensation.

 Rubber products should be stored in an environment offering a relative humidity under 65 %.

 Rubber products must be protected against light, particularly direct sunlight or high UV artificial.

 Rubber products should be kept away from forced ventilation and draughts; under these conditions they should be wrapped.
```

### chunk_5895944bd3a94b6f979bd803bd0efbfb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- sequence_number: `320`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/2`
- chunk type: `certification_info`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- token_count: `55`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- element_ids (2): `el_92f9b47d799444139d1a23c53741ffc4, el_0289c98fbeff49908a6394c879f89182`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:  As ozone is very aggressive and harmful to rubber products, they should not be stored in the same room with equ...`
- content:
```text
 As ozone is very aggressive and harmful to rubber products, they should not be stored in the same room with equipment likely to produce ozone, e.g. electric motors or other equipment which could electrically discharge or cause sparks.
 Rubber products should not be stored together with solvents, oil, grease, lubricants or any chemicals.
```

- content:
```text
Unless specified otherwise the pump is designed for a horizontal mounting, bolted securely to a sub-structure. The vent plug of the housing must always be mounted at the top of the gear box.
```

### chunk_ca87010283374f6ca6b140c2f38c7fbb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (5): `el_0c7ef8381c1b467598f53ddbeb1d655e, el_3daf6ac9855f48f299cafbe387e66ec6, el_4716fc526f664ba09f6e701c08845e29, el_797c9d2931464788b9eebcdfd0ab31eb, el_a31da8ba5d324f5f8c86f05249396435`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump Check the alignment after a short test run and make corrections if necessary. 7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow After bolting the b...`
- content:
```text
Check the alignment after a short test run and make corrections if necessary.

7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow

After bolting the baseplate to the foundation, remove the coupling guard and check the alignment of the coupling with a ruler and re-align if necessary. There is danger of distorting the baseplate if the foundation is uneven which will result in misalignment.

Permitted maximum misalignment tolerances for the coupling between drive and pump are:

 Radial shift (1): 1% of the largest diameter (2)

 Angular misalignment (3): 1°30'
```

### chunk_2127cbc9e62846d69fd0138c1a953355
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_58cb0e0aa73d4668af0b467cfcc16698`
- sequence_number: `325`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `3/4`
- chunk type: `certification_info`
- page_start/page_end: `76 -> 77`
- token_count: `193`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump`
- element_ids (8): `el_f11aac0a09164181a287ed4af85bdf6a, el_f30031d6b8a1475bb9692e7ecd730dde, el_eaf596c75e3b4332b5bb752e3b6b873f, el_311f1ef0fea141ecae68d8568b6bc49d, el_0e018222f7d74c4dadda7b49d871dd42, el_c283d98f7e374c1c80a89396f0595cf4, el_874dd822717641429628b7b3fbaa1271, el_b7723b556de44d5892ec916ffb86234d`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump The lobe pumps are reversible and therefore direction of rotation can be selected to provide for the required direction of flow. There a...`
- content:
```text
The lobe pumps are reversible and therefore direction of rotation can be selected to provide for the required direction of flow.

There are two ways to change the direction of rotation/flow:

 Pumps with one drive shaft: by changing the direction of rotation of the drive

 Pumps with two drive shafts: by switching the cardan shaft (3) from drive shaft (1) to (2)

7.3.6.3 Piping System

 Clean the pipework and rinse thoroughly before installing the pump.

 Arrange the inlet and outlet pipework so that when the pump is not running, the pumped media is still present before and after the pump (pump remains flooded); sufficient pumped media should remain inside the pump head to lubricate the pump head components during restart.

 Connect the pipework ensuring sufficient pipe supports are used so that no external forces or stress act on the pump. Where required pulsation dampers or compensators can be used between the pump and pipework

The maximum allowable twisting loads (Fx, Fy, Fz) and bending loads (Mx, My, Mz) for the pump inlet and outlet connections have to comply with the requirements of API 676 and EN ISO 14847:
```

### chunk_bfbf866c2c6042ada564a8f84bd8d06a
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_7388b9b684724f269f46e2faf6267185`
- sequence_number: `326`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `technical_specification`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- token_count: `149`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System`
- element_ids (1): `el_5de7bb60cf9e43b79a6426a9bc53b93f`
- table_ids (1): `table_2c00c825fffa4e27b54c4c444f704636`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System  Connect the pipework ensuring sufficient pipe supports are used so that no external forces or stress act on th...`
- content:
```text
 Connect the pipework ensuring sufficient pipe supports are used so that no external forces or stress act on the pump. Where required pulsation dampers or compensators can be used between the pump and pipework

The maximum allowable twisting loads (Fx, Fy, Fz) and bending loads (Mx, My, Mz) for the pump inlet and outlet connections have to comply with the requirements of API 676 and EN ISO 14847:

|   Standard nominal diameter [DN] |   Fx, Fy, Fz [N] |   Mx, My, Mz [Nm] |
|----------------------------------|------------------|-------------------|
|                               50 |              650 |               350 |
|                               65 |              850 |               435 |
|                               80 |             1040 |               560 |
|                              100 |             1300 |               700 |
|                              125 |             1700 |               865 |
|                              150 |             1950 |              1050 |
|                              200 |             2600 |              1400 |
|                              250 |             3250 |              1750 |
|                              300 |             3900 |              2100 |
```

```

In particular for control systems the latest version of the EC-Directive for Machinery, Annex I, Section 1.2 Controls should be observed.
WARNING: Follow all local and national electrical codes for all wiring. A qualified electrician must perform all wiring. Failure to comply may result in severe personal injury or property damage
```

Take Note: Never run the pump dry! - A few rotations in dry condition will damage the rotor lobes.

Pay strict attention to the following:
```

### chunk_ba99e062e84a4830a8ecfd687e7edddb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (6): `el_3f1fb6300ebe40b3abff6098a3c1d2ba, el_5ae3f042508649ba9c86e5c0e1bcbbd8, el_2e8fc15380d944b9aa5fd56fdc85f89f, el_a828b29b49ae4679a4d036e5bd0ddbc5, el_e477235e79f743bd863a7b4f655a92a4, el_f52f9e8f007443689ca779b87e1bb3fa`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7 Take Note: Never run the pump dry! - A few rotations in dry condition will damage the rotor lobes. Pay strict attention to the f...`
- content:
```text
Take Note: Never run the pump dry! - A few rotations in dry condition will damage the rotor lobes.

Pay strict attention to the following:

 Before starting up for the first time, fill the pump with medium to prime it.

 Priming is vital to initially lubricate the rubber lobe parts.

 Open valves and vents before starting the pump.

 Check the direction of rotation and flow by briefly switching on the pump drive.
```

CAUTION: This a positive displacement pump, which is capable of generating pressures that could cause damage – in extreme cases the bursting of pipes or vessels.
```

```text
CAUTION: Possible damage to the pump by exceeding the pressure rating of the pump head and inlet and outlet connections. Never run the pump against a closed valve.
```

- content:
```text
 Stop the pump by turning off the power and make sure it cannot be turned on accidently.

 Drain the pump head and if necessary, wash through if there is a risk of freezing or if the medium may solidify.
```

- content:
```text
Where a standby pump is installed as a back-up for the main pump and it is likely to stand idle for an extended period then it is recommended that it is operated from time to time.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.1 Pump in General > Safety Instructions The pump head should be regularly washed through or rinsed out if sediment from the pump...`
- content:
```text
The pump head should be regularly washed through or rinsed out if sediment from the pumped media is likely to settle out in the pump head.
WARNING: Hands can be crushed by moving parts! Before opening the pump head (removing the cover plate) turn off the power supply and ensure it cannot be turned on accidently (remove fuse).
```

### chunk_792f47f7df2647cc81c13a8073536521
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_415ce38817ba4387bb81ad37623a6347`
- sequence_number: `337`

- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- token_count: `60`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.2 Lubricating the Shaft Seals`
- element_ids (2): `el_9790d720672d46f09ba745b974012a30, el_38e0e3c0bb4b457db2e7af0dd686dc72`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.2 Lubricating the Shaft Seals The pump shaft seals are lubricated with grease via two grease points on the side of the gear hous...`
- content:
```text
The pump shaft seals are lubricated with grease via two grease points on the side of the gear housing, with two grease outlet points on the opposite side.
Lubrication should always be carried out while the pump is running – never during standstill . For this purpose, a high duty water-resistant grease, type NLGI – class 2, should be applied.
```

### chunk_e7c5f82b820841ab9d31900009c66662
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_a29264ecc31c409f88c89e967e3fab93`
- sequence_number: `338`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `maintenance_interval`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- token_count: `67`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Lubrication Schedule`
- element_ids (4): `el_c39fecc32fa74c20879e58bda96ba72a, el_83dc49f72ee844f98e2072f609af2c94, el_4230ec2beeae40c394142bb965352fc2, el_405bd739e2e8460383b8a5108ac0017f`
- table_ids (1): `table_ea7ef79b10a84b62ae33e7b7345acadf`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Lubrication Schedule  After every 350 hours of operation NB: The filling quantity with the hand-lever grease gun should not exceed 2...`
- content:
```text
 After every 350 hours of operation
NB: The filling quantity with the hand-lever grease gun should not exceed 2 to 3 strokes per grease nipple.
Recommended Lubricating Grease:
| Temperature range of the grease [5。]   | Designation DIN 51502   | Example              |
|-----------------------------------------|-------------------------|----------------------|
| -30... +120                             | K 3K - 20               | "SHELL"Alvania G3    |
| -30 ... +180                            | KE 3R -30               | "KLUBER"PETAMOGHY443 |
```

### chunk_31e1845de1a2402eb64feb561e9f6e73
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_58cb0e0aa73d4668af0b467cfcc16698`
- sequence_number: `340`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `4/4`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- token_count: `61`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump`
- element_ids (4): `el_bf7199ae911449a18d8fd967a085420a, el_c4c81738725f414fabc253940fb04c61, el_5443f969be09484ebcc2a3f046d05972, el_b4abf966b2454f7691d37aa5c3431ad0`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump The deaeration screw (1) has always to be at the top position. The magnetic oil drain screw (3) has always to be on the lowest position....`
- content:
```text
The deaeration screw (1) has always to be at the top position. The magnetic oil drain screw (3) has always to be on the lowest position.

Draining

 Open the magnetic drain screw (3) and let all the oil flow out.

 Clean the magnetic oil drain screw (3) at every oil change.

 Close the magnetic oil drain screw (3).
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Filling  Fill through the deaeration screw (1) until approx. the middle of the sight glass (2).  Close the deaeration screw (1).`
- content:
```text
 Fill through the deaeration screw (1) until approx. the middle of the sight glass (2).

 Close the deaeration screw (1).
```

- chunk type: `technical_specification`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- token_count: `95`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.4 Oil Quantities & Specification`
- element_ids (5): `el_61642a8a722142f1b43cae82c46d37b9, el_1f10906a36024b16bb7d52d2735edabf, el_b8023050fb7348c89605d7de4ba79d9b, el_4ff6630da4f9423e94f4fba6be161328, el_9cd91346950c4f0e9014381d9c64a868`
- table_ids (1): `table_b9db2881f5bd4279b52977c915f7590a`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.4 Oil Quantities & Specification The housing of the pump requires the following oil quantity: | Rotary Lobe Pump Type | Oil quan...`
- content:
```text
The housing of the pump requires the following oil quantity:
| Rotary Lobe  Pump Type   | Oil quantity  horizontal   | Oil quantity  vertical   | Change interval*                                                                                                               |
|--------------------------|----------------------------|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                          |                            | XB 0.6L 0.91L            | First change of oil after approx.  500 hours of operation or  12 months  Then after each 2000 hours of  operation or 12 months |
Oil specification:
SAE 75W-90 API GL-4 (or API GL-5)
* Change intervals are the base to maintain warranty; in aggressive conditions (high/low temp and humidity, full load operation etc.) the change interval can be shortened significantly.
```

### chunk_fc1f4d0928ea4f11921ad1f562a8685b
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c30317eec5064800b125362066137459`
- sequence_number: `348`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/5`
- chunk type: `safety_warning`
- page_start/page_end: `83`
- token_count: `102`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly > Safety Instructions`
- element_ids (7): `el_d7b7ab5d499444d2974ddab5e0b192e3, el_7dd5859bfefe446591ebc09bc4d1b449, el_1f071a0d25db4c54a6cc0346d697c51a, el_86fa4d77642941478e325b221a09fba2, el_ede121e384164e38a7253fc3e4f3c380, el_dd5207d71b1e4803b9acb389e5aee1e2, el_7d27afb677214af793ce65db837321ca`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly > Safety Instructions BIOHAZARD: Risk of illness and disease from the pumped medium! Decontaminate and empty the...`
- content:
```text
BIOHAZARD: Risk of illness and disease from the pumped medium! Decontaminate and empty the pump before opening. Wear appropriate personal protection equipment.
DANGER: Hands can be crushed by moving parts! Before opening the pump head (removing the cover plate) turn off the power supply and ensure it cannot be turned on accidently (remove fuse).
 Remove screws/spring washers (5030/5040) or hex nuts/spring washers (5035/5040).
 Remove the cover plate (5000).
 Remove screws (2070 with O-ring (2075) and fuse (2260).
 Remove end parts (2080) with O-rings (2085).
 Pull off the lobe rotors (2010) separately using a lobe extractor (pull-off device).
```

DANGER: Hands can be crushed by moving parts! Before opening the pump head (removing the cover plate) turn off the power supply and ensure it cannot be turned on accidently (remove fuse).
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: 7.3.11.2 Assembly

Assembly is a reversal of the above disassembly procedure, but additionally:

 In case of visible wear of the housings, crescents or wear plates these should be replaced.

 Fasten screws/nuts with a torque wrench as given in the following table:

 Secure screw (2070) with Loctite 245 or similar

 Secure or seal other screws with material according to the section drawing

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.2 Assembly Assembly is a reversal of the above disassembly procedure, but additionally:  In case of visible wear of the housin...`
- content:
```text
Assembly is a reversal of the above disassembly procedure, but additionally:
 In case of visible wear of the housings, crescents or wear plates these should be replaced.
 Fasten screws/nuts with a torque wrench as given in the following table:
 Secure screw (2070) with Loctite 245 or similar
 Secure or seal other screws with material according to the section drawing
| Size      | Material                | Material   | Pos. no.   | Remarks              |
|-----------|-------------------------|------------|------------|----------------------|
| Size      | Stainless steel A...-70 | Steel 10.9 | Pos. no.   | Remarks              |
| M8        | 30 Nm                   | 40 Nm      | 2070       |                      |
| M10       | 40 Nm                   | 50 Nm      | 2070       |                      |
| M12       | 60 Nm                   | 90 Nm      | 2070       |                      |
| M16       | 150 Nm                  | 200 Nm     | 2070       |                      |
| M24       | 100 Nm                  | 120 Nm     | 2080       | Types MB only        |
| M45 x 1.5 | 100 Nm                  | 120 Nm     | 5450       | Types XLB-.../2 only |
```

```text
Section overview: Safety Precautions & Symbols

Take Note: Before using the pump carefully read the information contained in this instruction manual.

WARNING: Electrical Hazard! ALWAYS check for no voltage before starting work!

WARNING: Electrical connection must be carried out by suitably a qualified electrician.

Subsections: Technical Data 7.4.2
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.4 Liquor Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions & Symbols > Technical Data 7.4.2 > Description 7.4.3 Close-coupled, centrifugal pumps; electric motor with extended s...`
- content:
```text
Close-coupled, centrifugal pumps; electric motor with extended shaft directly connected to the pump with open impeller.

Intended use for moderately dirty liquids (maximum size of solids: 4 mm), non-explosive, nonaggressive for the pump materials. Liquid temperature from -10 °C to +90 °C.
```

Take Note: When first starting, check the direction of rotation is as per the arrow on the casing Never run the pump dry, only start the pump after filling with liquid and venting the case.

Pay strict attention to the following:
```

### chunk_bcc54e1a53d4465aa3584378a61427f8
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`

- token_count: `81`
- section_path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- element_ids (4): `el_d60a5c61bb3042048925b10640817f61, el_2e7cf981c1ff42078e68894bd6f6d234, el_cda03910172041bead8dfd575b06ecd9, el_5223778ddca944e092f39ad4dc090af0`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4  Open valves and vents before starting the pump.  Before starting up for the first time, fill the pump and vent the casing using the...`
- content:
```text
 Open valves and vents before starting the pump.
 Before starting up for the first time, fill the pump and vent the casing using the vent fitted.
 Check the direction of rotation and flow by briefly switching on the pump drive.
 If the pump discharge piping is routed above the liquor tank a non return valve will be required to prevent back filling of the food waste system, a valve suitable for water with sediment should be selected.
```

### chunk_17955f22d0174f7286e093d36d3bbe75
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_27994732b53344ccb02681021d647896`
- sequence_number: `369`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `troubleshooting`
- page_start/page_end: `89`
- token_count: `380`
- section_path: `7 Components > Troubleshooting 7.4.5`
- element_ids (1): `el_1836127f814b451db13b76d4590cbb1e`
- table_ids (1): `table_9521f157b00f46938882a940190a3d7d`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > Troubleshooting 7.4.5 | PROBLEM | PROBABLE CAUSES | PROBABLE CAUSES | POSSIBLE REMEDIES | POSSIBLE REMEDIES | |-------------------------------------------|--------...`
- content:
```text
| PROBLEM                                   | PROBABLE CAUSES   | PROBABLE CAUSES                                              | POSSIBLE REMEDIES   | POSSIBLE REMEDIES                                                                                                                                 |
|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| (1)  The motor does  not start            | 1a)               | Motor overload protection  cuts in                           | 1a)                 | Check the power supply and make sure that the  pump shaft is turning freely. Check that the  thermal overload protection has been set  correctly. |
| (1)  The motor does  not start            | 1b)               | Shaft locked                                                 | 1b)                 | Remove the cause of lockage.                                                                                                                      |
| (2)  Pump locked                          | 2a)               | Prolonged periods of inactivity  forms rust inside the pump  | 2a)                 | Free the pump by rotating the shaft manually.                                                                                                     |
| (2)  Pump locked                          | 2b)               | Presence of solids in impeller                               | 2b)                 | Remove the wet end casing and remove any  solid solids the impeller.                                                                              |
| (2)  Pump locked                          | 2c)               | Bearings seized                                              | 2c)                 | Replace the bearings.                                                                                                                             |
| (3)  Pump runs with  no discharge         | 3a)               | Possible air leak on suction.                                | 3a)                 | Check.                                                                                                                                            |
| (3)  Pump runs with  no discharge         | 3b)               | Suction is blocked.                                          | 3b)                 | Clean blockage from suction.                                                                                                                      |
| (4)  Low flow  discharge.                 | 4a)               | Presence of deposits or solid  on the impeller               | 4a)                 | Clean the impeller.                                                                                                                               |
| (4)  Low flow  discharge.                 | 4b)               | Impeller damaged or worn                                     | 4b)                 | Replace the rotor.                                                                                                                                |
| (4)  Low flow  discharge.                 | 4c)               | Worn pump case                                               | 4c)                 | Replace the pump casing.                                                                                                                          |
| (5)  Noise and  vibrations from  the pump | 5a)               | Rotating part unbalanced                                     | 5a)                 | Check that no solid bodies are obstructing the  rotor.                                                                                            |
| (5)  Noise and  vibrations from  the pump | 5b)               | Worn bearings                                                | 5b)                 | Replace the bearings.                                                                                                                             |
| (6)  Leakage from the  mechanical seal    | 6a)               | The mechanical seal has been                                 | 6a)                 | Replace the mechanical seal.                                                                                                                      |
| (6)  Leakage from the  mechanical seal    | 6b)               | run dry or has stuck  Mechanical is damaged                  | 6b)                 | Replace the mechanical seal.                                                                                                                      |
| (6)  Leakage from the  mechanical seal    | 6c)               | Slight initial drip during filling  or on first start - up . | 6c)                 | Replace the mechanical seal.                                                                                                                      |
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.5 Actuated Valves > Safety Precautions 7.5.1 Section overview: Safety Precautions 7.5.1 Take Note: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and understand all...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Safety Precautions 7.5.1

Take Note: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and understand all instructions before servicing this machine . Failure to do so could result in personal injury or property damage .

CAUTION: Before working on a valve ensure system pressure has been released and pipes drained.

CAUTION: When installing or maintaining the actuated valve shut off and lock out power before removing any covers. Never make adjustments while in operation, except for those prescribed in this manual.

WARNING: Electrical Hazard! ALWAYS check for no voltage before starting work!

Subsections: Valve Description 7.5.2
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Valve Description 7.5.2

The stainless steel one-piece flanged ball valve with full bore is designed for service with vacuum and up to nominal pressure at a max 80degC. with ISO5211 mounting pad for electric actuation.

O

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.5 Actuated Valves > Safety Precautions 7.5.1 > Valve Description 7.5.2 > Exploded View 7.5.3 > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Actuator Description 7.5.4 Sect...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Actuator Description 7.5.4

The actuators are suited to the operation of ball and butterfly valves with a 90° rotation angle. They feature a water-resistant body with overload protection and a heating device against condensation formation. Power is transferred to the driveshaft via a self-stopping reduction worm gear to achieve precision control.

OPEN

CLOS

Attention

nanaer

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Wiring Diagram 7.5.5
```

### chunk_279bab8041c149a486cf322029e6bafe
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (2): `el_193060216a724f8da989dc5504cadcdc, el_183ab38759924c13ad6fce83b603206e`
- table_ids (1): `table_ecb304379b9b44de89e9b54374cf07c1`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.5 Actuated Valves > Safety Precautions 7.5.1 > Valve Description 7.5.2 > Exploded View 7.5.3 > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Actuator Description 7.5.4 The...`
- content:
```text
The actuators are suited to the operation of ball and butterfly valves with a 90° rotation angle. They feature a water-resistant body with overload protection and a heating device against condensation formation. Power is transferred to the driveshaft via a self-stopping reduction worm gear to achieve precision control.
| Pos.  Description          |
|----------------------------|
| 1 Motor                    |
| 2  Terminal block          |
| 3 Valve position indicator |
| 4 Gearbox                  |
| 5  Manual override         |
| 6 Limit switches           |
| 7 Heater                   |
```

### chunk_fe5020f6b6324103b500945f1f01aad1
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_bf96696961d74983a11ab427e1cf7766`
- sequence_number: `388`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/2`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `95`
- token_count: `100`
- section_path: `7 Components`
- element_ids (2): `el_354ec43dcf3e492cb3df5bb592aba9ce, el_211354f41a6c41cbb41770a23b1124a9`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components Manually set the valve in the closed position. Loosen screw (1) on the lower cam (2) and adjust the cam to contact the lower micro switch (3). If the mechanical stop...`
- content:
```text
Manually set the valve in the closed position. Loosen screw (1) on the lower cam (2) and adjust the cam to contact the lower micro switch (3). If the mechanical stop prevents the cam rotating sufficiently turn the mechanical stop screw 2 turns counterclockwise from the closed position and lock it off. After adjustment of the cam (2) secure the cam with the screw (1).

Adjustment of 2 nd "CLOSED" Limit Switch

Loosen screw (4) on cam (5) and adjust the cam to contact the micro switch (6). After adjustment of cam (5) secure the cam with the screw (4).
```

### chunk_bd959e472ab540e293b357381fd2d896
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_23123ca927a2466faf1b7a302558273e`
- sequence_number: `390`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `96`
- token_count: `108`
- section_path: `7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7`
- element_ids (3): `el_67da812c7b5a4dda886c623382ba2326, el_da10e24c11504b2ab39379099b06904a, el_86e78995863f4c238e2a8562b952e656`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7 In the case of a power or control failure, or a fault in the actuator, the actuator has the capability for manual operation. Take care ab...`
- content:
```text
In the case of a power or control failure, or a fault in the actuator, the actuator has the capability for manual operation. Take care about the turning direction of the manual override.
The actuator is fitted with an Allen key on the underside, it fits in the socket on the end of the gear shaft. To close the actuator, turn the Allen key clockwise and to open the actuator turn the Allen key counterclockwise.
CAUTION: When finished with manual operation, remove the Allen key from the gear shaft to prevent the injury of persons or the damage of the actuator by normal operation of the electric actuator.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 1 General > 1.1 Explanation of Documentation > Safety Instructions This documentation is designed to assist with becoming familiar with the system and how to operate it for its i...`
- content:
```text
This documentation is designed to assist with becoming familiar with the system and how to operate it for its intended purposes. Important safety and hazard notices help you operate the system safely and properly. This system documentation is subject to revision. All previous releases are invalid when the new release has been delivered.
```

### chunk_d65dffbe441b418a9a33c044f705d3bb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_caca12036dbb430799fb17bf23ab93d1`
- sequence_number: `8`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `certification_info`
- page_start/page_end: `6`
- token_count: `92`
- section_path: `1 General > 1.2 Other Applicable Documents`
- element_ids (2): `el_264660f6315044ff972846825e80c1ba, el_69b24efe3f274d9c92e574622bc2998a`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 1 General > 1.2 Other Applicable Documents The components of other manufacturers which are used within the plant (e.g. e-motors), have a risk assessment from the respective manuf...`
- content:
```text
The components of other manufacturers which are used within the plant (e.g. e-motors), have a risk assessment from the respective manufacturer. The obligation to ensure that their design complies with the applicable national and European regulations is certificated by these manufacturers.
The operation, maintenance, and repair manuals of the other manufacturers for the relevant parts are inseparable components of this technical manual. The instructions contained in these documents regarding safety, set-up, installation, operation, maintenance, dismantling and disposal of the components are to be followed without limitation by the personnel operating the plant.
```

- content:
```text
Important safety-related notes in this technical manual are signified by symbols. These notes regarding work safety must be adhered to and complied with to secure the protection of persons and the safe operation of the plant.
Note: This symbol indicates and identifies tips and information to help ensure efficient and trouble free operation of the plant.
```

CAUTION: This symbol indicates and identifies information that, if not complied with, may lead to minor damage or injury.
WARNING: This symbol indicates and identifies information that, if not complied with, may cause death or serious injury.
DANGER: This symbol indicates and identifies information that, if not complied with, will cause death or serious injury.
WARNING: Electrical hazard! This symbol identifies information that, if not complied with, could lead to health hazards, injury or death.
WARNING: Biohazard, This symbol identifies information that, if not complied with, could lead to illness, or death.
DANGER: This symbol indicates and identifies information that, if not complied with, will cause death or serious injury.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 1 General > 1.6 Copyright Protection The technical manual must be treated confidential. It is distributed exclusively for those persons working on and with the plant. All content...`
- content:
```text
The technical manual must be treated confidential. It is distributed exclusively for those persons working on and with the plant. All content-related information, text, drawings, pictures, and other representations are protected as defined by copyright protection law and are subject to other industrial property rights. Any improper utilization is liable to prosecution.

It is not permitted to pass on the technical manual to third parties, to copy it in any way or form even extracts - or to recycle and/or communicate the contents without written permission of FMD (the manufacturer).

Violations incur the obligation to pay compensation, all other rights reserved. FMD reserves the right to exercise industrial property rights.
```

- content:
```text
Alterations or changes to the treatment plants are only permissible with express FMD written confirmation thereof.

1.8 Liability and Warranty

This technical manual has been compiled while taking into consideration the applicable regulations, current technology and the experiences and developments of many years. FMD assumes no liability for any damages or malfunctions that occur from non-compliance to the contents of the technical manual. The English version of this technical manual is the authorized version; any translations of the manual were created to the best of our ability, however we cannot assume liability for any translation errors.

All obligations of the manufacturer are defined in the purchase contract, which also includes the entire and sole valid warranty agreement. The contents of the technical manual will not amplify or restrict any contractual warranty.

The texts and illustrations do not necessarily correspond exactly to the scope of delivery. The drawings and diagrams are not scaled 1:1. FMD reserves the right to make technical changes to the product within the context of improving the functionality and further development.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety Section overview: 2 Safety This section includes instructions that must be observed during installation, operation, and maintenance of the equipment. The operation instr...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: 2 Safety

This section includes instructions that must be observed during installation, operation, and maintenance of the equipment.

The operation instructions must be read by any contractor, operator or person prior to installation, start-up, operation, or maintenance and must always be available.

Subsections: 2.1 General Operating Information; 2.2 Operator Safety; 2.3 Maintenance Safety; Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered; 2.4 Proper Use; 2.5 Qualified Personnel; 2.6 Protective Equipment; 2.7 Risk of Ignored Safety Instructions
```

This section includes instructions that must be observed during installation, operation, and maintenance of the equipment.
The operation instructions must be read by any contractor, operator or person prior to installation, start-up, operation, or maintenance and must always be available.
```

### chunk_a8b5cd28993f4ac7b6dc9e3a3bdf24d8
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_87826e56f19b435a9aada7b3aa368d2b`
- sequence_number: `22`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `9`
- token_count: `90`
- section_path: `2 Safety > 2.1 General Operating Information`
- element_ids (2): `el_199313f1c7ef4fc49e8c03fddb02a436, el_f10b20919c144fcf87edf7fed03abf5d`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.1 General Operating Information Personnel charged with installation, operation, maintenance, inspection, and assembly must be appropriately qualified. Before carryin...`
- content:
```text
Personnel charged with installation, operation, maintenance, inspection, and assembly must be appropriately qualified. Before carrying out any work which involves complete or partial opening of the plant, the person responsible must ensure that power to the plant is locked out (following the ships' isolation procedures) and that it is impossible for the plant to be started. Guards and covers may be removed only when the plant has been secured and locked out. All electrical components must be disconnected before disassembly.
Before restarting the plant, all guards must be in place.
```

### chunk_12eb2d2e01d04277bbf6ce5648aca56e
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c5e5f491b8284549b7e2097d94597680`
- sequence_number: `25`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `9`
- token_count: `115`
- section_path: `2 Safety > 2.2 Operator Safety > · WHEN IN DOUBT ASK > Shutdown`
- element_ids (5): `el_aa051558a5cb498b97fedba0d097b559, el_e39fb56ee513493c869d83f7c4af0d73, el_3bda1ab5f7b1404fbaf30b31d91d8cac, el_068ff19fd62f4a3d9b3a905b28849d22, el_6f1cd9636b524a27b7c37f2f7b58d089`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.2 Operator Safety > · WHEN IN DOUBT ASK > Shutdown When opening a component of the system such as a pump, pipe or housing WEAR PPE including, but not limited to, pro...`
- content:
```text
When opening a component of the system such as a pump, pipe or housing WEAR PPE including, but not limited to, protective clothing, gloves and a face shield.
DO NOT ASK a tradesman to repair the equipment if they may come in contact with the process stream by doing so.
MAKE IT SAFE FIRST flush out bio-residues from the pumps and pipelines and isolate the equipment. Many pipes contain wastewater even when shut down for some time.
Always electrically isolate the equipment and lock out the local isolator BEFORE you dismantle any electrical equipment. Remember that equipment can be turned on by accident.
Do not undertake any works unless the consequences are carefully thought through.
```

### chunk_edd9100276eb489680fc351570f09e8b
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_be4bbd355f984591b24e5a639205fdd3`

- token_count: `72`
- section_path: `2 Safety > 2.4 Proper Use > Approval information`
- element_ids (3): `el_61c285c0e3094bb3849100b233ce6cec, el_d0a3ff0d481645a6abdb5e9b56e97da4, el_6975ca42e0a6465f99dbac0dd0669654`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.4 Proper Use > Approval information For safety and functionality reasons the FWC12 may not be altered or modified. If any modification of the system is required, FMD...`
- content:
```text
For safety and functionality reasons the FWC12 may not be altered or modified. If any modification of the system is required, FMD is to be consulted for prior written approval.
The FWC12 is designed and manufactured with the latest technology and is safe to operate.
Any change in the plant which indicates a malfunction (increased noise level, higher temperatures than normal etc.) must be immediately investigated and where possible the fault rectified.
```

### chunk_1d40a596625e4261a8f1a0d81ef0d8f5
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_f8fed64102154e58b9e3c4d6aa31f0d9`
- sequence_number: `29`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/3`
- chunk type: `safety_warning`
- page_start/page_end: `10`
- token_count: `217`
- section_path: `2 Safety > 2.5 Qualified Personnel`
- element_ids (5): `el_a0c06269f6164b6dbcddd9014d693098, el_aa51947f0d7b4bb6beab13c2c30ef471, el_d8fd0ccca226454b87acefb5fb1095fb, el_2758350fcd5d4c54a439f5c37787d1dd, el_186d507c6f5f41d4872c47aa75c33480`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.5 Qualified Personnel All personnel that are instructed to work with or on the system must observe the rules and regulations for operational safety and accident prev...`
- content:
```text
All personnel that are instructed to work with or on the system must observe the rules and regulations for operational safety and accident prevention and must have read the system documentation, including all warning notices, prior to starting work. The plant must be operated, serviced, and maintained by suitably trained and instructed personnel. Instructions and procedures given by the vessel operator to prevent and avoid potential dangers are compulsory.
Qualified personnel are by reason of their training, experience, instruction and their knowledge of the relevant standards, regulations, accident prevention rules and working conditions, authorized by the person responsible for the safety of the plant to perform the appropriate activities required, and thereby able to recognize and prevent potentially dangerous situations. Knowledge of first aid and local rescue organization arrangements must also be included.
Persons under the influence of drugs, alcohol or reaction-impairing medication must not be allowed to operate or work on the plant.
The personnel responsible for the installation, operation and maintenance of the equipment must be appropriately qualified. The operator must ensure that they have completely understood the instructions contained within this manual.
All warranties and guarantees with respect to the machine function and durability shall be void should the operator fail to adhere to these safety instructions or any other instructions within this manual.
```

```

### chunk_af140a1bb057469295b48528b0330aa6
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (5): `el_69c7e3b13b5a413282654e1c35862d1a, el_50b8333957c94f24b0d1c80c66d66c63, el_dceb360cc28340e0a0257945143f3efc, el_af74640aebf045539744b8a7b3cb2d05, el_b120dd7cb1c44551bf7271bb1f976fd4`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.7 Risk of Ignored Safety Instructions Rotating parts and the energy released with a sudden stop can cause serious damage to personnel and equipment, which can also r...`
- content:
```text
Rotating parts and the energy released with a sudden stop can cause serious damage to personnel and equipment, which can also result in extended downtime and high repair costs with damaged parts.
The associated risks due to failure to adhere to these safety instructions include but are not limited to:
 Endangering people due to electrical, mechanical, and/or chemical/biochemical hazards.
 Endangering the environment due to leakage of hazardous material (where chemicals are involved).
Failure of important equipment and process functions leading to inferior performance.
```

### chunk_5ba11f6efd4c491d8de8ab8e1b10e068

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 2 Safety > 2.8 Spare Parts Only original spare parts and equipment authorised by FMD are suitable and safe for use. Note: Incorrect or faulty spare parts can lead to damage, malf...`
- content:
```text
Only original spare parts and equipment authorised by FMD are suitable and safe for use.
Note: Incorrect or faulty spare parts can lead to damage, malfunction or complete breakdown of the equipment!
Installation of any parts or any modifications not authorised by FMD will nullify liability for any resulting damage or injury and change in performance, all guarantee, service, compensation, and liability claim against the manufacturer, its agents or representatives are forfeit.
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 3 System Introduction > 3.3 What it Does The FWC system is designed to collect food waste from attached macerator stations using vacuum generated by the integrated pump and trans...`
- content:
```text
The FWC system is designed to collect food waste from attached macerator stations using vacuum generated by the integrated pump and transfer the macerated slurry to the food waste dewatering press for separation of the solids from the liquid or to the holding tank for later processing or discharge overboard.
```

### chunk_20f149a1cad946b7919ab083c979e5e9
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_2dc494e053fd4b67a9fba8a66a2d17dc`
- sequence_number: `40`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `13`
- token_count: `215`
- section_path: `3 System Introduction > 3.4 How it Works`
- element_ids (4): `el_cbe26dbb9c424d5bb5e6f0d68136632d, el_e4da4dd12c6240a6816ceeb51bee65bf, el_bcfdf0d23e314fd1921c47c427c31974, el_f244ccab1e294a2aa8073074245ebd89`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 3 System Introduction > 3.4 How it Works When a macerator station lid is closed and the start button is pressed, the control system of the FWC12 opens the appropriate valves base...`
- content:
```text
When a macerator station lid is closed and the start button is pressed, the control system of the FWC12 opens the appropriate valves based on the selected mode and then starts the vacuum pump. When vacuum builds up in the piping system to the required level the macerator will start and the transport water solenoid will open, after a few seconds the macerator discharge valve will open and the macerated food waste will be drawn through the pipe work by vacuum until it reaches the pump.
Normal operation will see the food waste sent to the dewatering press immediately with the food waste tank remaining empty during normal operation, this prevents the anaerobic breakdown of any organic material sitting in the tank and the production of associated smells.
The food waste press separates the solids and the liquids at 150 microns, the liquid is transferred via pump to the ships grease trap. The solids fall by gravity into an endless bag that seals in all smells whilst the unit is in operation. The bag is monitored by a sensor that measures how full the bag is stopping the system and sending a common alarm when the bag requires changing.
Food waste can be discharged in accordance with the most recent revision Marpol Annex V regulations.
```

- content:
```text
Normal operation will see the food waste sent to the dewatering press immediately with the food waste tank remaining empty during normal operation, this prevents the anaerobic breakdown of any organic material sitting in the tank and the production of associated smells.
The food waste press separates the solids and the liquids at 150 microns, the liquid is transferred via pump to the ships grease trap. The solids fall by gravity into an endless bag that seals in all smells whilst the unit is in operation. The bag is monitored by a sensor that measures how full the bag is stopping the system and sending a common alarm when the bag requires changing.
Food waste can be discharged in accordance with the most recent revision Marpol Annex V regulations.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 3 System Introduction > 3.5 Don'ts Section overview: 3.5 Don'ts Do not attempt to process the following waste groups in the macerators or FWC12 system:  Cooking oils & Fats  Do...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: 3.5 Don'ts

Do not attempt to process the following waste groups in the macerators or FWC12 system:

 Cooking oils & Fats

 Dough

 Cutlery, glass, crockery

 Plastic or solid waste

 Paints, aerosols, acids or alkali

 Chemicals

 Substances that can potentially lead to explosion or infection

Note: Equipment can be damaged if inappropriate items or materials are processed.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

- content:
```text
All safety regulations and instructions are to be observed.
The regulations and instructions in the Technical Manual must be observed.
Prior to commissioning FMD must be consulted.
It is recommended that commissioning be completed by a service technician from FMD.
The power supply may not vary from the contract specifications of the system.
The installation may not vary from the FMD approved installation drawings without prior written agreement.
```

- content:
```text
Lifting connections should only be made and executed as per the Lifting and Transport drawing supplied (where relevant). Transport and lifting should only be carried out with appropriate lifting equipment by trained persons. Attention is to be paid to all components extending past the main structure, so no damage occurs during lifting and transport operations.
```

Section overview: 4.4 Mounting

The FWC System is to be mounted in the upright position on the appropriate baseplates in accordance with the Mounting and Pipe Connection drawing supplied.

Note: FMD is to be consulted prior to any welding directly on the supplied plant.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

```

### chunk_09281a0783404dba83974a298d3e2626
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_deb5b48e1f7f40f5986f20501a57eaf6`
- sequence_number: `57`

- chunk type: `installation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `16`
- token_count: `95`
- section_path: `5 Commissioning > 5.2 Objective`
- element_ids (8): `el_ddaf25768917413488250371ab46d207, el_34dd04ceb44b412ea8f52d53be54b972, el_faf85bb23ead4b189f6df08ee0f61f42, el_72670192e2aa4fd8bca4eeac6463db53, el_b3a4950dc7a24267a066d8c01bf685bd, el_77c1b8ba7e1649d595a618f3276b7dd5, el_d9e0ff7ce2e5454eac421a89fa5161c0, el_32e47137869a48a1be9af98a4df4ca15`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 5 Commissioning > 5.2 Objective  Installation check of all main and auxiliary components.  Check all interconnecting pipework is complete and correct with no leaks.  Testing o...`
- content:
```text
 Installation check of all main and auxiliary components.
 Check all interconnecting pipework is complete and correct with no leaks.
 Testing of all individual components and instrumentation for correct operation
 Testing of the control system to ensure correct function as per design and contract requirements.
 Establish plants operation in automatic and manual modes as required by contract documents and specifications.
 Confirm that all relevant acceptance test criteria have been met.
 Confirm that all alarms work as intended.
 Running of the system in auto for process establishment and evaluation.
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 5 Commissioning > 5.3 Communication Section overview: 5.3 Communication Commissioning meetings with relevant stakeholders should be held as required. Topics should include:  Com...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: 5.3 Communication

Commissioning meetings with relevant stakeholders should be held as required. Topics should include:

 Commissioning program / schedules / procedures and checklists

 Commissioning resource requirements and allocation

 Commissioning progress to date

 Interfaces and interruptions with operating plant or external systems where they occur

 Interference issues between Construction, Commissioning and Handover

 Permit to Work requirements, operational requirements

 Planned major events, e.g. shutdowns, performance test periods

 Notice to relevant authorities

 Safety requirements

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 5 Commissioning > 5.6 Pre-Commissioning Section overview: 5.6 Pre-Commissioning All equipment is on site installed as per relevant instructions, GA, schematics & drawings with el...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: 5.6 Pre-Commissioning

All equipment is on site installed as per relevant instructions, GA, schematics & drawings with electrical connections and tests completed.

Pre-commissioning starts after completion of the installation of the Plant and includes all activities that are needed to verify the correct installation of each individual component.

At the start of this phase the power should be connected to the main isolator and locked out by the commissioning engineer in charge.

 Check all supplies are connected correctly and isolated

 Undertake pre-commissioning checklists

 Close out any punch list items

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

All equipment is on site installed as per relevant instructions, GA, schematics & drawings with electrical connections and tests completed.
Pre-commissioning starts after completion of the installation of the Plant and includes all activities that are needed to verify the correct installation of each individual component.
At the start of this phase the power should be connected to the main isolator and locked out by the commissioning engineer in charge.
 Check all supplies are connected correctly and isolated
 Undertake pre-commissioning checklists
 Close out any punch list items
```

Normal use mode with all control and safety systems active. From the home page select the Auto button on the screen, the Automatic Operation Page will appear on screen. Pressing the Home button will return to the home page where the red Stop All button is available to stop the plant running.

- content:
```text
Section overview: Manual Operation Page 6.1.4

All main components fitted to the system can be operated manually from the HMI. All components usable in manual appear on the screen with a yellow box highlighting them, this forms the Start / Stop button for the specific component.

CAUTION: When operating in manual it may be possible to start pumps with valves closed, particular care must be taken not to damage the plant with this type of operation.

User:

Overboard/Ashore

mbar

FoodwasteTank

2.5m3

Water

50

25

mEStop

m3/h

100

mbar

HotWater

mbar

ds33

mbar

Grease Trap

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

CAUTION: When operating in manual it may be possible to start pumps with valves closed, particular care must be taken not to damage the plant with this type of operation.

- content:
```text
The day-to-day operator involvement required during normal operation is minimal and consists of basic daily observations, minor maintenance that has come due and response to any alarms.
Any changes or adjustments should be made by appropriately qualified persons and should ideally be recorded.
```

### chunk_7f316eb4a1fd41288630caecfd5ab599
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_76d349ddf89348d09dd24bbe09c5318b`

- token_count: `47`
- section_path: `6 Operation & General Maintenance > Auto to Tank 6.2.1`
- element_ids (1): `el_447ac0d969f5431e88e6fa98bbc79e70`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 6 Operation & General Maintenance > Auto to Tank 6.2.1 Select Auto to Tank on the home page then press the Automatic Run button, this will set the FWC12 plant to automatic operat...`
- content:
```text
Select Auto to Tank on the home page then press the Automatic Run button, this will set the FWC12 plant to automatic operation whereby pressing of the macerator Start button will set the run sequence in operation and discharge the macerated food waste into the holding tank.
```

- content:
```text
Select Auto to De-watering Press on the home page then press the Automatic Run button, this will set the FWC12 plant to automatic operation whereby pressing of the macerator Start button will set the run sequence in operation and discharge the macerated food waste into the de-watering press.
```

### chunk_e3fd4c2d9b994a60a5a575db88a9ee0c
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_bfc6fc9b82f648539662fa11677b9968`
- sequence_number: `86`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `24`
- token_count: `93`
- section_path: `6 Operation & General Maintenance > 6.3 Operation Macerator`
- element_ids (1): `el_729ca31c380546b5aca8a1081b7b63ba`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 6 Operation & General Maintenance > 6.3 Operation Macerator To start the macerator, it must be in the ready status, the E-Stop should not be illuminated, and the Start / Run butt...`
- content:
```text
To start the macerator, it must be in the ready status, the E-Stop should not be illuminated, and the Start / Run button should be illuminated solid green. Once the macerator is filled with food and the lid is closed the press Start / Run button it will change from solid green to flashing green to show the macerator is active. It will follow the automated sequence programmed through to completion and the Start / Run button will return to solid green showing the macerator is complete and ready for the next batch.
```

- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Owner / User Responsibility There are several forewarnings indicated throughout this manual that may stress a possible unsafe condition or importa...`
- content:
```text
There are several forewarnings indicated throughout this manual that may stress a possible unsafe condition or important information regarding the use of this equipment.
The system operator must ensure that all relevant accident prevention regulations of their industry are observed and complied with. These safety regulations are only supplementary to the existing safety regulations of the entire system.
This manual has been prepared to provide you with a simple and understandable guide for equipment operation and maintenance. To keep your unit in top running condition, follow the specific maintenance procedures given in this manual.
```

```

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > General Warnings:`
- element_ids (4): `el_1491da1cb64441be863b6337811506a6, el_179e49f7fbd94fa18966bfaa9629eb58, el_64d856fda18c487fb41eb6e03d43438d, el_ee08894afd834aa6a18d0e141580b352`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > General Warnings: WARNING: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and symbols and understand all instructions before installing, operating, o...`
- content:
```text
WARNING: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and symbols and understand all instructions before installing, operating, or servicing this machine . Failure to do so could result in severe personal injury or property damage .
CAUTION: When installing or maintaining the pre-screen, shut off and lock out power before removing any covers. Never make adjustments while in operation, except for those prescribed in this manual.
DANGER: Keep doors and guards in place at all times to avoid exposure to rotating parts. While the power is on, severe injury can occur if hands or feet reach inside the unit.
WARNING: A qualified electrician must perform all wiring. Failure to comply may result in severe personal injury or property damage.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Electrical System Precautions > Safety Instructions The electrical equipment must be secured against unauthorised access and may only be tested an...`
- content:
```text
The electrical equipment must be secured against unauthorised access and may only be tested and maintained by trained specialists.
WARNING: Electrical Hazard! ALWAYS check for no voltage before starting work!
When working on the switchgear and motors, the system must de-energized, tagged out and secured against re-connection in accordance with the ship's working practices and permit to work policy.
```

- element_ids (3): `el_5feeb5fdd9694d0a9927a8867cf3e22f, el_dcb0d501c228490dbfa07d61278b3056, el_e8dfbfb940984517811abef90b81bc92`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Biohazard > Safety Instructions The food waste press can contain a variety of bacteria and viruses. In general, these are nonpathogenic strains, h...`
- content:
```text
The food waste press can contain a variety of bacteria and viruses. In general, these are nonpathogenic strains, however, the inhalation of aerosols and contact with the skin and mucous membranes should be avoided.
WARNING: Biohazard! When working on the system the operator must wear suitable clothes, eye protection and rubber gloves suitable for contact with wastewater. Failure to do so could lead to illness or death.
The nature, control and extent of the necessary vaccinations and pre/post treatment examinations should be determined by the ship or company medical professionals.
```

Context: WARNING: Biohazard! When working on the system the operator must wear suitable clothes, eye protection and rubber gloves suitable for contact with wastewater. Failure to do so could lead to illness or death.

The nature, control and extent of the necessary vaccinations and pre/post treatment examinations should be determined by the ship or company medical professionals.
```

Food waste is fed into the machine and ground into small particles for transport by vacuum to the holding tank or dewatering press.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > How it Works 7.1.4 Food waste disposers use flails mounted on a spinning plate taking advantage of centrifugal power to force food waste particles...`
- content:
```text
Food waste disposers use flails mounted on a spinning plate taking advantage of centrifugal power to force food waste particles against a grinding ring breaking the food down into small particles.
```

- element_ids (3): `el_76ac44a1cfd74dba8984646ae7fe25f4, el_14feb6b5b78a427bae8c3069f5649515, el_657699f1cbd5453f96b41f0fdbeb92ab`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Transport of the Macerator 7.1.5 If the machine is to be stored before installation it should be stored in a dry and clean room where the relative...`
- content:
```text
If the machine is to be stored before installation it should be stored in a dry and clean room where the relative humidity may not exceed 60%.
Recommended storage temperature of -20 °C to + 60 °C.
The machine should not be lifted or moved by hand. During installation, we recommend using a pallet jack or forklift.
```

### chunk_804133ca4b614b63a69cd315457ba0d2
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_b344dfbaca66463780e4b9516bbd1513`
- sequence_number: `105`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `installation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `28`
- token_count: `70`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Mounting 7.1.6`
- element_ids (1): `el_31d2cd706a4f4bf585391e8b3f39ab50`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Mounting 7.1.6 In order to weld a strong joint and to be able to grind to an even and fine surface between the discharge cone and the working benc...`
- content:
```text
In order to weld a strong joint and to be able to grind to an even and fine surface between the discharge cone and the working bench/tabletop, the cone is welded with its upper flange against the underside of the working top. The opening in the worktop must be at least 398 ± 2 mm in diameter. The disposer is then screwed into the lower attachment ring of the cone.
```

### chunk_a1d43b4748ce4183bd84e23f543161ea
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c518b0040a4a4c439d36f3cff09d23d6`
- sequence_number: `106`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/3`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `28`
- token_count: `264`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Mounting of models 520, 530, 550 and 575, delivered with legs as standard`
- element_ids (10): `el_b6d436b9dae944c2b42169f8c4aa4f34, el_c8938b89e72647ecbee7d5f8cf359166, el_9a0ad3f7f8bb4811a5c9bdfeec497a25, el_f0379be35c384fa8b5fec3a7877d6ff5, el_19e8415a049a4522b26ba16239ab1ae0, el_bf6600b06d434f01819a886be0261ea7, el_656c066d7f3b48d3acf63d496ac6dfb8, el_373d0e00f0d5492aa9ae028de135fb3c, ... (+2 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Mounting of models 520, 530, 550 and 575, delivered with legs as standard The weights of models 520, 530, 550 and 575 require legs for support. At...`
- content:
```text
The weights of models 520, 530, 550 and 575 require legs for support. At installation site the separately delivered 3 legs must be fixed to the 3 clamps in the disposer bottom plate.
When mounting the legs:
 Measure the height (X mm) between the flange of the mounting assembly and the floor/floor plate.
 Measure the height (Y mm) of the disposer without legs including the 3 mm rubber seal.
 Lay down the disposer carefully on its side. Slide the three legs into respective clamp. Each leg should protrude X – Y - 4 mm.
 Tighten the clamps. Make sure that the legs are fixated, and that their position have not changed during the tightening of the screw.
 Place the disposer so that it stands on its legs. Move the disposer carefully into position under the flange of the mounting assembly.
 Using the six screws with nuts fixate the disposer onto the flange with the 3 mm rubber seal in between. Tighten the screws alternately and evenly until the rubber seal is compressed approximately 1 mm.
 Use a jack or a lever to push up the disposer towards the flange of the mounting assembly.
While this upward pressure is maintained, untighten each leg and pull it downwards. Make sure that all legs are firmly pressed against the floor. Tighten the clamps using a torque of 17 Nm. As this is a critical matter, a torque wrench should be used. If no torque wrench is available use a leaver of approximately 30 cm and a force of 6 kg.
```

- content:
```text
Context: The weights of models 520, 530, 550 and 575 require legs for support. At installation site the separately delivered 3 legs must be fixed to the 3 clamps in the disposer bottom plate.

When mounting the legs:

 Measure the height (X mm) between the flange of the mounting assembly and the floor/floor plate.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Electrical Installation 7.1.7 The electrical connection must be done by an authorized electrician and in accordance to valid local regulations. Th...`
- content:
```text
The electrical connection must be done by an authorized electrician and in accordance to valid local regulations.
The wiring made at the factory, and the electrical connections to be done during installation, are shown in the electrical wiring diagram attached to this manual. The machine's specific electrical data are given in this manual, as well as on its serial number plate located on the outer cover of the macerator.
```

Check that the supply voltage to be connected corresponds to the specified voltage on the machine's serial number plate.

Check that the supply voltage for the delivered machine is fused and protected as specified in this manual.
```

- content:
```text
Use connection cable having 1.5 mm² wires for machines having a rated current up to 14A. For machines having a rated current above 14A, use 2.5 mm² wires. The rated voltage and current is specified on the machine's serial number plate.
```

The disposer operates correctly irrespective of the motor's rotational direction. It is therefore irrelevant in which sequence the electrical phases are connected to the disposer motor.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up Section overview: Check before Start Up Disconnect the power at the main isolator and lock...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Check before Start Up

Disconnect the power at the main isolator and lock out according to vessel lockout procedures. Check that the rotary shredder in the inlet opening of the disposer turns freely 360° in both directions by hand, and make sure that no foreign object has been dropped into the grinding unit during installation.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally Responsible Solutions Engineered
```

Check that the rotary shredder in the inlet opening of the disposer turns freely 360° in both directions by hand, and make sure that no foreign object has been dropped into the grinding unit during installation.

- element_ids (8): `el_96e0df76f2e544e7bef586109d133367, el_2c81e53580374a2ebb6d44698cd69553, el_dec41f04851b443f8ca421f691ab5ed0, el_293f9965bdfd4931a5c6aa321a6c2ecc, el_a4bdec34f5e3492c90844cf327e5d6df, el_d5762fc4238448ff9824a4d5d116647a, el_c471d7105626490a8576568d0159ae01, el_c30be4bba05343238c7656a0fc2462ad`
- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > Check before Start Up > Checks during Start Up Section overview: Checks during Start Up  Start the disposer and...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Checks during Start Up

 Start the disposer and determine that the grinder rotate.

 Check for automatic flushing water. The water should come directly when starting the machine.

 Check assembly, flushing water connections and drain connections for possible leaks.

 Make sure that the safety interlock switch functions and stops the machine if the lid is lifted.

AL

DANGER: Keep doors and guards in place at all times to avoid exposure to rotating parts. While the power is on, severe injury can occur if hands or feet reach inside the unit.

Subsections: Operation 7.1.9
```
