```

### chunk_b18195dc51a34911b1fb64b762cad112
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_12d849b22f5b407e8e64d8f7ecdc8f9e`
- sequence_number: `226`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/2`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `55`
- token_count: `162`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8`
- element_ids (9): `el_a042e5ab54bd4cf8bd086596a834e228, el_4bbb3781fa42450681c398cbba3a679d, el_ed09f6c095724332b63e20893f15ac89, el_96915d95880f4b148d4634a5940a184d, el_08b02f89cac84b3a9580003a66763a4f, el_bdb1fb314fc84984874220d41dc33a63, el_3e0b81600cf241faa89d5d3b59983a20, el_a2c85099792344299ac82a1a41ae2794, ... (+1 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8  Never set the air pressure higher than 2.0 bar.  Once the plug is established the optimum air pressure is genera...`
- content:
```text
 Never set the air pressure higher than 2.0 bar.

 Once the plug is established the optimum air pressure is generally 0.6 – 0.8 bar for GW/BW and 1.0-1.5 bar for food waste.

 The higher the air pressure the higher the chance a blockage may form in the press, which in turn leads to increased wear, break down and possible irreparable damage.

 Observe the press for a while. Reduce or increase the pressing force until the solid discharge runs evenly and continuously.

 If the press begins to vibrate, this is often a sign of excessive pressing force and an impending blockage.

7.2.7.4 Shutdown

 Turn off and isolate the inlet supply pumps to prevent them starting.

 Wait until the feed pipes have emptied.

 Reduce the pressing force and move the compressed air cylinder to the rear.

 Let the separator run for a short while longer until the solids in the machine have been completely discharged.
```

### chunk_8d05a2f717d448858150c367459854de
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_94e7600881d24f8f87a1da530de49c1c`
- sequence_number: `227`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `55`
- token_count: `103`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > 7.2.7.4 Shutdown`
- element_ids (4): `el_e8b798b42819419baba2d248df8fed26, el_292edc7114f24fc4b844f414ceefae3f, el_844cc5cb6fb4456ea9fb9b0ee4b38d33, el_25d52dcd96c542f990da6d40a984e7e8`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Commissioning & Shutdown 7.1.8 > 7.2.7.4 Shutdown  The system must be thoroughly cleaned.  Put up signage to notify that the press has been take...`
- content:
```text
 The system must be thoroughly cleaned.
 Put up signage to notify that the press has been taken out of operation.
Take Note: If the press is to be idle or shut down for more than 72 hours, there is a risk that the solids plug (formed against the cone) will begin dry out and solidify – see below:
If the plug has dried out and the press is restarted, after a short time of operation the pressure behind the plug will increase until it releases suddenly - and there is the potential for wastewater to carry over into the solids bag.
```

### chunk_c41e3e0ff6c04d169ad24d25d7f4c847
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_1ddc71f6cf584f11aa9ebf16855600f1`
- sequence_number: `229`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/3`
- chunk type: `maintenance_interval`
- page_start/page_end: `56`
- token_count: `179`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Overview & Maintenance Intervals`
- element_ids (4): `el_2e88bfcb680d47fb96e0a44ed796fd15, el_254e6b9ca8f54076b8d3d7f072f0ae2a, el_e3d23b524fd443b3b85a437c5506ff51, el_89ecf8b3750f48c2b6a19da85906b0c6`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Overview & Maintenance Intervals To maintain operational readiness, possible damage should be detected at an early stage. To preserve warran...`
- content:
```text
To maintain operational readiness, possible damage should be detected at an early stage. To preserve warranty and guarantee entitlements the operator is obliged to carry out regular inspection and maintenance work on the system in accordance with the respective operating and maintenance instructions as detailed in 7.1.10 Failure to observe these regulations will lead to the invalidation of the warranty. Operating persons must be informed of, and understand, all safety requirements before beginning start-up and maintenance work. For all maintenance, inspection and repair works the press is to be shut down and isolated with the main power isolator locked out in accordance with the onboard regulations and permit system.
CAUTION: Pay attention to all safety instructions during all maintenance and servicing work and the safety policies of the vessel.
WARNING: Before working on the press, isolate the power supply and lock out or remove fuses. There is a risk of crushed hands and limbs from the rotating shaft/screw in the drive area.
All system parts and operating media connected upstream of the press must be protected against unintended start.
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Modifications to the Press 7.2.9

No modifications, attachments or rebuilding of the press may occur without the prior written authorisation of FMD.

Machine parts that are not in a safe usable condition are to be replaced immediately.

Take Note: Only original spare and wear parts may be used. Other parts are not warranted.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

Machine parts that are not in a safe usable condition are to be replaced immediately.
```

- element_ids (15): `el_153326d35cf2488c91a49a406db40839, el_c867173657ad4115939c926b35894c33, el_465a4643cf3c49189da23e33782ff21e, el_fab46858d0d24ae7a49a11ab1562e25b, el_6803c1af014b4a9f98f672559aeb8d74, el_3597fac5b8fc4781a5433c25ef3fe56a, el_f694d3547d354c948cc8c2ad1344b2bc, el_8ea8b69829d4445daeea801c996d5f10, ... (+7 more)`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 Section overview: Preventi...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11

The instructions for all visual inspections, maintenance and repair work must be observed.

WARNING: Before working on the TSP, isolate the power supply and lock out or remove fuses. There is a risk of crushed hands and limbs from the rotating shaft/screw in the drive area.

The essential maintenance for the TSP is summarised in the Preventive Maintenance Tasks table where the following abbreviations/codes are used:

D Daily maintenance work

W Weekly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 100 operating hours

M Monthly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 450 operating hours

Q Quarterly maintenance work

S Half-yearly maintenance work

A Annual maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 5000 operating hours

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- element_ids (10): `el_c867173657ad4115939c926b35894c33, el_fab46858d0d24ae7a49a11ab1562e25b, el_6803c1af014b4a9f98f672559aeb8d74, el_3597fac5b8fc4781a5433c25ef3fe56a, el_f694d3547d354c948cc8c2ad1344b2bc, el_8ea8b69829d4445daeea801c996d5f10, el_507b292a2c4f4f73b3fdfd0188cc9056, el_9072c031783542609d8842164c1cb90b, ... (+2 more)`
- table_ids (1): `table_d6a04d24aff4455d914a00a72b6bb78a`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Safety Instructions The...`
- content:
```text
The instructions for all visual inspections, maintenance and repair work must be observed.
WARNING: Before working on the TSP, isolate the power supply and lock out or remove fuses. There is a risk of crushed hands and limbs from the rotating shaft/screw in the drive area.
The essential maintenance for the TSP is summarised in the Preventive Maintenance Tasks table where the following abbreviations/codes are used:
D Daily maintenance work
W Weekly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 100 operating hours
M Monthly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 450 operating hours
Q Quarterly maintenance work
S Half-yearly maintenance work
A Annual maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 5000 operating hours
| D                                     | Q  Q                                  | M  S  A                                                                                                          | Task Reference                        |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press                                                                            | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press |
| X                                     |                                       | General visual inspection daily or after period of particularly high  load                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check basket for fat, fibre growth or blockages & clogging                                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check the mechanics for possible malfunctions of the drive, shaft  bearing, cone etc                             |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check all nuts, screws, bolts and connecting elements for tightness                                              |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | The complete mechanics, connections and bearings must be  carefully checked and rectified as required.           |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw                                                                                 | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      |
| X                                     |                                       | Check that the screw runs evenly in the basket                                                                   |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                                                                                                      | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           |
| X                                     |                                       | Check gearbox for leaks                                                                                          | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Change the lubricant after every 6 months or after 1000 hrs of  operation and thoroughly flush the gear housing. | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check intake air filter for contamination, clean or replace as  required.                                        | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Clean dirt from the housing                                                                                      | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Clean fan cover, fan wheel, ventilation grille and cooling fins                                                  | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
```

The essential maintenance for the TSP is summarised in the Preventive Maintenance Tasks table where the following abbreviations/codes are used:
D Daily maintenance work
W Weekly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 100 operating hours
M Monthly maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 450 operating hours
Q Quarterly maintenance work
S Half-yearly maintenance work
A Annual maintenance work: performed as required, latest after 5000 operating hours

|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press                                                                            | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press | General Maintenance Work on the Press |
| X                                     |                                       | General visual inspection daily or after period of particularly high  load                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check basket for fat, fibre growth or blockages & clogging                                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check the mechanics for possible malfunctions of the drive, shaft  bearing, cone etc                             |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check all nuts, screws, bolts and connecting elements for tightness                                              |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | The complete mechanics, connections and bearings must be  carefully checked and rectified as required.           |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw                                                                                 | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      |
| X                                     |                                       | Check that the screw runs evenly in the basket                                                                   |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                                                                                                      | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           |
| X                                     |                                       | Check gearbox for leaks                                                                                          | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Change the lubricant after every 6 months or after 1000 hrs of  operation and thoroughly flush the gear housing. | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check intake air filter for contamination, clean or replace as  required.                                        | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |

| X                                     |                                       | Clean fan cover, fan wheel, ventilation grille and cooling fins                                                  | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |

| X                                     |                                       | General visual inspection daily or after period of particularly high  load                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check basket for fat, fibre growth or blockages & clogging                                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check the mechanics for possible malfunctions of the drive, shaft  bearing, cone etc                             |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check all nuts, screws, bolts and connecting elements for tightness                                              |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | The complete mechanics, connections and bearings must be  carefully checked and rectified as required.           |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw                                                                                 | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      | Maintenance work on sieve, screw      |
| X                                     |                                       | Check that the screw runs evenly in the basket                                                                   |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                                                                                                      | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           | Screw drive                           |
| X                                     |                                       | Check gearbox for leaks                                                                                          | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Change the lubricant after every 6 months or after 1000 hrs of  operation and thoroughly flush the gear housing. | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Check intake air filter for contamination, clean or replace as  required.                                        | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
|                                       | X                                     | Clean dirt from the housing                                                                                      | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
| X                                     |                                       | Clean fan cover, fan wheel, ventilation grille and cooling fins                                                  | See gearbox  Annex                    |                                       |                                       |                                       |                                       |
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate

Loosen and remove the screws on the discharge chute retaining plate, remove the retaining plate including the air cylinder and press cone.

Subsections: Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure
```

- token_count: `58`

- element_ids (3): `el_d379b430ab454ad6bb14ff5104edfe8b, el_d9f3e6763fde4722a4c84798e4d84878, el_fdd71c44bb8244698fa0f41d6e8933b1`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning o...`
- content:
```text
Engineered

Removal of the Screen Basket

Now the screen basket can be pulled out of the separator using care.

Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming!

Once the screen basket has been pulled out of the housing by roughly half of its length, the initial resistance experienced reduces considerably.
```

Section overview: Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming!

Once the screen basket has been pulled out of the housing by roughly half of its length, the initial resistance experienced reduces considerably.

Picture: Removal of the Screen Basket

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Check the Screen Basket
```

- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_d11347d6335d47bdbce1f0eeb8f7bf5b`

- chunk type: `overview`
- page_start/page_end: `62`
- token_count: `90`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning of the Screen Basket 7.2.12 > Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate > Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure > Discharge Chute Removed > Removal of the Press Zone > Removal of the Screen Basket > Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming! > Check the Screen Basket`
- element_ids (8): `el_0e349d6706ae49eabcd5d4b47131ae75, el_7dc7f16ed4c642588466b7482ae375e4, el_9b81616ed51a4bd9ae95a50d36104576, el_0b0c0e406b534b0b9e3b5a2ca3c0cb8e, el_a7f472fd9961434ab2270c07deafcdd7, el_3c6543d4f011449bbdb144a205cd9d33, el_f2cd6b1d794a4e04a664aaa37fcdcb87, el_dea0b38831054129aaff466de6be8167`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning o...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Check the Screen Basket

When the screen basket has been removed and carefully cleaned, prior to re-installation it should be checked to see if damage exists on the surface and that seamless movement of the screw is ensured. The screen basket should not have any deformations or oval distortions. If there is any uncertainty as to what distortion tolerance is permissible, contact FMD.

Any new screen should also be subjected to an inspection for deformations and distortion prior to being fitted.

Picture: Screen Basket

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

### chunk_d82f89302ad74c3c952dc0eaaff33a85
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_ac112fbbe8a74a29997c829d39028756`
- sequence_number: `269`

- chunk type: `overview`

- token_count: `54`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning of the Screen Basket 7.2.12 > Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate > Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure > Discharge Chute Removed > Removal of the Press Zone > Removal of the Screen Basket > Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming! > Check the Screen Basket > Screen Basket Installation`
- element_ids (2): `el_86fa906cd23e433ba877a36fde1beac6, el_4cea2ceae04f43599c8fad12deda7c01`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning o...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Screen Basket Installation

The installation of a cleaned or new screen basket occurs in the reverse order to disassembly. When inserting the screen basket, be sure to push it in as straight as possible in order to prevent it jamming and potentially damaging the screw or basket.

Subsections: Fitting the Press Zone
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- content:
```text
Section overview: Fitting the Press Zone

If the screen basket has been inserted correctly, then the press zone can be re-attached to the press. Ensure it easily fastens without any gap to the main housing.

O

After attaching the press zone, check all screws and tighten to the correct torque of 35 Nm.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- sequence_number: `275`

- page_start/page_end: `64`
- token_count: `52`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning of the Screen Basket 7.2.12 > Disassembly of Cylinder Retaining Plate > Removal of the Discharge Chute Retaining Plate and Enclosure > Discharge Chute Removed > Removal of the Press Zone > Removal of the Screen Basket > Ensure that this is carried out as straight as possible to prevent the screen basket jamming! > Check the Screen Basket > Screen Basket Installation > Fitting the Press Zone > Maintenance of the Screw 7.2.13`
- element_ids (3): `el_65563129112c4d6eb6342e083be5f441, el_3249fd14aa5642ae95b8e0b6680c59dd, el_ffd2a61c5a11499d8e98a9b005e5be63`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Modifications to the Press 7.2.9 > Spare Parts > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Preventive Maintenance 7.2.11 > Maintenance & Cleaning o...`
- content:
```text
If it is necessary to change the screw or carry out an inspection, then first remove the screen basket as described above. After dismantling the screen basket, the outer holding screws of the screw mounting plate can be released.
Rotate the screw fixing plate in an anticlockwise direction.
Removing the Fixing Plate
```

Context: If it is necessary to change the screw or carry out an inspection, then first remove the screen basket as described above. After dismantling the screen basket, the outer holding screws of the screw mounting plate can be released.
```

- content:
```text
The locating holes for the screw holder can be used for inserting a tool for pulling the screw from the shaft (available on request from FMD).

Once the screw has been released and the initial friction overcome, the screw can be pulled out carefully. Two people may be required to lift it out.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Pulling out the Screw Figure: CAUTION: As the screw is pulled through the rear hole, DO NOT allow the screw to drop freely into the housing!...`
- content:
```text
Figure: CAUTION: As the screw is pulled through the rear hole, DO NOT allow the screw to drop freely into the housing! The carbide edges of the screw are brittle and easily damaged by impact!
```

```

```

- content:
```text
At the end of the mounting zone, the resistance when introducing the screw will be noticeably greater. At this point, the fully home position should be reached carefully using a soft-face hammer.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > Attaching the Fixing Plate Finally, the fixing plate must be attached to the shaft. In this process, the correct play-free installation must...`
- content:
```text
Finally, the fixing plate must be attached to the shaft. In this process, the correct play-free installation must be ensured and the correct tightening torque applied.
After this step, the screen basket can be reinserted as described previously.
```

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw`
- element_ids (3): `el_d15ccb74e7564ce5bd90554e806abb31, el_f44c33d051044d41b66f89aaa5790810, el_84f32607016641ae91871bee952652db`

- content:
```text
Section overview: Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw

If the screen basket and the screw are removed, maintenance work and replacement of the shaft and the shaft seals can be performed. To do this, the screw of the retaining plate is to be released on the gear side.

Subsections: Driving out the Shaft
```

If the screen basket and the screw are removed, maintenance work and replacement of the shaft and the shaft seals can be performed. To do this, the screw of the retaining plate is to be released on the gear side.
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Driving out the Shaft

The shaft can be driven forward to the press side by lightly hitting it with a soft-faced hammer. Ensure that the shaft is not knocked out completely.

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

### chunk_b0e4429a58e04657bfb3bc0902e22f1b
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (3): `el_0ae1463c584c45bea110ab3c5f08a3a1, el_8d9c9722f8c64017a2cc1dd9911fcf15, el_333dc49079b54644a0f35030f5d9cd01`
- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft > Loosening and Removing the S...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Loosening and Removing the Shaft

If the shaft has been loosened, it can be pulled out to the front/separator side. The feather key will fall out and remain on the gearbox side. Make sure that the feather key does not fall out of the housing. The key and shaft should be cleaned of dirt and grease residues before they are reinstalled.

Subsections: Shaft seals in Position
```

```text
Section overview: Greasing the Holder

Once the shaft has been removed, the shaft seals located in the rear housing can be removed. After an inspection or replacement of the shaft, the shaft seals must always be replaced. The holder for the shaft and for the shaft seals must be cleaned and greased prior to inserting the shaft.

T0

Subsections: Feather Key Insertion
```

Once the shaft has been removed, the shaft seals located in the rear housing can be removed. After an inspection or replacement of the shaft, the shaft seals must always be replaced. The holder for the shaft and for the shaft seals must be cleaned and greased prior to inserting the shaft.

- token_count: `47`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft > Loosening and Removing the Shaft > Shaft seals in Position > Greasing the Holder > Feather Key Insertion`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.2 Food Waste Press > 7.2.13.1 Maintenance of the Shaft & Shaft Seals > Loosening the Retaining Plate Screw > Driving out the Shaft > Loosening and Removing the S...`
- content:
```text
After cleaning and applying grease, the shaft can be reinserted. During this process, it must be ensured that the bracket for the feather key on the shaft matches the location on the gear unit. The feather key can be introduced through light hitting with a soft-faced hammer.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > Mount the Retaining Plate Once the feather key has been inserted flush, the retaining plate can be attached. For corrosion protection, the grease discharged during...`
- content:
```text
Once the feather key has been inserted flush, the retaining plate can be attached. For corrosion protection, the grease discharged during installation can be distributed on the surface and the excess grease removed.
The retaining plate must be attached with the specified torque.
```

If both shaft seals have been inserted correctly, they seal flush with the housing. When the seals are properly inserted, the installation of the screw and the screen basket can be carried out as previously described.
```

### chunk_9d62cdf8599b4101b32e8d57952981c0
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c26d285e3fd847029790c19c5c7a0f75`

- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions 7.3.1`
- element_ids (7): `el_eaa4da841e604b5389d8a3ed8729b697, el_af53096526eb43718743d846c190c650, el_51932f72c49546fe81bd0129023950bb, el_aeaccba6ab3f429aa876169f679a1940, el_1baac025e5014c89816fd13785df7cc8, el_abce47b5b5e546c6a73a5a9f39f4295d, el_dc66898fd03644b69468f27e0747945b`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions 7.3.1 Section overview: Safety Precautions 7.3.1 This manual contains basic instructions which must be observed whe...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Safety Precautions 7.3.1

This manual contains basic instructions which must be observed when installing, operating and servicing the vacuum / transfer pump. It is essential for the user / installer or responsible technician to read the manual thoroughly prior to installation and operation.

A copy of the manual must always be at hand where the pump is being used. In addition to the general safety instructions listed in the main manual section on safety, it is necessary to observe the specific safety rules included in the various sections of the manual.

It shall be the plant operator's responsibility to ensure that all maintenance, inspection and assembly work is performed by authorized and qualified personnel who have adequately familiarized themselves with the subject matter by studying this manual in detail.

Any work on the machine shall only be performed when it is at a standstill, it being imperative that the procedure for shutting down the machine described in this manual be followed. Pumps and pumps units which convey hazardous media must be decontaminated.

Replacement parts that have not been supplied by the manufacturer are not tested and not approved for use. The installation and usage of such replacement parts can have a negative impact upon the pump characteristics.

The manufacturer is not liable for any damages caused by the usage of non-original parts.

Subsections: Technical Data 7.3.2
```

- content:
```text
This manual contains basic instructions which must be observed when installing, operating and servicing the vacuum / transfer pump. It is essential for the user / installer or responsible technician to read the manual thoroughly prior to installation and operation.
A copy of the manual must always be at hand where the pump is being used. In addition to the general safety instructions listed in the main manual section on safety, it is necessary to observe the specific safety rules included in the various sections of the manual.
It shall be the plant operator's responsibility to ensure that all maintenance, inspection and assembly work is performed by authorized and qualified personnel who have adequately familiarized themselves with the subject matter by studying this manual in detail.
Any work on the machine shall only be performed when it is at a standstill, it being imperative that the procedure for shutting down the machine described in this manual be followed. Pumps and pumps units which convey hazardous media must be decontaminated.
Replacement parts that have not been supplied by the manufacturer are not tested and not approved for use. The installation and usage of such replacement parts can have a negative impact upon the pump characteristics.
The manufacturer is not liable for any damages caused by the usage of non-original parts.
```

- content:
```text
The illustrations in these instructions may not always correspond exactly to the installed machine. But they correctly describe the basic structure and the sequence of work steps.

The vacuum / transfer pump is a rotary lobe pump, which is of a positive displacement type. The main components which determine the pumping principle are two lobe rotors rotating in opposite directions within a housing, basically as shown below:
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4  The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).  The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) o...`
- content:
```text
 The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).

 The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) of the housing (2).

| (1)   | drive shaft   | (5)   | hull        |
|-------|---------------|-------|-------------|
| (2)   | housing       | (6)   | lobe rotor  |
| (3)   | shaft         | (7)   | plate       |
| (4)   | wear plate    | (8)   | front cover |
```

### chunk_854bb4e0fa954fe0b670600a1b7a79f4
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_1be72187fc92428cb00142eb5447476e`
- sequence_number: `314`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `3/4`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `74`
- token_count: `110`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4`
- element_ids (6): `el_8a6ed4a56b7544c6a28580af08b8b892, el_9719ea85e9f14d2b8b7c18eb89fc4857, el_0f44bc14d92a42d982e5a4385985ac08, el_1d0d4229029d4cf6a5e3a158a7ef8e25, el_9356cb27486c467d909aeb36ee6f8c75, el_51cfeeb2bf324f638d697e34165860f6`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Main Parts 7.3.4  The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).  The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) o...`
- content:
```text
 The drive is attached to the drive shaft (1).

 The drive shaft (1) is the extension of one of the two shafts (3) of the housing (2).

 In the housing (2) the movement of the drive shaft (1) is transmitted to shaft (3b), which turns in the opposite direction.

 The pump housing is formed by the front plate of the housing (2), the two hulls (5), the two wear plates (4), the plate (7) and the cover plate (8).

 The lobe rotors (6) transport the medium within the pump housing.

 Different versions of this pump have different forms and numbers of lobe rotors (6).
```

- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > General Most rubber products may change their physical properties under unfavourable conditions or if treated improperly, which will r...`
- content:
```text
Most rubber products may change their physical properties under unfavourable conditions or if treated improperly, which will result in a shorter lifetime. They can become unsuitable for use through excessive hardening, ageing, degeneration or permanent deformation, or because of blistering, cracking or other damage appearing on the surfaces.

These changes may occur under the influence of oxygen, ozone, heat, light, humidity, solvents or because of storing the products under load.

If stored and treated properly, rubber products will retain their properties even during long term storage (some years) with little change. However, this does not apply to uncured rubber compounds.
```

### chunk_ae08c7476d004e7b80e9e68649233017
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- sequence_number: `319`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `certification_info`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- token_count: `119`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- element_ids (6): `el_ac94a83fd62c4ce8803b8b6979051990, el_ee4f40b841d14d8cba34a420c9fae7d2, el_f3ecd5c2058641d5ae78ba4e0f8c1e16, el_24d714038ad94030a5f2eb3fdcfef2e5, el_9c24cb99b07e49bc9850cf741c382b15, el_96f760ee7ece45b4ab407b7c032e0b13`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:  The environment in which rubber products are being kept must be cool, dry, free of dust and naturally ventilate...`
- content:
```text
 The environment in which rubber products are being kept must be cool, dry, free of dust and naturally ventilated.

 Rubber products must not be stored in the open, not even in an area sheltered from the weather.

 Rubber products should be stored at temperatures above minus 10 °C and not more than plus 15 °C. Storerooms should not be damp and there should be no condensation.

 Rubber products should be stored in an environment offering a relative humidity under 65 %.

 Rubber products must be protected against light, particularly direct sunlight or high UV artificial.

 Rubber products should be kept away from forced ventilation and draughts; under these conditions they should be wrapped.
```

### chunk_5895944bd3a94b6f979bd803bd0efbfb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_674d61f398164316925c07c0aaf5ed21`
- sequence_number: `320`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/2`
- chunk type: `certification_info`
- page_start/page_end: `75`
- token_count: `55`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:`
- element_ids (2): `el_92f9b47d799444139d1a23c53741ffc4, el_0289c98fbeff49908a6394c879f89182`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Storage Environment:  As ozone is very aggressive and harmful to rubber products, they should not be stored in the same room with equ...`
- content:
```text
 As ozone is very aggressive and harmful to rubber products, they should not be stored in the same room with equipment likely to produce ozone, e.g. electric motors or other equipment which could electrically discharge or cause sparks.
 Rubber products should not be stored together with solvents, oil, grease, lubricants or any chemicals.
```

- content:
```text
Unless specified otherwise the pump is designed for a horizontal mounting, bolted securely to a sub-structure. The vent plug of the housing must always be mounted at the top of the gear box.
```

### chunk_ca87010283374f6ca6b140c2f38c7fbb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (5): `el_0c7ef8381c1b467598f53ddbeb1d655e, el_3daf6ac9855f48f299cafbe387e66ec6, el_4716fc526f664ba09f6e701c08845e29, el_797c9d2931464788b9eebcdfd0ab31eb, el_a31da8ba5d324f5f8c86f05249396435`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump Check the alignment after a short test run and make corrections if necessary. 7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow After bolting the b...`
- content:
```text
Check the alignment after a short test run and make corrections if necessary.

7.3.6.2 Direction of Rotation and Flow

After bolting the baseplate to the foundation, remove the coupling guard and check the alignment of the coupling with a ruler and re-align if necessary. There is danger of distorting the baseplate if the foundation is uneven which will result in misalignment.

Permitted maximum misalignment tolerances for the coupling between drive and pump are:

 Radial shift (1): 1% of the largest diameter (2)

 Angular misalignment (3): 1°30'
```

### chunk_2127cbc9e62846d69fd0138c1a953355
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_58cb0e0aa73d4668af0b467cfcc16698`
- sequence_number: `325`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `3/4`
- chunk type: `certification_info`
- page_start/page_end: `76 -> 77`
- token_count: `193`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump`
- element_ids (8): `el_f11aac0a09164181a287ed4af85bdf6a, el_f30031d6b8a1475bb9692e7ecd730dde, el_eaf596c75e3b4332b5bb752e3b6b873f, el_311f1ef0fea141ecae68d8568b6bc49d, el_0e018222f7d74c4dadda7b49d871dd42, el_c283d98f7e374c1c80a89396f0595cf4, el_874dd822717641429628b7b3fbaa1271, el_b7723b556de44d5892ec916ffb86234d`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump The lobe pumps are reversible and therefore direction of rotation can be selected to provide for the required direction of flow. There a...`
- content:
```text
The lobe pumps are reversible and therefore direction of rotation can be selected to provide for the required direction of flow.

There are two ways to change the direction of rotation/flow:

 Pumps with one drive shaft: by changing the direction of rotation of the drive

 Pumps with two drive shafts: by switching the cardan shaft (3) from drive shaft (1) to (2)

7.3.6.3 Piping System

 Clean the pipework and rinse thoroughly before installing the pump.

 Arrange the inlet and outlet pipework so that when the pump is not running, the pumped media is still present before and after the pump (pump remains flooded); sufficient pumped media should remain inside the pump head to lubricate the pump head components during restart.

 Connect the pipework ensuring sufficient pipe supports are used so that no external forces or stress act on the pump. Where required pulsation dampers or compensators can be used between the pump and pipework

The maximum allowable twisting loads (Fx, Fy, Fz) and bending loads (Mx, My, Mz) for the pump inlet and outlet connections have to comply with the requirements of API 676 and EN ISO 14847:
```

### chunk_bfbf866c2c6042ada564a8f84bd8d06a
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_7388b9b684724f269f46e2faf6267185`
- sequence_number: `326`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `technical_specification`
- page_start/page_end: `77`
- token_count: `149`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System`
- element_ids (1): `el_5de7bb60cf9e43b79a6426a9bc53b93f`
- table_ids (1): `table_2c00c825fffa4e27b54c4c444f704636`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.6.3 Piping System  Connect the pipework ensuring sufficient pipe supports are used so that no external forces or stress act on th...`
- content:
```text
 Connect the pipework ensuring sufficient pipe supports are used so that no external forces or stress act on the pump. Where required pulsation dampers or compensators can be used between the pump and pipework

The maximum allowable twisting loads (Fx, Fy, Fz) and bending loads (Mx, My, Mz) for the pump inlet and outlet connections have to comply with the requirements of API 676 and EN ISO 14847:

|   Standard nominal diameter [DN] |   Fx, Fy, Fz [N] |   Mx, My, Mz [Nm] |
|----------------------------------|------------------|-------------------|
|                               50 |              650 |               350 |
|                               65 |              850 |               435 |
|                               80 |             1040 |               560 |
|                              100 |             1300 |               700 |
|                              125 |             1700 |               865 |
|                              150 |             1950 |              1050 |
|                              200 |             2600 |              1400 |
|                              250 |             3250 |              1750 |
|                              300 |             3900 |              2100 |
```

```

In particular for control systems the latest version of the EC-Directive for Machinery, Annex I, Section 1.2 Controls should be observed.
WARNING: Follow all local and national electrical codes for all wiring. A qualified electrician must perform all wiring. Failure to comply may result in severe personal injury or property damage
```

Take Note: Never run the pump dry! - A few rotations in dry condition will damage the rotor lobes.

Pay strict attention to the following:
```

### chunk_ba99e062e84a4830a8ecfd687e7edddb
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (6): `el_3f1fb6300ebe40b3abff6098a3c1d2ba, el_5ae3f042508649ba9c86e5c0e1bcbbd8, el_2e8fc15380d944b9aa5fd56fdc85f89f, el_a828b29b49ae4679a4d036e5bd0ddbc5, el_e477235e79f743bd863a7b4f655a92a4, el_f52f9e8f007443689ca779b87e1bb3fa`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.7 Take Note: Never run the pump dry! - A few rotations in dry condition will damage the rotor lobes. Pay strict attention to the f...`
- content:
```text
Take Note: Never run the pump dry! - A few rotations in dry condition will damage the rotor lobes.

Pay strict attention to the following:

 Before starting up for the first time, fill the pump with medium to prime it.

 Priming is vital to initially lubricate the rubber lobe parts.

 Open valves and vents before starting the pump.

 Check the direction of rotation and flow by briefly switching on the pump drive.
```

CAUTION: This a positive displacement pump, which is capable of generating pressures that could cause damage – in extreme cases the bursting of pipes or vessels.
```

```text
CAUTION: Possible damage to the pump by exceeding the pressure rating of the pump head and inlet and outlet connections. Never run the pump against a closed valve.
```

- content:
```text
 Stop the pump by turning off the power and make sure it cannot be turned on accidently.

 Drain the pump head and if necessary, wash through if there is a risk of freezing or if the medium may solidify.
```

- content:
```text
Where a standby pump is installed as a back-up for the main pump and it is likely to stand idle for an extended period then it is recommended that it is operated from time to time.
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.1 Pump in General > Safety Instructions The pump head should be regularly washed through or rinsed out if sediment from the pump...`
- content:
```text
The pump head should be regularly washed through or rinsed out if sediment from the pumped media is likely to settle out in the pump head.
WARNING: Hands can be crushed by moving parts! Before opening the pump head (removing the cover plate) turn off the power supply and ensure it cannot be turned on accidently (remove fuse).
```

### chunk_792f47f7df2647cc81c13a8073536521
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_415ce38817ba4387bb81ad37623a6347`
- sequence_number: `337`

- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- token_count: `60`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.2 Lubricating the Shaft Seals`
- element_ids (2): `el_9790d720672d46f09ba745b974012a30, el_38e0e3c0bb4b457db2e7af0dd686dc72`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.2 Lubricating the Shaft Seals The pump shaft seals are lubricated with grease via two grease points on the side of the gear hous...`
- content:
```text
The pump shaft seals are lubricated with grease via two grease points on the side of the gear housing, with two grease outlet points on the opposite side.
Lubrication should always be carried out while the pump is running – never during standstill . For this purpose, a high duty water-resistant grease, type NLGI – class 2, should be applied.
```

### chunk_e7c5f82b820841ab9d31900009c66662
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_a29264ecc31c409f88c89e967e3fab93`
- sequence_number: `338`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `maintenance_interval`
- page_start/page_end: `79`
- token_count: `67`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Lubrication Schedule`
- element_ids (4): `el_c39fecc32fa74c20879e58bda96ba72a, el_83dc49f72ee844f98e2072f609af2c94, el_4230ec2beeae40c394142bb965352fc2, el_405bd739e2e8460383b8a5108ac0017f`
- table_ids (1): `table_ea7ef79b10a84b62ae33e7b7345acadf`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Lubrication Schedule  After every 350 hours of operation NB: The filling quantity with the hand-lever grease gun should not exceed 2...`
- content:
```text
 After every 350 hours of operation
NB: The filling quantity with the hand-lever grease gun should not exceed 2 to 3 strokes per grease nipple.
Recommended Lubricating Grease:
| Temperature range of the grease [5。]   | Designation DIN 51502   | Example              |
|-----------------------------------------|-------------------------|----------------------|
| -30... +120                             | K 3K - 20               | "SHELL"Alvania G3    |
| -30 ... +180                            | KE 3R -30               | "KLUBER"PETAMOGHY443 |
```

### chunk_31e1845de1a2402eb64feb561e9f6e73
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_58cb0e0aa73d4668af0b467cfcc16698`
- sequence_number: `340`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `4/4`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- token_count: `61`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump`
- element_ids (4): `el_bf7199ae911449a18d8fd967a085420a, el_c4c81738725f414fabc253940fb04c61, el_5443f969be09484ebcc2a3f046d05972, el_b4abf966b2454f7691d37aa5c3431ad0`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump The deaeration screw (1) has always to be at the top position. The magnetic oil drain screw (3) has always to be on the lowest position....`
- content:
```text
The deaeration screw (1) has always to be at the top position. The magnetic oil drain screw (3) has always to be on the lowest position.

Draining

 Open the magnetic drain screw (3) and let all the oil flow out.

 Clean the magnetic oil drain screw (3) at every oil change.

 Close the magnetic oil drain screw (3).
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > Filling  Fill through the deaeration screw (1) until approx. the middle of the sight glass (2).  Close the deaeration screw (1).`
- content:
```text
 Fill through the deaeration screw (1) until approx. the middle of the sight glass (2).

 Close the deaeration screw (1).
```

- chunk type: `technical_specification`
- page_start/page_end: `80`
- token_count: `95`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.4 Oil Quantities & Specification`
- element_ids (5): `el_61642a8a722142f1b43cae82c46d37b9, el_1f10906a36024b16bb7d52d2735edabf, el_b8023050fb7348c89605d7de4ba79d9b, el_4ff6630da4f9423e94f4fba6be161328, el_9cd91346950c4f0e9014381d9c64a868`
- table_ids (1): `table_b9db2881f5bd4279b52977c915f7590a`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.9.4 Oil Quantities & Specification The housing of the pump requires the following oil quantity: | Rotary Lobe Pump Type | Oil quan...`
- content:
```text
The housing of the pump requires the following oil quantity:
| Rotary Lobe  Pump Type   | Oil quantity  horizontal   | Oil quantity  vertical   | Change interval*                                                                                                               |
|--------------------------|----------------------------|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                          |                            | XB 0.6L 0.91L            | First change of oil after approx.  500 hours of operation or  12 months  Then after each 2000 hours of  operation or 12 months |
Oil specification:
SAE 75W-90 API GL-4 (or API GL-5)
* Change intervals are the base to maintain warranty; in aggressive conditions (high/low temp and humidity, full load operation etc.) the change interval can be shortened significantly.
```

### chunk_fc1f4d0928ea4f11921ad1f562a8685b
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_c30317eec5064800b125362066137459`
- sequence_number: `348`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/5`
- chunk type: `safety_warning`
- page_start/page_end: `83`
- token_count: `102`
- section_path: `7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly > Safety Instructions`
- element_ids (7): `el_d7b7ab5d499444d2974ddab5e0b192e3, el_7dd5859bfefe446591ebc09bc4d1b449, el_1f071a0d25db4c54a6cc0346d697c51a, el_86fa4d77642941478e325b221a09fba2, el_ede121e384164e38a7253fc3e4f3c380, el_dd5207d71b1e4803b9acb389e5aee1e2, el_7d27afb677214af793ce65db837321ca`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.1 Disassembly > Safety Instructions BIOHAZARD: Risk of illness and disease from the pumped medium! Decontaminate and empty the...`
- content:
```text
BIOHAZARD: Risk of illness and disease from the pumped medium! Decontaminate and empty the pump before opening. Wear appropriate personal protection equipment.
DANGER: Hands can be crushed by moving parts! Before opening the pump head (removing the cover plate) turn off the power supply and ensure it cannot be turned on accidently (remove fuse).
 Remove screws/spring washers (5030/5040) or hex nuts/spring washers (5035/5040).
 Remove the cover plate (5000).
 Remove screws (2070 with O-ring (2075) and fuse (2260).
 Remove end parts (2080) with O-rings (2085).
 Pull off the lobe rotors (2010) separately using a lobe extractor (pull-off device).
```

DANGER: Hands can be crushed by moving parts! Before opening the pump head (removing the cover plate) turn off the power supply and ensure it cannot be turned on accidently (remove fuse).
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: 7.3.11.2 Assembly

Assembly is a reversal of the above disassembly procedure, but additionally:

 In case of visible wear of the housings, crescents or wear plates these should be replaced.

 Fasten screws/nuts with a torque wrench as given in the following table:

 Secure screw (2070) with Loctite 245 or similar

 Secure or seal other screws with material according to the section drawing

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.3 Vacuum / Transfer Pump > 7.3.11.2 Assembly Assembly is a reversal of the above disassembly procedure, but additionally:  In case of visible wear of the housin...`
- content:
```text
Assembly is a reversal of the above disassembly procedure, but additionally:
 In case of visible wear of the housings, crescents or wear plates these should be replaced.
 Fasten screws/nuts with a torque wrench as given in the following table:
 Secure screw (2070) with Loctite 245 or similar
 Secure or seal other screws with material according to the section drawing
| Size      | Material                | Material   | Pos. no.   | Remarks              |
|-----------|-------------------------|------------|------------|----------------------|
| Size      | Stainless steel A...-70 | Steel 10.9 | Pos. no.   | Remarks              |
| M8        | 30 Nm                   | 40 Nm      | 2070       |                      |
| M10       | 40 Nm                   | 50 Nm      | 2070       |                      |
| M12       | 60 Nm                   | 90 Nm      | 2070       |                      |
| M16       | 150 Nm                  | 200 Nm     | 2070       |                      |
| M24       | 100 Nm                  | 120 Nm     | 2080       | Types MB only        |
| M45 x 1.5 | 100 Nm                  | 120 Nm     | 5450       | Types XLB-.../2 only |
```

```text
Section overview: Safety Precautions & Symbols

Take Note: Before using the pump carefully read the information contained in this instruction manual.

WARNING: Electrical Hazard! ALWAYS check for no voltage before starting work!

WARNING: Electrical connection must be carried out by suitably a qualified electrician.

Subsections: Technical Data 7.4.2
```

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.4 Liquor Transfer Pump > Safety Precautions & Symbols > Technical Data 7.4.2 > Description 7.4.3 Close-coupled, centrifugal pumps; electric motor with extended s...`
- content:
```text
Close-coupled, centrifugal pumps; electric motor with extended shaft directly connected to the pump with open impeller.

Intended use for moderately dirty liquids (maximum size of solids: 4 mm), non-explosive, nonaggressive for the pump materials. Liquid temperature from -10 °C to +90 °C.
```

Take Note: When first starting, check the direction of rotation is as per the arrow on the casing Never run the pump dry, only start the pump after filling with liquid and venting the case.

Pay strict attention to the following:
```

### chunk_bcc54e1a53d4465aa3584378a61427f8
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_e44ff08375804d58aa4e6687b8725fdd`

- token_count: `81`
- section_path: `7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4`
- element_ids (4): `el_d60a5c61bb3042048925b10640817f61, el_2e7cf981c1ff42078e68894bd6f6d234, el_cda03910172041bead8dfd575b06ecd9, el_5223778ddca944e092f39ad4dc090af0`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > Start-up and Operation 7.4.4  Open valves and vents before starting the pump.  Before starting up for the first time, fill the pump and vent the casing using the...`
- content:
```text
 Open valves and vents before starting the pump.
 Before starting up for the first time, fill the pump and vent the casing using the vent fitted.
 Check the direction of rotation and flow by briefly switching on the pump drive.
 If the pump discharge piping is routed above the liquor tank a non return valve will be required to prevent back filling of the food waste system, a valve suitable for water with sediment should be selected.
```

### chunk_17955f22d0174f7286e093d36d3bbe75
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_27994732b53344ccb02681021d647896`
- sequence_number: `369`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/1`
- chunk type: `troubleshooting`
- page_start/page_end: `89`
- token_count: `380`
- section_path: `7 Components > Troubleshooting 7.4.5`
- element_ids (1): `el_1836127f814b451db13b76d4590cbb1e`
- table_ids (1): `table_9521f157b00f46938882a940190a3d7d`
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > Troubleshooting 7.4.5 | PROBLEM | PROBABLE CAUSES | PROBABLE CAUSES | POSSIBLE REMEDIES | POSSIBLE REMEDIES | |-------------------------------------------|--------...`
- content:
```text
| PROBLEM                                   | PROBABLE CAUSES   | PROBABLE CAUSES                                              | POSSIBLE REMEDIES   | POSSIBLE REMEDIES                                                                                                                                 |
|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| (1)  The motor does  not start            | 1a)               | Motor overload protection  cuts in                           | 1a)                 | Check the power supply and make sure that the  pump shaft is turning freely. Check that the  thermal overload protection has been set  correctly. |
| (1)  The motor does  not start            | 1b)               | Shaft locked                                                 | 1b)                 | Remove the cause of lockage.                                                                                                                      |
| (2)  Pump locked                          | 2a)               | Prolonged periods of inactivity  forms rust inside the pump  | 2a)                 | Free the pump by rotating the shaft manually.                                                                                                     |
| (2)  Pump locked                          | 2b)               | Presence of solids in impeller                               | 2b)                 | Remove the wet end casing and remove any  solid solids the impeller.                                                                              |
| (2)  Pump locked                          | 2c)               | Bearings seized                                              | 2c)                 | Replace the bearings.                                                                                                                             |
| (3)  Pump runs with  no discharge         | 3a)               | Possible air leak on suction.                                | 3a)                 | Check.                                                                                                                                            |
| (3)  Pump runs with  no discharge         | 3b)               | Suction is blocked.                                          | 3b)                 | Clean blockage from suction.                                                                                                                      |
| (4)  Low flow  discharge.                 | 4a)               | Presence of deposits or solid  on the impeller               | 4a)                 | Clean the impeller.                                                                                                                               |
| (4)  Low flow  discharge.                 | 4b)               | Impeller damaged or worn                                     | 4b)                 | Replace the rotor.                                                                                                                                |
| (4)  Low flow  discharge.                 | 4c)               | Worn pump case                                               | 4c)                 | Replace the pump casing.                                                                                                                          |
| (5)  Noise and  vibrations from  the pump | 5a)               | Rotating part unbalanced                                     | 5a)                 | Check that no solid bodies are obstructing the  rotor.                                                                                            |
| (5)  Noise and  vibrations from  the pump | 5b)               | Worn bearings                                                | 5b)                 | Replace the bearings.                                                                                                                             |
| (6)  Leakage from the  mechanical seal    | 6a)               | The mechanical seal has been                                 | 6a)                 | Replace the mechanical seal.                                                                                                                      |
| (6)  Leakage from the  mechanical seal    | 6b)               | run dry or has stuck  Mechanical is damaged                  | 6b)                 | Replace the mechanical seal.                                                                                                                      |
| (6)  Leakage from the  mechanical seal    | 6c)               | Slight initial drip during filling  or on first start - up . | 6c)                 | Replace the mechanical seal.                                                                                                                      |
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.5 Actuated Valves > Safety Precautions 7.5.1 Section overview: Safety Precautions 7.5.1 Take Note: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and understand all...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Safety Precautions 7.5.1

Take Note: Ensure you have read the safety definitions and understand all instructions before servicing this machine . Failure to do so could result in personal injury or property damage .

CAUTION: Before working on a valve ensure system pressure has been released and pipes drained.

CAUTION: When installing or maintaining the actuated valve shut off and lock out power before removing any covers. Never make adjustments while in operation, except for those prescribed in this manual.

WARNING: Electrical Hazard! ALWAYS check for no voltage before starting work!

Subsections: Valve Description 7.5.2
```

- content:
```text
Section overview: Valve Description 7.5.2

The stainless steel one-piece flanged ball valve with full bore is designed for service with vacuum and up to nominal pressure at a max 80degC. with ISO5211 mounting pad for electric actuation.

O

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Environmentally
```

- table_ids (0): ``

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.5 Actuated Valves > Safety Precautions 7.5.1 > Valve Description 7.5.2 > Exploded View 7.5.3 > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Actuator Description 7.5.4 Sect...`
- content:
```text
Section overview: Actuator Description 7.5.4

The actuators are suited to the operation of ball and butterfly valves with a 90° rotation angle. They feature a water-resistant body with overload protection and a heating device against condensation formation. Power is transferred to the driveshaft via a self-stopping reduction worm gear to achieve precision control.

OPEN

CLOS

Attention

nanaer

FMD

FundamentalMarineDevelopments

Subsections: Wiring Diagram 7.5.5
```

### chunk_279bab8041c149a486cf322029e6bafe
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`

- element_ids (2): `el_193060216a724f8da989dc5504cadcdc, el_183ab38759924c13ad6fce83b603206e`
- table_ids (1): `table_ecb304379b9b44de89e9b54374cf07c1`

- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > 7.5 Actuated Valves > Safety Precautions 7.5.1 > Valve Description 7.5.2 > Exploded View 7.5.3 > Responsible Solutions Engineered > Actuator Description 7.5.4 The...`
- content:
```text
The actuators are suited to the operation of ball and butterfly valves with a 90° rotation angle. They feature a water-resistant body with overload protection and a heating device against condensation formation. Power is transferred to the driveshaft via a self-stopping reduction worm gear to achieve precision control.
| Pos.  Description          |
|----------------------------|
| 1 Motor                    |
| 2  Terminal block          |
| 3 Valve position indicator |
| 4 Gearbox                  |
| 5  Manual override         |
| 6 Limit switches           |
| 7 Heater                   |
```

### chunk_fe5020f6b6324103b500945f1f01aad1
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_bf96696961d74983a11ab427e1cf7766`
- sequence_number: `388`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `2/2`
- chunk type: `general`
- page_start/page_end: `95`
- token_count: `100`
- section_path: `7 Components`
- element_ids (2): `el_354ec43dcf3e492cb3df5bb592aba9ce, el_211354f41a6c41cbb41770a23b1124a9`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components Manually set the valve in the closed position. Loosen screw (1) on the lower cam (2) and adjust the cam to contact the lower micro switch (3). If the mechanical stop...`
- content:
```text
Manually set the valve in the closed position. Loosen screw (1) on the lower cam (2) and adjust the cam to contact the lower micro switch (3). If the mechanical stop prevents the cam rotating sufficiently turn the mechanical stop screw 2 turns counterclockwise from the closed position and lock it off. After adjustment of the cam (2) secure the cam with the screw (1).

Adjustment of 2 nd "CLOSED" Limit Switch

Loosen screw (4) on cam (5) and adjust the cam to contact the micro switch (6). After adjustment of cam (5) secure the cam with the screw (4).
```

### chunk_bd959e472ab540e293b357381fd2d896
- document id: `doc_1d7801da483048c4862535c934ee17b1`
- section id: `sec_23123ca927a2466faf1b7a302558273e`
- sequence_number: `390`
- chunk_index/chunk_total: `1/2`
- chunk type: `operation_instruction`
- page_start/page_end: `96`
- token_count: `108`
- section_path: `7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7`
- element_ids (3): `el_67da812c7b5a4dda886c623382ba2326, el_da10e24c11504b2ab39379099b06904a, el_86e78995863f4c238e2a8562b952e656`
- table_ids (0): ``
- picture_ids (0): ``
- embedding_text preview: `Document title: 19P006-31-FWC12-5-1-0_Manual Section path: 7 Components > Emergency Operation 7.5.7 In the case of a power or control failure, or a fault in the actuator, the actuator has the capability for manual operation. Take care ab...`
- content:
```text
In the case of a power or control failure, or a fault in the actuator, the actuator has the capability for manual operation. Take care about the turning direction of the manual override.
The actuator is fitted with an Allen key on the underside, it fits in the socket on the end of the gear shaft. To close the actuator, turn the Allen key clockwise and to open the actuator turn the Allen key counterclockwise.
CAUTION: When finished with manual operation, remove the Allen key from the gear shaft to prevent the injury of persons or the damage of the actuator by normal operation of the electric actuator.
```
